# Thread: Doctors Target Virus as Link to Solve Polio-Like Illness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors-target-virus-as-link-to-solve-polio-like-illness.11856/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2014-10-07T03:04:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors-target-virus-as-link-to-solve-polio-like-illness.11856/post-143101>

**Doctors Target Virus as Link to Solve Polio-Like Illness**
[http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2014-...irus-as-link-to-solve-polio-like-illness.html](http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2014-10-06/doctors-target-virus-as-link-to-solve-polio-like-illness.html)

While numbers of patients with Enterovirus D68 are still low over all, it was concerning enough when the worst consequence seemed to be severe respiratory distress in children with asthma. Now it has taken a more frightening turn with the apparent addition of paralysis in, right now, still relatively small percentage of cases.

Good to know that research attention is being paid to this virus--I hope it is scoped out and stopped quickly. I knew too many people severely afflicted by polio when I was growing up. How terrible if a similar paralyzing disease takes hold again.

_In cases where children develop paralysis, the symptoms usually start with those similar to a cold or stomach bug. They are followed by a fever, headaches, neck pain and, within a few days or weeks, a swift and sudden paralysis that in the most severe cases left children bound to a wheelchair, unable to speak or breathe on their own, said [Keith Van Haren](https://med.stanford.edu/profiles/keith-vanharen), a Stanford School of Medicine neurologist who has been studying a link between the virus and paralysis since 2012._

And . . .

_While there were some variations, most images from medical scans of those with paralysis looked strikingly similar, with the connection between the spinal cord and the muscles killed off with little chance of rebuilding the bridge -- just like seen in scans of those infected with polio, Greenberg said. . . .

The CDC hasn’t determined definitively whether the virus is causing the paralysis. EV-D68 hasn’t been detected in all of the paralyzed patients and given its widespread circulation, its presence could be coincidental, the agency said last week.

While the CDC continues to study the paralysis cases, “as a member of the clinical community, I think it is just a matter of time before we establish a definitive link between EV-D68 and this polio-like illness that follows,” Van Haren said._

And . . .

_Greenberg, who spoke by telephone, said he didn’t realize the full scope of the problem until early last week. After reports of a cluster of 10 paralysis cases in [Colorado](http://topics.bloomberg.com/colorado/), he and 30 other neurologists, infectious disease experts and public health officials hastily organized a conference call Sept. 29.

**Many Cases**

To Greenberg’s surprise, almost every doctor on the call, from Boston to [Chicago](http://topics.bloomberg.com/chicago/) to Los Angeles, had seen at least one case of unexplained paralysis in the past several months, and some had seen as many as 10._

In the meantime, paying attention to the Kruse Protocol of maximizing redox and DHA levels, minimizing exposure to nn-EMF, handwashing and cough hygiene in our kids would seem to be the best defense.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-08T16:43:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors-target-virus-as-link-to-solve-polio-like-illness.11856/post-143247>

### nonchalant — 2014-10-13T05:15:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-craving-seasonal-fall-carbs.11859/post-143685>

While traveling yesterday, I stopped at a grocery store to pick up some sort of snack. I became frustrated when I realized that nothing in this small-town store would suffice. No veggies/fruit/dairy/processed meats. The nuts were all cooked with oil. The tiny selection of dark chocolate was not very dark. No raw salami. Even the seafood was uninspiring. I walked around a while, until it dawned on me that it was a waste of time.

What I needed, actually, was direct energy, not indirect. I needed grounding and sunshine (it was cloudy). And I had been away from a Magnetico bed for several nights. I bought a bottle of water and stood barefoot on the concrete parking lot for a while.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-10-13T05:40:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-craving-seasonal-fall-carbs.11859/post-143690>

I feel the same way nonchalant. Even the Farmer's market leaves me uninspired. Being in the mountains I leave wishing for so much more. I discovered, though, that my office is an old building built on a pure concrete foundation. No plastic or anything else between my floor and the earth. I've stopped sitting, and I've been standing barefoot while working now for nearly 2 months (desk job with awesome standing desk). In these past couple months I've noticed some pretty significant changes to my body just from standing barefoot and not sitting. It could only be made more perfect by moving my workspace outside to the canyon.

### fitness@home — 2014-10-13T15:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-craving-seasonal-fall-carbs.11859/post-143708>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=143685)
>
> While traveling yesterday, I stopped at a grocery store to pick up some sort of snack. I became frustrated when I realized that nothing in this small-town store would suffice. No veggies/fruit/dairy/processed meats. The nuts were all cooked with oil. The tiny selection of dark chocolate was not very dark. No raw salami. Even the seafood was uninspiring. I walked around a while, until it dawned on me that it was a waste of time.

Sally, We were traveling to/from Dallas on Saturday+Sunday. Too much time in a car! Didn't help that it rained during both commutes. That meant two stops at the Buc-ees Travel Center on I-45. At least there were plenty of good (although not optimal) choices for a snack like veggies, raw nuts, & jerky . Unfortunately, the only seafood I saw was tuna salad.
Was craving sunshine and that "connection" by the time we got home. Did some bathtub CT with an hour after getting home.
Back to original subject matter of this thread - - since I indulged in the squash, etc. last week the cravings are now nearly gone. Off to make a BPC coffee now.

### Shijin13 — 2014-10-13T15:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-craving-seasonal-fall-carbs.11859/post-143709>

Yesterday I spent 2hrs mowing the lawn barefoot! (DH went over the grass with the tractor for the first pass, and I followed up to get what he missed b/c the grass was SO LONG!)
the gras was a bit wet too. maximum grounding occurred yesterday. I followed that up with a 12min HIIT workout, then a 45 min CT!

It was awesome!

I wasn't all that hungry for dinner...

now that the light is changing, I'm going to be making an extra effort to walk mid day when I'm at the office... just to get outside and absorb what little light we've got. I'll also eat outside and ground while I do it...

# Thread: breathing difficulty
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nikita — 2014-10-07T18:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143144>

Hi everyone,
To begin with a humble thanks to Dr JK for all that u r doing for humans.
This is my first post,but have been a reader and follower of Dr Kruses protocol for a year.
I live in Mumbai india.
Initially was paleo/primal but was still struggling with energy.
After going epi paleo and keto have more energy and wake up feeling fresh.
I consume loads of fresh(crustaceans,crabs) fish and eggs that we get out here very easily.consume ro water and also take care to reduce non native emf.
Two problems that I am facing is breathing difficult(can'take take full breaths) and tremendous hair fall which has not stopped for the past 6 to 8 months. New hair are sprouting back though.
Any help will be of great value.

### nikita — 2014-10-07T18:26:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143151>

I have owned a cell phone for the past 14 years and have kept it in my pocket or by my head side turned on, along with a wifi modem on all this while until recently, I changed and started doing the right things.
I have also ended up hurting my ligament on my wrists of both my hands which Dr JK spoke about in one of his blog posts which mentioned about repetitive stress injury and not sure if it is carpel tunnel syndrome.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-07T19:03:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143168>

Hi nikita, welcome! Sounds like you are doing well with the seafood and water. Do you get much sunshine?

### fitness@home — 2014-10-07T20:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143174>

Hi Nikita,

Welcome!

Mumbai is pretty densely populated, isn't it? I would expect that it is also very "technologically populated" (IT, engineering, etc). Have you tested your environment at home or work?

### nikita — 2014-10-07T20:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143179>

Thanks for responding,
I spend atleast 4 to 5 hours a week directly connected to the earth and get plenty sunshine, I certainly could do better than that.
I stay on a small mountain hill which has good tree coverage and is not very populated.
I am a stay at home mother.
I had once got the mobile company guys to come and test for emf from the mobile tower which is around 100 to150 meters away and the results came out low.
I sleep for good 8 to 9 hours unless my baby wakes me up for any reason.
She will turn 2 on Oct 10.I also did bf her for 18 months while consuming loads of seafood even then.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-07T20:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143182>

I used to have breathing difficulties, where I couldn't breathe out very well. And before that I had full-blown asthma. I think you are on the right track.

### nikita — 2014-10-07T20:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143184>

I also have been doing spot ct with ice packs on my back and belly area and cold showers regularly.it never gets cold where I live.
Never goes below 20°Celsius. .

### caroline — 2014-10-08T00:08:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-143211>

Hi nikita ...and welcome!

Please start a journal in the journal section .... Dr. kruse needs context.

Do you have dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX .... lots of great information and also the first two years of the blog.

Do you listen to all Jack's podcasts? a great and easy way to get lots of info.

### nikita — 2014-12-30T06:32:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breathing-difficulty.11860/post-151171>

# Thread: Nerve pain...cortisone ok?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2014-10-08T00:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143210>

Think I may have a pinched nerve. Acute pain in shoulder with intermittent radiating pain to elbow. Index finger very numb. Thumb and middle numb also though lesser. Left side.

Seeing doc in morning. Cortisone ok or should be avoided?

Have iced, sunned, earthed, hydrated for days no relief.

### cantweight — 2014-10-08T01:23:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143215>

Any suggestions then? This shit hurts bad and I am not a wuss. Screwing with my sleep too. I've even popped advil which I never do! Sucker hurts!

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-10-08T01:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143216>

you need to have someone look at head/neck and thoracid mechanics, diaphragm control, balance of rotator cuff and pectoral girdle, etc etc
cortisone is the easy way out of pain but does nothing to correct dysfunction
cortisone is a derivative of cortisol, it kills inflammation acutely but degrades collagen long term, i see this all the time when people draw for injections without looking for the causes. one does not simply acquire a shoulder tera or impingement out of thin air.

### Jude — 2014-10-08T01:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143218>

Gord..................... it's painful when the advice is right.....................
So sorry for your pain Christi...hope you can find some relief and help

### cantweight — 2014-10-08T01:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143220>

Just once an easy way out would be so nice

Thanks for the advice, will skip the shot

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-08T02:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143222>

not a fan at all........now DHA and iodine count me all in for nerve pain.

### cantweight — 2014-10-08T17:23:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143249>

So its a compression at c5 c6.
They gave me medrol, soma, norco, and physical therapy.

So do I ice like mad, hydrate the crap out of myself, eat oysters and go back in a month and see where we're at?

8 X rays today...need to do some serious grounding. Think I'll lay in the sun with ice on the neck...Feeling like a dumb ass for icing my shoulder this whole time.

Thinking no on the steroids, will do some turmeric instead. The muscle relaxers and pain pills are tempting though.

As always thoughts appreciated.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-10-08T17:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143251>

Spinal decompression at the chiro works. .... especially in cervical area. Do your homework; there are 3 major companies that make the machines. I found a chiro using the simplest and least expensive machine. Very pleased with the results in freeing up movement in my cervical.

### cantweight — 2014-10-09T03:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143270>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) you've got me thinking about the iodine.

I have no history of neck issues, but am recently off my thyroid meds. Is there any possible correlation between my thyroid maybe drawing on my iodine supply far more than it was when being dosed with 180mg armour? Perhaps it is not a coincidence the issue is close in proximity to the thyroid....or am I just talking out my ass here?

Compression sounds like it would be mitochondrial based on the Ling theory...loss of water structure in the cells.

There was also calcification at c5/c6 with the compression which makes me question the thyroid as well.

Grasping at straws...

### Penny — 2014-10-09T06:15:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nerve-pain-cortisone-ok.11862/post-143278>

You might try a thera cane - you can compress the trigger point with it - otherwise, ice it - and love love love the turmeric - was recently experiencing carpal tunnel like pain - put a couple tablespoons in my coffee and then noticed the pain was gone - black pepper makes it go and ginger is very synergistic with it as well - the other thing I've found helpful for neck pain is those turquoise thera balls - you put one under your neck, inhale then turn your head from side to side - my mother had issues like that and I remember she would hang this bag of water over a door and the other end would pull on her neck - some kind of traction device - but a chiropractor would be awesome - however, I don't let them manually adjust my neck - mine uses what he calls an "activator" - it's sort of a targeted punch and it always helps me - of course, your mileage may vary...  One last thing - you might want to spray magnesium oil (mag chloride flakes mixed with water at a 1 to 2 ratio) - Dr. K suggested it for achilles tendonitis and it worked great - maybe because mag is hydrophylic it works? Epsom salt compress would probably works as well - I thought Dr. K said he once prescribed a boatload of vitamin D and K2 for someone with a compressed disk - boatloads of water and ice - it took a month but it helped - I vaguely recall it was some huge amount of vitamin d like 30000-50000 - I think the k2 makes the calcium stay in the bone instead of going elsewhere -

### Penny — 2014-10-09T18:41:57+0300

### K123 — 2014-10-09T11:42:38+0300

Sorry if it is basic to some of you but is blue light during daytime harmful? If you work long hours on the computer during daytime is it better to wear the blue blockers all day? I start wearing the blue blockers only when the sun goes down... this article ([http://www.huffingtonpost.com/dr-michael-j-breus/blue-light-sleep\_b\_4993859.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/dr-michael-j-breus/blue-light-sleep_b_4993859.html)) sort of said that blue light during daytime is good but Im not sure what Dr.K says. I remember him saying we look at our phones over a 100 times a day and that is destroying the serotonin in our brains?? Please help.

### caroline — 2014-10-09T13:18:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-during-day-time-question.11865/post-143307>

Hi K123 .... can you put up a pic please .......Dr.K's rule

# Thread: What time of the day do you work out?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K123 — 2014-10-09T13:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143304>

Some places Dr.K recommends best time for activity as 1-4pm and some place else best time to work out as 4-6pm. I am trying to do strength training, HIIT and CT all twice a week with Sunday being the day of rest. Can you share what time you typically work out and what time have you found to be the best if you have tried various timings?
I work out with pretty heavy weights like Dr.McGuff suggests in Body by science and do HIIT for 25 mins with 2 mins walks (3mi/hr) and 30 sec sprints at 8mi/hr speed. Quite a bit overweight (55lbs) but I am able to do these without much trouble...can finish and not be sore the next day. Would like to hear if you have any insights into what I am doing vs what I could be benefiting more from. Thank you!!

### Danco3636 — 2014-10-09T19:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143335>

For me all day is a workout (fun-in). I always get up in the morning with some activity, mobility, stretching or tree climbing fun.
Most of my lifting is around 10-11:00 fasted- three days a week- minamist- high intensity. Large meal sometime post between 12-2:00, largest meal of the day.
I may do some higher intensity stuff around 4-6 just before I have my last meal around 7:00 light protein/ fat meal.
My cardio is always done fasted before noon.
But I incorporate movement all day even if it is a quick hand stand, tree hang, walk, mobility, etc.
I do what feels right.

I am sure when you workout plays into:
Your context and lifestyle matters and dictates.

### shah78 — 2014-10-09T20:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143342>

Welcome to the forum. Why do you do 25 min. of HIIT AFTER a BBS workout.? You've incorporated two HIT workouts in one day. Thus you're be definition overtraining and at the same time doin gso much quantity of work that you not really doing BBS either. Lying down,or reading forums is post BBS activity, not getting on a tread mill and cranking out 25 more minnutes of HIT. JUst my two cents.

### K123 — 2014-10-09T23:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-143351>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=143342)
>
> Welcome to the forum. Why do you do 25 min. of HIIT AFTER a BBS workout.? You've incorporated two HIT workouts in one day. Thus you're be definition overtraining and at the same time doin gso much quantity of work that you not really doing BBS either. Lying down,or reading forums is post BBS activity, not getting on a tread mill and cranking out 25 more minnutes of HIT. JUst my two cents.

Hi Shah. I did BBS alone for a while but it has been really slow going and especially since I am home all day life becomes too sedentary living in a tiny apartment in London. So I am trying to do BBS Mondays and Thursdays, 25 mins HIIT Tuesdays and Fridays and CT on Wednesdays and Saturdays. Just annoying that I have to lose about 55lbs to be in the ideal weight range for my height and BBS once a week is just not cutting it. Especially women over 30 lose muscle mass fast and I even read McGuff saying that women can probably lift twice a week. I'm just beginning to test this plan so I will see in a week or two how Im doing with over all inflammation and weight loss I thought  Brilliant plan or not is yet to be seen

### K123 — 2014-10-10T00:00:35+0300

### oceanwild — 2015-07-27T10:41:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-of-the-day-do-you-work-out.11866/post-173197>

well/ on weekdays, I work out for 2 times at night. on weekend I go play outdoor sports 1 time. so mostly at night time for me to do the work out. after that, sometimes I take a massage, It is very helpful adding the [Cinnamon oil](http://www.best-dietary-supplements.com/) into the essence oil couple drops. great release on pressures.

You will learn a ton in this blog and it is deep in science that many of you have never considered. Welcome to Grotthuss mechanism, protonicity, plasma chemistry, and ionic fluids with iodides. You will know more about protons than you can imagine. This blog takes Pollack's work to the smallest scales of science. Here you will learn how transgenerational epigenetic switching occurs and how leptin resistance begins fundamentally. Wow, Jack

I'm CTing outside in the bright OZ sun with mouth agape having just read this incredible blog post that I will need to re-read a few times. You certainly delivered the goods on this one. Personally I think it is your best one yet, extremely well written and flows like liquid gold and brings together so much for me. I need to get Gilbert to read it.

### ssj3 — 2014-10-10T11:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-water-works-in-us-fundamentally-hydrogen-bonding.11869/post-143380>

Jack,

Why do you not consider _n_→π\* interaction since these have been shown to be worth 5−25% of a hydrogen
bond strength?

> Recently, another type of electron delocalization, the _n_→π\* interaction between carbonyl groups, has been shown to play a role in stabilizing protein structure... We found that an _n_→π\* interaction is worth ∼5−25% of a hydrogen bond and that stronger hydrogen bonds tend to attenuate or obscure _n_→π\* interactions. Conversely, weaker hydrogen bonds correlate with stronger _n_→π\* interactions and demixing of the orbitals occupied by the oxygen lone pairs. Thus, these two interactions conspire to stabilize local backbone−side-chain contacts, which argues for the inclusion of _n_→π\* interactions in the inventory of non-covalent forces that contribute to protein stability and thus in force fields for biomolecular modeling

[http://webcache.googleusercontent.c...fs/Bartlett2013.pdf+&cd=2&hl=en&ct=clnk&gl=au](http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:0jAcnbeQ2HsJ:www.biochem.wisc.edu/faculty/raines/lab/pdfs/Bartlett2013.pdf+&cd=2&hl=en&ct=clnk&gl=au)

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-10T12:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-water-works-in-us-fundamentally-hydrogen-bonding.11869/post-143381>

That was a concise blog post (but my excerpts/notes are still pretty long  -- [http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-tensegrity-6](http://tanyewwei.com/notes/jk-tensegrity-6))

Most of my questions regarding halogens are answered, though obviously there are a few more which arise.

For example, iodine degradation and recycling -- an "iodine cycle" in the body has not been formally described, but the mechanisms described in Tensegrity 6 obviously has iodine gaining and losing electrons constantly to bolster hydrogen bonding. Oxygen seems to play a huge role here, and look to be the electron donors for iodine. ie: oxygen pays DHA through iodine (electrons are the currency).

Anyway, I need to study this interaction more to be able to answer questions like "What is the quickest way to deplete your iodine stores?", or "Why isn't iodine from Lugol's as effective as iodine from seaweed?"

Then of course, we now have the basis for information transfer, but not yet the code for information storage, and nothing yet about information retrieval or manifestation. These are likely the mechanics of the topological insulators of the brain, which I guess I'll have to wait for future blog posts to touch on this (and start looking at the structures of the regions of the brain thought to be responsible for memory).

### av8r — 2014-10-10T19:15:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-water-works-in-us-fundamentally-hydrogen-bonding.11869/post-143423>

“A cell uses hydrogen and oxygen to un-condense our protein polymers, ever so slightly, to allow life to exist. When we sleep we are designed to be fully condensed. This implies that life can only exist when our protein polymers are slightly un-folded. This un-folding happens when electrons are withdrawn from proteins.”

Condensed and un-condensing of protein polymers makes me think of Keiber’s law, maximizing surface area to volume. The foliage is in full bloom in the northeast this time of year and soon the leaves will all fall; the trees are following Keiber’s law and folding up for the upcoming winter.

### ssj3 — 2014-10-11T10:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-can-i-get-good-chocolate-95.11870/post-143505>

You could always try to make your own... this was my last attempt which turned out very nice...

Cacao Butter ~ 150G
Cacao Powder ~ 240G
Coconut Oil ~ 50G
Coconut Cream ~ 100G
Sugar Alcohol/Stevia Extract ~ 40G (depending how bitter the cacao is)
Honey - 1 tspn
Vanilla Essence
Macadamia Nuts (as many as I could be bothered breaking with the hammer)

# Thread: The Cold Never Bothered Me Anyway
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-10T11:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143379>

It's taken a while for me to dive into the forum, but here's my journal and I hope that I can add something of value every now and again.

I'm coming at this from a performance point of view for myself, and from a health perspective for those that I am fortunate enough to be able to help working as a nutritionist (specialising in sports nutrition) and bodyworker.
I'm predominantly a runner, with the intention of working up to ultra distance events over the next few years, though I also swim, mountain bike and strength train.
'All or nothing' would be a great understatement when describing my character, and it is excellence which motivates me in all that I pursue.

Over the last 6 years I have been delving into the whole fat-adapted scene, experimenting, and gradually working towards the point where I can do long training sessions in the fasted state, and can delay refuelling until my appetite returns with no detrimental effects.
My levels of training are greater than many, and I recognise the need to have all of my ducks in a row in other areas of my life to offset this:
\-Diet is epi-paleo and 100% raw
\-CT is _sans_ ice as I lack the facilities to create an ice bath, but I am training in a cold pool five days per week for 40-80 mins
\-I wear blue blocking glasses after sundown and have f.lux installed on my laptop
\-I hate technology and avoid it unless absolutely necessary.
\-I avoid fluoridated/chlorinated drinking water
\-I ground myself as much as possible

Those are the biggies, though as i'm kinda 'fringe' there are other things that I now think of as normal but likely appear strange to others.

Things haven't always been this way, and i've suffered in the past with anorexia (up and down for 7 years) a problem which became much worse when I left home for university; interestingly this was a move away from my countryside life into a relative EMF soup (though it could have been infinitely worse) and to the only part of England that fluoridates the water. This problem started out of trying to achieve perfection, and in trying to fix myself I immersed myself in studying. The understanding of EMF was the missing part and now explains why I was unable to turn things around despite doing everything that conventional Paleo would tell you is right on the money.

Though at times I regret having made the decisions that I did at that time, it was this personal struggle that forced me to dig deeper than most, and it is to this that I attribute the knowledge I now have and am able to use for benefit of others which was my goal with my career.
I feel that I owe all of this to God, as it was through payer that something finally clicked in m head, and I recognised the potential I had, and how this would be lost if I wound up dying in my sleep, to which I came frighteningly close.
To those that are curious, my screen name is a reference to my favourite Bible verse which was read to me on the day that I asked God into my life.

_"God did not give us a spirit of fear and timidity, but of power, love and self-discipline"_

What I am currently working on:

\-Taking steps to create the life I truly wish to live.
I currently live outside of a city, and am not exposed to the associated city-life problems but I have always wanted to be way out in the countryside, minutes from the trails and fells. Likely this will necessitate working for myself, which I am moving towards, but is a little daunting.

\-Avoiding despair
I am prone to becoming very negative and critical both towards myself, and when viewing the world and those that inhabit it. I can often only see things getting worse as I see the misguided actions of Man.
I do no watch the news which is a start, but I have to be more discerning over to what I give my attention.

\-Experimenting further with diet to optimise performance
I have not tried protein refeeding which I have noticed mentioned on the forum but have not explored further.

I could go on, but will add further bits and pieces as I go along.
One final thing if anyone fancies a read... I have a blog and have included a link below to an old post which gained Jack's approval when I tweeted it him a while back.

[http://iowemysolitudetootherpeople....41650/humankind-has-not-woven-the-web-of-life](http://iowemysolitudetootherpeople.tumblr.com/post/74487141650/humankind-has-not-woven-the-web-of-life)

Out.

### Inger — 2014-10-10T13:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143387>

I love it Timothy..........

Tell me, how came you started to eat all raw? Why?

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-10T15:11:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143396>

I forget exactly why it took my interest initially, likely the more I came to understand the importance of enzymes and the problems associated with cooking (accrylamides, oxidation etc) but then I read Aajonus' book and that lead to a lifestyle change rather than just experimenting. I always found my digestion better with raw anyway, and I have to say that I prefer the taste except for the odd time when I wonder why I do any of this to myself, and wish I could just switch my brain off, watch a movie and eat a pizza and popcorn. But I never have been able to do that (and would likely regret it even if I could). They say you don't miss what you've never had, but I can't help wondering sometimes!

### Inger — 2014-10-10T18:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143402>

Well... Timothy.. I'll tell you what. How I feel about it... I just thought about it today you see, actually a lot last days! When I do something "not good" - I just choose to do so.. even if I know it is not optimal for me. I sometimes do that kind of stuff - not gonna tell...LOL BUT. It is like I am not scared... because the beauty I have experienced is so strong it will always drag me like a magnet!!! So I kind of cannot get addicted to anything bad anymore.. I am helplessly spoiled  by the beauty, the love and the light.............. I know this, in the deepest of my heart. It will always be the force that drags me to it.....

I am not saying one should do stuff that are not optimal! But I am saying you can feel the light so strong, and you will know what you are willing to risk. I cant do much "bad stuff", I am not that strong yet, but look at Jack.. he is the master  look how bold he is, it amazes me to no end. To do these biohacks of his..ouch! I could NOT! But I know he can do it, he knows it too, he has the energy
Love gives energy too. Jack is one of the strongest loving humans I have ever met.. that is why I fell in love with him from the beginning
I read it between the lines from his blog and posts
so we just need to know - how and when can we stray from the path.. I think when you really have felt the magic of this life, the only thing you can get addicted to is.. that
IDK what it is, life force.. love... the sun the moon and the stars.. but I know you know what I mean

That is why I had to put this quote from Peter on my Kitchen wall

> Try everything - but to do that you must be loaded with energy so YOU can decide what is important, this applies to every single detail in life

Timothy... I love the quote from the Bible, that is your favorite... it is truly amazing!!!! Thank you for sharing... might need to put that on my kitchen wall too!

You know what helps me so much too? To sing.... I mean.. really sing from the deepest of my heart. It feels so healing, I sing all the time! But only when I think no one listens...lol just my cat

Timothy I had my share of pizza in my youth and I do not miss that feeling at all I can assure you
you could try and find out  then you will know - in a heart beat..lol

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-11T13:05:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143513>

That's an excellent way of looking at it Inger, and I have always known in my heart that those things would not really bring any pleasure, but at times when i'm feeling lonely or whatever it's easy to imagine that a life that is completely the opposite from that which you are living would be more pleasurable.
Love is something i'm missing right now, and is likely why I feel a little empty.

Love the quote!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-11T16:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-143516>

We're all in this together and we all helped create it at some level. So you can go on bitching about your life of do something about it. The best plan of action is educate others about what really is going on around us. ........No one is useless in this world who lightens the burdens of another. For me, the purpose of my life is not to be successful or happy. I can create those two things for myself quite well. Instead it is to be useful, to be honorable, to be compassionate, to have it make some difference to to others so that you have lived fully and lived well when they put you in the ground. No one has ever become poor by giving someone else the extension of their hands or the thoughts in their mind. Natural exercise for my heart and muscles is reaching down and lifting people up to see a scale of science they were blind to before they met me. This scale can change everything about them, even though few realize it at the beginning.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-22T19:34:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-144731>

Been a little quiet since starting this journal; partly because the comments made have made me reflect upon the way I have been observing and judging the world, and party because I hate typing on my laptop.
I recognise what I need to work on. Lonelines makes one very bitter and critical, and this is my challenge to overcome right now as I am blessed to no longer be suffering with health issues as other members of the forums are. It is certainly the isolation that causes problems as when I am actually with other people this attitude changes, and my demeanour when working to help others is even above that; I just need my hot buttons pushing to get me going.

Bits & pieces:

-   Have been down to the river on several occasions recently, plenty of paddling and scrambling on the rocks. Next is to go beyond the knee depth I have exposed myself to thus far. Dread to think of the temperature but I'm sure I can manage it. (Picture attached)

-   Listened to some interesting podcast interviews recently. Dr Bonlie from Magnetico was on the Live to 110 podcast and that as pretty good. I've been looking at the sleep mattresses and searched the forums about them picking up bits here and there but would be interested to hear feedback on those that have one and which strength people are going for. On the magnetic theme i'd also be interested on any input on magnets for local use with injuries. I have looked myself and know of the Earth Pulse, but recommendations are always handy.

-   Seafood consumption is at an all time high, along with nori/wakame seaweed. I really love raw prawns (guess you'd call them shrimp), and I can do oysters but I find them kind of unfulfilling.

Inger, as a fellow raw foodie are most of your saturated fats mainly coming attached to muscle meats or are you doing any bone marrow? They are a little harder to get in when you can't melt butter on things... I only really thought this the other day that my saturated fat consumption dropped off in the last year when my diet evolved.

Also, better picture of me now, I think, and my name is Tim.

### Inger — 2014-10-22T19:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-144734>

Hi Tim
Love your new pic    And the river is so beautiful.. lucky you that can go paddling!
You seem to be a lonely wolf..like I a bit? I need to be alone too... but maybe you are too much alone? If you do not like it you could contact friends or make some friends or talk to people, there are always some around, like if you go shopping or stuff

I love bone marrow! I do not have it often but when... uh I enjoy it so much! I just eat it plain..yum! I think we need saturated fats too. I sometimes crave it if I had too little for a while. I do muscle fat from beef too, I can get that very cheap. I love to eat it plain. I sometimes do grassfed butter too, even if it is pasteurized. I can get raw butter from Austria tho, it is so good. But I try not eat too much dairy.
You might try to not get religious about 100% raw, I would say. I roll so well when I eat almost everything raw but allow me a rare steak or butter or gently cooked fish once in a while. It does not seem to hurt me. I know many rawfooders who are very religious about eating nothing cooked and I think it does not make them happy? Because I met some and they were very narrow minded and complained about almost everything  go figure! I am not thinking 100% raw is the only road to health... there are many factors that are more important.. and that say I, a raw addict...lol

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-22T20:33:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-144742>

I am a lone wolf; it's unfortunate that often when trying to make ourelves well we have to distance ourselves from others. I also movwd a long way from home for a job and haven't really met others with whom I can spend time outside work. Admittedly I don't make it easy for myself!
I have a friend in France that I used to write to and speak with via skype, and I wished that I had the chance to spend time with her back then. We soke for the first time in months tonight and I felt nothing. There is a void that must be filled. Time to go searching.

How do you do the bone marrow? I wouldn't even know where to start!
I have found that mt digestion especially is better when raw, alo my skin glows. I gravitated towards raw because I slowly found cooked animal products repulsive, and the studying came from that feeling. I would take any raw meat or fish over cooked, but steamed brocolli and onions with butter would be amazing!
I certainly think that a majority raw is favourable. and that there may be cases where the exclusion of certain groups (by colour) could benefit certain conditions as Aajonus's book outlines.
I am going to increase saturated fats as we come into winter, and reduce my use of olive/avocado oils which are convenient on raw things.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-10-31T22:09:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145820>

Haven't been very good at updating this, as i'm not entirely sure what my journal is right now. Whereas many are tracking their health conditions my challenges lie more in being decisive to move closer to the life that I want for myself and whomever I will share it with.
Keeping my body temperature on the lower side, but not to a point where it makes me miserable, really helps with this and I hope that by achieving small wins I will build up the confidence to take more and more calculated risks.

I am really trying to increase the seafood at the moment. Though I don't have a problem with oysters I just don't find them very satisfying.
Raw prawns and salmon are my favourites and i'm going to make Inger's pickled herrings as soon as the fishmonger has them in. Since really increasing seafood consumption the most notable thing is increased tolerance in situations which would normally have me irritated. A calmness that I have not always possessed.

Another challenge for me right now, and something I have continually tried with occasional small successes, is to help my family. Most notably my dad as in my eyes he's at the greatest risk of trouble. He needs this blog, but I know that it will take me to get the key principles through to him.
He has been a competitive athlete much of his life, racing up to ironman distance, marathons and ultras, swimming... and after a spell of just being generally active is now training for similar events. This wouldn't necessarily be an issue but for the fact that he has followed a standard diet for almost his whole life (apart from the brief times I have been able to steer him onto a path more conducive to health)
Gluten, dairy etc etc and although 85% of the diet is home cooked and by no means a train wreck by most standards it is carb heavy with no respect for seasons, there is snacking on biscuits and his evening meals are basically whatever he and my mum prepare.

The home itself is well away from the biggies when it comes to EMF, but wifi is always on, he carries his phone, has an ipad all of that stuff. Will watcjh TV up until going to bed with all the lights on.
Oh and he's only recently retired from a job which was either behind a computer in the room where the wifi router is, or travelling across the globe ~8-10 times per year for two week stretches. Prior to this he worked as an electrician.
I couldn't make this stuff up, and I doubt it could be a lot worse unless he lived under a phone mast.

Every time I visit we'll have some conversation or another, and i'll again be labelled a crackpot for turning the lights off/grounding/fasting/eating raw meat and fish and generally being counter culture. They will both concede that much of what I say makes sense, less so my mum who is a nurse practitioner, but it will not be enough to affect change.
My dad wouldn't read a book or a blog, and is sceptical about the more 'fringe' things that I say which is why no changes have been made at home regarding lighting/EMF.

I understand as a parent that it would be hard to take direction from your son but I know that he does look to me for guidance. Perhaps i'll have have to come at this from a different angle?
I read jack's blog about having to visit his father in law in hospital, and that in this same way Jack had given him the information but it wasn't implemented. I would hate for something to happen before the necessary changes were made.

### Inger — 2014-10-31T22:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145822>

Timothy oysters do not fill me up too well either, but I just eat something together with them and I am fine  They are my medicine  Maybe you can make fish head smoothie? It is pretty filling and amazing for mood issues too

I eat the bone marrow plain and raw. I love it like that.  yum!

You need to go hunting, Timothy..... i am sure you will find something beautiful.... go hunting without meaning to hunt....
I have realized... being an explorer... there will always happen interesting stuff

Maybe you can massage your dads feet? My dad loves that more than anything.... I have massaged his feet all the years as I grew up.. I bet it did him some good  he is pretty healthy now
You will figure out a way to help him, that he likes too, I am sure! Just be patient and make baby steps  and always remember to take care of you first

My trips to the river are satisfying my adventurous side, as well as getting me some cold. I said, perhaps foolishly, that I want to go in the sea on Christmas day, and I need to prepare so I am geeing myself up for a dip in the river soon.

I will add bone marrow to my list of things to try. I'm going to make your herring recipe this weekend; it sounds really good!
I see the MHS mentioned with increasing frequency. I figured i'd be generous and save more of them for you guys....

It's always difficult with family, and more so when they do not show any real problems as a result of their lifestyle. That said my dad's mum is a maverick; kicking ass and taking names in her 70's, so I think he has probably inherited a better set of genes than many.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-31T22:52:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145832>

> [2Timothy1:7 said:](/goto/post?id=144731)
>
> On the magnetic theme i'd also be interested on any input on magnets for local use with injuries.

I remember Dr. Bonlie talking in a podcast somewhere about magnets for localised healing. A main point he made was that their use should follow a basic tenet of acupuncture that limits the amount of time for use (something like 1 hour per week but not sure). This was because when you use them your are drawing resources to the injury/area that might be needed elsewhere. It seems our bodies have a list of priorities independent from what we think we need or want and you wouldnt want to starve your vital organs to fix youre wrist or ankle if resources are limited.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-01T16:22:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145862>

> You can leave the garlic out and use only ginger... i like it even better then! And if you want you can add curry spices too.. or fresh dill.....
> I cannot tell my boyfriend I still do the fish head smoothies... he made me promise not to.... but i cannot leave them out so I just do them secretly
> i understand he thinks it is yucky! haha... but man are they good for mood
> so if you feel down, Timothy, just make a MHS  great medicine  just hold your nose and pretend you drink chocolate

I'll put it all in there the first time, and alter it in the future. I'm going to leave the honey out though.
I could probably make the smoothie at least palatable with some ionion and spices. Just hope my food processor is strong enough to break those suckers down!
I'm off work tomorrow, and a dip in the river is on the agenda.

> I remember Dr. Bonlie talking in a podcast somewhere about magnets for localised healing. A main point he made was that their use should follow a basic tenet of acupuncture that limits the amount of time for use (something like 1 hour per week but not sure). This was because when you use them your are drawing resources to the injury/area that might be needed elsewhere. It seems our bodies have a list of priorities independent from what we think we need or want and you wouldnt want to starve your vital organs to fix youre wrist or ankle if resources are limited.

That's very interesting, thanks, and makes sense I guess. Often our priorities are not our deeper biological need.
I have listened to Dr Bonlie, his episode on the Live to 110 podcast was very good. I would really like a magnetico sleep pad; might have to save up a little longer and get the 10 gauss pad as i'm not sure how much benefit is afforded by the 5 gauss model (even though i'm pretty healthy, I think)

### kovita — 2014-11-01T19:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145870>

> [2Timothy1:7 said:](/goto/post?id=145820)
>
> Haven't been very good at updating this, as i'm not entirely sure what my journal is right now. Whereas many are tracking their health conditions my challenges lie more in being decisive to move closer to the life that I want for myself and whomever I will share it with.
> Keeping my body temperature on the lower side, but not to a point where it makes me miserable, really helps with this and I hope that by achieving small wins I will build up the confidence to take more and more calculated risks.
>
> I am really trying to increase the seafood at the moment. Though I don't have a problem with oysters I just don't find them very satisfying.
> Raw prawns and salmon are my favourites and i'm going to make Inger's pickled herrings as soon as the fishmonger has them in. Since really increasing seafood consumption the most notable thing is increased tolerance in situations which would normally have me irritated. A calmness that I have not always possessed.
>
> Another challenge for me right now, and something I have continually tried with occasional small successes, is to help my family. Most notably my dad as in my eyes he's at the greatest risk of trouble. He needs this blog, but I know that it will take me to get the key principles through to him.
> He has been a competitive athlete much of his life, racing up to ironman distance, marathons and ultras, swimming... and after a spell of just being generally active is now training for similar events. This wouldn't necessarily be an issue but for the fact that he has followed a standard diet for almost his whole life (apart from the brief times I have been able to steer him onto a path more conducive to health)
> Gluten, dairy etc etc and although 85% of the diet is home cooked and by no means a train wreck by most standards it is carb heavy with no respect for seasons, there is snacking on biscuits and his evening meals are basically whatever he and my mum prepare.
>
> The home itself is well away from the biggies when it comes to EMF, but wifi is always on, he carries his phone, has an ipad all of that stuff. Will watcjh TV up until going to bed with all the lights on.
> Oh and he's only recently retired from a job which was either behind a computer in the room where the wifi router is, or travelling across the globe ~8-10 times per year for two week stretches. Prior to this he worked as an electrician.
> I couldn't make this stuff up, and I doubt it could be a lot worse unless he lived under a phone mast.
>
> Every time I visit we'll have some conversation or another, and i'll again be labelled a crackpot for turning the lights off/grounding/fasting/eating raw meat and fish and generally being counter culture. They will both concede that much of what I say makes sense, less so my mum who is a nurse practitioner, but it will not be enough to affect change.
> My dad wouldn't read a book or a blog, and is sceptical about the more 'fringe' things that I say which is why no changes have been made at home regarding lighting/EMF.
>
> I understand as a parent that it would be hard to take direction from your son but I know that he does look to me for guidance. Perhaps i'll have have to come at this from a different angle?
> I read jack's blog about having to visit his father in law in hospital, and that in this same way Jack had given him the information but it wasn't implemented. I would hate for something to happen before the necessary changes were made.

Tim, I can share with you the experience i made in my own family. I have been occasionally telling them what I do, why I do it and what it has brought to my life. No pushing at all, just sharing in suitable moments and discussions. I have discovered a lot of surprising thoughts in the heads of my immediate family members. this is just wonderfull, beacuse I feel like I finally started to get them know really. With the time, they started to come to me telling me how good i started to look gradually and how big is being the change in the mental/personality level. So it is being reciprocal process. I am being given a lot of great things and I share the best of what I have learnt and experienced. I have fo rexample discovered that my mother's mystical tendencies i always hated are intersted enough to consider them in the context of hard science and it. ertainly helped me to open myself to the word where the thoughts and feelings are the ultimate tool of wellbeing. This has helped me tremendously on the health nd well eing level. On the practical side they slowly started to experiement with ketosis, endurance sports in the ketosis, seasonality and circadian rhytms in the nature and CT. All by themselfes. they usually tell me when they already experience some benefits or just "good feelings". This autumn the whole family started to go for a swim to the lake where they live. Together and regulary and in every weather and it has united them and broughts them a lot of new experiences lived together even it is just a swim and not hardcore CT. I can see that even a babysteps matter a lot.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-02T20:11:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-145947>

So, as planned I went to the river, and I took the plunge!
Despite the intense cold I felt so happy! When I got out I put on my shoes and walked home 20mins in my wet clothes to get in more cold exposure. I was singing to myself at the top of my voice like a crazy person - high on the cold - and people were driving past and staring at me like "is that guy really walking around dripping wet in the cold wind?" and I was thinking HELL YES I AM!
Allowed myself to warm up naturally by stripping off when I got home and have since felt both energised and sleepy.
Attached is photographic evidence

### Inger — 2014-11-02T20:40:25+0200

haha... did you plunge with your clothes on...????  lol
I always dip nude... I did today too... I go almost every day to the tiny lake in he woods.. 20 min. walk.. mostly as the sun sets... the water in the lake is black... it calls me! and I just take off my clothes and swim a short round and shake off the water and put my clothes on... who needs a towel? I feel so amazing after it is so worth it every time    So glad you plunged too    next time nude! It is even better

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-12T17:22:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-147147>

Thanks for filling my journal with comments; I like that people are coming to join me.

reporting in because last night I had, for the first time ever, horrendous sleep.
I woke at 00:30am and wasn't able to get back to sleep.
I woke up and knew that any kind of training would be stupid, so I ate a BAB a la leptin reset and noticed: I have gone off the freakin' deep end today. Like, I could eat EVERYTHING.
I have also been significantly hotter than usual. The latter makes sense because of the protein load in the A.M, but my appetite has been outta control.
Does this likely have more to do with the bad night of sleep? Or eating breakfast for the first time in ages and ages? Is it indicative of anything?
I have, for as long as I can remember, been training fasted in the A.M and eating a single meal around 1pm. Today I have felt like a bottomless pit.

It is interesting because I have been thinking a lot recently, and this time feels like i'm on the brink of some radical changes.

I am curious to hear, but do not wish to start another thread, whether a protocol such as the leptin reset could be the difference between a 9-11% body fat and a consistent single digit body fat?
Could a persistent bout of tendonitis be indicative of an HS CRP that is out of whack?

### Inger — 2014-11-12T20:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-147172>

I do not know the answer to you question Timothy... all I know is, if I slept too little or bad I might have increased appetite the next day too

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-19T17:44:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-147827>

> I do not know the answer to you question Timothy... all I know is, if I slept too little or bad I might have increased appetite the next day too

Yeah that was definitely the case; need to chill out a bit bout that! It corrected itself in subsequent days and found that I compensated such that it likely evened out.
Weird that I never really paid for that night of little sleep. It almost felt like my body didn't want any more sleep if that's even possible. I am getting 8-10 hours every night though.

Have been enjoying myself just recently.
On monday I went in the river again, but then followed this with a longer walk in my wet clothes of around 50 minutes. This was sooooo much fun, as my route took me through the woods, and past a rugby club which had tractor tyres on the pitch so I went over and flipped it around for a bit.
I found that immediately prior to getting into the river I had an anticipatory rush of heat which made it a lot easier to get straight in there, and my walk afterwards was euphoria-inducing.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-26T18:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-148565>

I'm going to try and update this more frequently as I think it helps to get out of my own way by putting things down in writing.
Since beginning this log I have seen big improvements in my mood which can be a problem at times as i'll become overly critical of myself and others, often fearful of the future, regretful of the past and lonely.
Things that I think have helps this are: more seafood & CT (no surprises there) and also feelings of attaction to someone that I have not felt for years. Which has conributed the most I could not conclude, but in concert they've been wonderful.
Another thing I have done is addressed a zinc deficiency and IT IS INCREDIBLE. Seriously, I cannot even express how successful this has been/is. I am currently continuing with this but already can conclude that nobody should expect to address it without a focused effort.

I'm going to be honest, i'm struggling with the blog. I can't seem to get it now that it's gone all physics on our asses; totally out of my comfort zone and as much as I want to understand the nature of things the subject matter just doesn't appeal to me in the slightest; perhaps I should request that he blog be written in fairy tale format which I would find more accessible? I'd like to get all hot about this quantum business but I can't get my head around it.
This isn't helped by the fact that I hate reading on a computer and find it hard to focus as opposed to a book, but hey, the information is free and hat is so good of Jack to do.
It feels that there are so many moving parts in all of this, which appear at times to be contradictory.

I do think hat it is also very simple, and that this explanation is not necessarily required; the fingers pointing at the moon you could say.
I love the freedom of nature, and being connected to it when possible. I love movement and physical performance. I love nourishing my body with high quality foods and I am thankful to God everyday that I can do these things when so many have struggles.

I find it funny that I was lead to various protocols/lifestyle choices before I understood the true effects they would have, for example fasted training which I have engaged in for around 5 years now, a low carb/ketoenic type diet, grounding, avoiding blue light etc. The blog and the book have then taught me why I benefited from these things.

I have found recently that I have quite pronounced fluctuations in my appetite and will usually go for several days without any major hunger, and then maybe one day where I feel like I overeat, but in actuality is probably a day of redressing the balance which is a natural thing to do.

As I haven't ever written this out i'll give an idea as to what I am currently doing
AM fasted training between 1 & 4 hours in length which can consist of pool running (see below), mountain biking, swimming, strength training (barbell/dumbbell, body weight and kettlebell) and currently very litle running (again see below) - usually a combination of disciplines.

Get home, cold shower and go to work.

First meal anywhere from 2-4 hours later. Currently just one meal a day, but I can be flexible with this if I need to (my fast is usually 20+ hours)

Blue blockers after sunset-ish

Sleep usually 8.30/9pm

Supplements - Betaine HCl, zinc, bitter melon

CT in the river twice a week

What I need to work on

Eating only out of hunger, as sometimes a meal will be larger than I actually need not based upon my appetite but because of another emotion that I am feeling, or i'll eat earlier than I need to just out of habit, when in fact I feel at time that I could fast even longer.

Not eating nuts - there are better choices and they tend to make me want more once I begin eating them which is a sure sign that I shouldn't bother with them. Sometimes I feel like my body is asking for them, but I can't think of anything in there that could not be obtained from other sources.

Beating a case of tendonitis in my lower leg which has plagued me or a while now. I am currently at the stage of testing it a little with very short runs but my main workouts are in the pool and on the bike.

Tidying up my appearance

And, purely for vanity, i'd like to drop the remaining fat that stands between 'athletic' and 'would happily take m shirt off in front of people anytime anywhere'
Right now I don't know what it is that is standing in between me and something that although I feel is a horrible goal is something that i'd like to achieve. It could be raininjg volume, which I know is not 'optimal' in terms of health, it could be eating too much (or too few) of a macronutrient or calories (as whether calories matter is one of those things that seems like a grey area, though I have read excellent posts on the topic on this forum and blog), it could be that I need more CT, it could be that I need to change my environment. I just don't know.

Audience participation is encouraged

### fitness@home — 2014-11-26T19:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-148579>

Good update Timothy.

I still read every blog as they come out, but find there are times it is over my head. Then I recall that's exactly how i felt about some of the older blogs and now I do understand them . Just keep learning!

### Inger — 2014-11-27T17:47:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-148645>

So glad you are doing better

Are you sure you are eating enough? IDK if it is worth it to strive for a ripped body.... IMHO there are way more important issues to be worked at on this planet earth
Sure if it does not take much of your energy and comes naturally and is fun it is great... but i am kind of careful to be too obsessed with how the body looks.....? It is perfect when it works and feels like it did as a kid..... that is how I measure it  1-4 hours training every day is pretty much.. and you might need to eat more then. IDK..... some eat less and train more and still gain muscles... I am no expert in this at all, to be honest. I guess it depends on so many things. It is your won N=1 ans you will figure it out

Nuts have many good things in them but I have the same issue like you... man they are so good at times I can barely stop eating...lol I just had a LOT of raw, organic pistachios... I just craved them
Should stop eating nuts soon, it goes into winter here...
It might help if you only buy nuts within the shell, that way you will sure eat less. I do that a lot, macadamias and hazelnuts with shell, pistachios too

### caroline — 2014-11-28T00:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-148673>

Hi Tim ..... you seem to be doing so well with C/T. I try not to buy nuts very often - can't stop eating them!

If I have a very poor nite's sleep ..... I am starving all day.

### 2Timothy1:7 — 2014-11-29T13:20:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cold-never-bothered-me-anyway.11871/post-148760>

> So glad you are doing better
>
> Are you sure you are eating enough? IDK if it is worth it to strive for a ripped body.... IMHO there are way more important issues to be worked at on this planet earth
> Sure if it does not take much of your energy and comes naturally and is fun it is great... but i am kind of careful to be too obsessed with how the body looks.....? It is perfect when it works and feels like it did as a kid..... that is how I measure it  1-4 hours training every day is pretty much.. and you might need to eat more then. IDK..... some eat less and train more and still gain muscles... I am no expert in this at all, to be honest. I guess it depends on so many things. It is your won N=1 ans you will figure it out

> Should stop eating nuts soon, it goes into winter here...

Attainment of that goal is very close, and I feel that it is coming more as a by-product of striving to do what is right for health. I learned this lesson through an eating disorder.
I also think you may be right about needing to eat more, and that perhaps my appetite for nuts is probably because they are calorically dense, and the body ain't stupid.
I am doing better, for numerous reasons I think, and I am hopeful. There's also someone that I care about greatly, that makes me laugh everyday and gives off good vibrations, but i'm yet to act upon this yet as I sometimes think that unrequited love is more exciting and as we work together it could be awkward.

> Hi Tim ..... you seem to be doing so well with C/T. I try not to buy nuts very often - can't stop eating them!
>
> If I have a very poor nite's sleep ..... I am starving all day.

CT is really enjoyable, and as it is in the river it's a similar progression to Milo and the bull, only in my case the water is getting colder, as we head further into winter, and I get more tolerant of the cold. It leaves me so invigorated, and as I also use cold showers daily and turn the heating off in my room with the window open I actually don't feel it as being too much of a challenge.
As I said above, I think the nut thing is more just my body's smart way of offsetting a substantial deficit, and it's just about replacing them with better choices.

> Good update Timothy.
>
> I still read every blog as they come out, but find there are times it is over my head. Then I recall that's exactly how i felt about some of the older blogs and now I do understand them . Just keep learning!

Yes i'll keep wading through them. Currently reading tensegrity 9 in short pieces, with each paragraph being scoured multiple times as I hope tat repetition will get it to click.
I'll get it eventually!

### fitness@home — 2014-10-10T19:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/noticed-new-item-at-grocery-store-today.11872/post-143427>

Peanut butter advertised as Omega-3. Contains anchovy, sardine, and tilapia oils.

Just eat the fish oils in their natural package!

On the other hand, although it may be skewed, the message seems to be getting across if it is now showing up in regular processed fare from the grocery store.

# Thread: Galvanized steel tub for CT, good bad neither both?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2014-10-10T20:09:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-143434>

Anyone using galvanized steel tub for CT? Any reason I should or shouldnt?

Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-10T20:45:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-143438>

Good....but be careful because metal gets a lot colder than water.......you can develop frost bite if your skin is on the metal.....but it is way more CT power.

### Shijin13 — 2014-10-10T20:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-143440>

So @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) are you heading out to tractor supply to pick up a 100gal galvanized tank???

### cantweight — 2014-10-10T21:19:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-143442>

Yeah the outside one gets too icky and freezes fast. Thinking we'll stick this one in the basement shower. Only metal comes in dimensions that fit in there.

### cantweight — 2014-10-10T21:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-143443>

Just have to be careful not to get frostbite on my ass !

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-10-23T04:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/galvanized-steel-tub-for-ct-good-bad-neither-both.11873/post-144791>

I suggest some nice pieces of stone to sit on. Sandstone flagstone, or granite. I want to put them in my bathtub! You could layer the entire bottom of the tub with natural stone and increase your grounding potential, plus some natural radiation. Have you tested the mV of your teeth to address if they are healthy?

Other alloys, such as nickel in the metal base of bridges and under porcelain crowns, have been documented to lower the T-4 and T-8 lymphocyte levels (David Eggleston,DDS. "Effect of Dental Amalgam and Nickel Alloys on T-Lymphocytes: Preliminary Report", J. Prosthetic Dent, 1984, 51(5);617-623.) In predisposed patients, exposure to nickel can be a contributory factor in the development of cancer of the lungs, nasal passages and larynx.
Any metal materials in the mouth such as gold crowns, chrome cobalt partial dentures, mercury fillings, titanium implants, etc. will set the stage for galvanic currents. In 1985, a research team (A. Knappworst, E. Gura, D. Fuhrmann and A. Enginalev) revealed that when mercury fillings were in close proximity to gold crowns, the **mercury release was ten times greater** when compared to mercury fillings alone (p132. Mercury Poisoning from Dental Amalgam- a Hazard to Human Brain by Patrick Stortebecker, MD, Ph.D. published in USA by Bio Probe, Orlando, FL).

### Sammy — 2014-10-15T06:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/october-2014-webinar-vitamin-d-non-native-emf-effects-and-light.11877/post-143891>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=143815)
>
> Not true.......i have never has one of my amalgams removed. I fixed my T reg cells and there is no issue.

Hi Dr. Kruse! In your latest podcast, you mentioned that **electrons activate T regulatory cells**. Could you please elaborate this a bit further? I major in immunology and I am trying to connect what I learn at uni and the information here.

Are you saying electrons activate already existing T regulatory cells(naive/anergic even?)? Or do electrons induce the development of T reg cells in the thymus? Or both? If you have the time....could you explain the mechanism for this?

My understanding so far......
Lack of electrons = more inflammation => more stimulatory dendritic cells => more self reactive T cells become activated => autoimmunity
More electrons = lower inflammation => more tolerogenic dendritic cells => self reactive T cells become anergic/die by apoptosis => self-tolerance/protection

### Sammy — 2014-10-15T06:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/october-2014-webinar-vitamin-d-non-native-emf-effects-and-light.11877/post-143894>

I've thought about it some more....

T regs actually have moderate affinity to self proteins. This means that they are constantly activated under physiological conditions(80% sure about this...). But if cells are constantly activated, they use up more energy and die faster which is bad because we want these T regs to live long and prosper to protect us.

If there's one thing I learnt from this site: electrons = energy

In this case, it's not exactly the electrons that are activating the T regs, rather having electrons means that T reg cell activity can be sustained.

### caroline — 2014-10-15T13:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/october-2014-webinar-vitamin-d-non-native-emf-effects-and-light.11877/post-143912>

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-15T14:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/october-2014-webinar-vitamin-d-non-native-emf-effects-and-light.11877/post-143919>

> [Sammy said:](/goto/post?id=143894)
>
> I've thought about it some more....
>
> T regs actually have moderate affinity to self proteins. This means that they are constantly activated under physiological conditions(80% sure about this...). But if cells are constantly activated, they use up more energy and die faster which is bad because we want these T regs to live long and prosper to protect us.
>
> If there's one thing I learnt from this site: electrons = energy
>
> In this case, it's not exactly the electrons that are activating the T regs, rather having electrons means that T reg cell activity can be sustained.

Correct. The key is the cells become active in the thymus where we have lots of electron density when we are younger. These electrons are what turn them on. TO turn off a T regulator cell you just need to remove a few of their electrons. To turn them back on you need to reenergize them in the plasma or interstitial fluid.

**Lighting up the world**
This year's Nobel Prize in Physics goes to Isamu Akasaki Meijo University, Nagoya, Japan and Nagoya University, Japan, Hiroshi Amano Nagoya University, Japan and Shuji Nakamura University of California, Santa Barbara, CA, USA “for the invention of efficient blue light-emitting diodes which has enabled bright and energy-saving white light sources”. This is most definitely a discovery in physics, but as with so many discoveries in other sciences, chemistry underpins the research. Akasaki, Amano and Nakamura produced bright blue light from semiconductor devices based on gallium nitride and related compounds in the early 1990s. Akasaki worked together with Amano at the University of Nagoya, while Nakamura was employed at Nichia Chemicals, a small company in Tokushima. Blue LEDs found their way into optical storage via the blue laser diode and are used to produce low-energy white light LEDs for domestic and other applications.
