### Josh — 2014-10-11T21:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-effects-calcium-efflux-and-more-in-images-and-maps.11878/post-143536>

Cilia:

> Cilia are microtubule-based organelles that project like antennae from the surface of most cells in the body. Motile cilia move fluid past cells, for example mucus in the airway. Non-motile primary cilia, however, transduce a multitude of sensory stimuli, including chemical concentrations of growth factors, hormones, odorants, and developmental morphogens, as well as osmolarity, light intensity, and fluid flow. Cilia have evolved a complex ultrastructure to accommodate these diverse functions, and an extensive molecular machinery has developed to support the assembly of these organelles. Defects in the cilia themselves, or the machinery required to assemble them, lead to a broad spectrum of human disease symptoms, including polycystic kidney disease, nephronophthisis, hydrocephalus, polydactyly, situs inversus, retinal degeneration, and obesity. While these diseases highlight the pivotal roles of cilia in physiology and development, the mechanistic link between cilia, physiology, and disease remains unclear.

[http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(06)01831-8](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822\(06\)01831-8)

### Josh — 2014-10-11T21:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-effects-calcium-efflux-and-more-in-images-and-maps.11878/post-143539>

> Figure 2. Role of cilia in left–right symmetry
> breaking.
> Three models for how rotational movement
> of cilia leads to leftward fluid flow.
> (The cartoon represents a ventral view of
> the animal with the left side of the animal
> being to the right of the cone.) (A) Tilted
> rotation leading to a leftward flow layer
> above the surface and a rightward flow
> layer near the surface, as proposed by
> Cartwright and co-workers \[84\]. The rightward
> flow predicted by this model is not
> observed experimentally. (B) No-slip
> boundary condition at surface (depicted by the hand) causes the rightward phase of rotation to be ineffective in moving fluid, while
> the leftward phase, which is less constrained by the surface boundary layer, is able to move fluid productively towards the left \[85\].
> The ability of this model to generate flow has been verified with a mechanical model \[85\]. (C) Intrinsic asymmetry in beating cycle,
> so that the leftward velocity is higher than the rightward velocity, which requires prior symmetry breaking \[87\].

[http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(06)01831-8](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822\(06\)01831-8)

### Josh — 2014-10-11T21:10:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-effects-calcium-efflux-and-more-in-images-and-maps.11878/post-143540>

> Figure 3. Models for nodal flow sensing.
> (A) Classic morphogen accumulation model. A secreted molecule (X) makes a concentration gradient
> by leftward flow (red arrow). Okada et al. \[86\] estimated the molecular weight of X to be 15–50
> kDa con- sidering effects of diffusion (green arrows) and returning flow (blue arrow). (B) Original
> two-cilia model proposed by McGrath et al. \[88\]. Perinodal cilia are immotile and act as
> PKD2-bearing mechanosensors. They are bent at the left side to cause cal- cium influx (Ca). (C) A
> modified form of the two-cilia model that is more hydrodynami- cally feasible. Mechanosensory cilia
> are di- rectionally oriented so as to cause calcium influx only in response to lateral bending away
> from the midline of the embryo. (D) Nodal vesicular parcel model. Multivesicu- lar parcels are
> pinched off from node cells, blown leftward, and smashed against the left wall of the node to
> release inner vesi- cles containing signal molecules, Sonic hedgehog and retinoic acid \[90\].

[http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(06)01831-8](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822\(06\)01831-8)

# Thread: CT with low body temp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-low-body-temp.11879/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### david weiss — 2014-10-12T17:20:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-low-body-temp.11879/post-143593>

Hi All,

I am new to the forum. Just read jack's book and am spending a lot of time on the web site. I have adrenal fatigue, thyroid issues, and weak digestion. I have been eating really clean for over a year, high fat, medium protein low card. Still not making a lot of progress though. I am 57 years old, 5'10" and 150 pounds. Been exercising most of my life, but in the last year have cut back to just walking.

I would like to start doing CT, but my body temperature is always low, usually around 97.7. I am cold most of the time and have cold hands and feet. Is it okay to start CT even if I have low body temperature? Also should I do the Leptin reset before I start CT? I thought maybe I don't need the Leptin reset since my body weight is normal. Thanks, really appreciate the help.

Vid
New Member

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-12T20:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-low-body-temp.11879/post-143603>

Dear Vid:

The short answer is yes on both. CT always help any eutherian mammal. The Leptin Rx is best for overweight folks because it works on light cycles. Your temp is low because you have a lack of iodine in choroid plexus and thyroid. Tensegrity 6, the current blog tells you exactly why. Welcome............and read this.

Just thinking about the impact of WW I and WW II. Many of the countries that fought in these wars did it in alliances. Alliances bring the numbers we need, they bring in shared values and purpose and they target same enemy or same opportunities. It also means that they have to use a shared strategy to attack the enemy. If we use this principle: ''Alliances'' I guess it takes us to a new level of doing things. Getting to a new level of doing things makes you realize that where medicine is today is quite offensive to your health span. Working with others here at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/) makes the burden lighter, brings a broader view of understanding the issues, involves others in our cause and builds a winning strategy. True leaders don't go it alone. They chart the way through a shared vision and shared execution and in most cases they win by building starfish that help light the pathway for other who find us later.........................The message is the same.......the time you join is immaterial.

Being in a leadership position of several alliances over the years has been a rewarding experience. Alliances can make us strong, but they can also weaken us in many respects. There is a great feeling of reward when you tackle a goal together. It is almost quantum magic. However, they are downfalls to a team based approach as well. Sometimes egos gets in the way, which is why there needs to be a negotiator. However, if everyone understands the "ALL","I" and "ACE" in alliance then it works out for the better. For those that have never played volleyball "ACE" is when you hit it to the other team on a serve without hitting it back, and that can very well be accomplished with an alliance. It really does not matter who's "TEAM" you are on, but understanding that you are on a bigger "TEAM" an "Alliance" that is for each other regardless of where you stand. When you begin.......it might seem like an insurmountable task. It is not. If I did it, so can you.

Remain very optimistic when you get here and read all that is here. The owner of this site is an eternal optimist. Do not think of today's failures, but of the success that may come tomorrow. You have set yourselves a difficult task by coming here, but you will succeed if you persevere throughout all the words I have written; and you will find a joy in overcoming obstacles that your environment and biology challenge you with. Remember, no effort that we make to attain something beautiful is ever lost. A challenge only becomes an obstacle when you bow to it. Here we do not. We face it daily. Nothing is better for self-esteem than optimal survival. When I began, I wrote down 10 things you would do in my life I would do if I had absolutely no fear of repercussions. Then I picked one and did it. [www.JackKruse.com](http://www.JackKruse.com/) is that one pick...............My burden is my purpose. Without it, I wouldn't be. Never be afraid to innovate. Fear will do one thing and one thing only: hold you back from an Optimal Life. No glass ceiling was ever shattered by a whiner but those windows were made clear by a doer who used windex on the glass to focus and make the future they want. Somebody built lighthouses to see people through storms. Then they built storms to remind people to find lighthouses. This place is built by one of those people.......think differently. Innovate what you want.

**Good results are difficult when indifference predominates. You must seek your answer. When we are seeking our answer, we cannot be indifferent about the questions we ask, because we want the answer that brings transformation. So, the first questions that lead to the right answers we must ask of ourselves: Am I making a positive deposit into my future? What will I reap from this decision? What is motivating me to take this action? When we ask ourselves the right questions we will lead ourselves to the right answers. The problem is not to find the answer, it's to face the answer. Begin to live with the answer than die with the question.
Adding value to people doesn't always show up in the present. Sometimes you get to see it when you have moved on from that area, person, and then they come into your present again. The things that they are doing, the places they have been and what they have accomplished comes out. Yet it started when you added value to them in some way in the past. So just keep innovating with your inner circle to better our world. Always reevaluate your caucus. Strive for perfect 10's, not comfortable 5's in your circle of influence!

Nature is always fundamental in health but no one wants to understand it is at is smallest levels to see why we are so off track. This is why I tell people to still read my blog even if the do not understand quantum physics. What I write is important for all living things. **_The goal of the blog has always been to convince you not to turn away your attention because you do not understand the material. It is to draw your consciousness in to peer into how nature really works and light your curiosity on fire._** QED has always be relevant to me but its concepts are not self evident , nor parsimonious until you see the world through nature's laws first. The problem for mankind is that they don’t look at biology through a quantum lens.........but they must. I am just putting some windex on their glass eyes.

On my blog we focus in on the "why" of things. Today begin to focus in on the "how" of things. The "how" of things really is a means to an end. Once you know the answer to your "why", your "how" will fall into place. "How" includes decisions and choices.

How requires questioning.

How requires education.

How includes a plan.

How includes actions steps.

How requires consistency.

How is...Hunting On Ways. Each blog is a hunt for a new nugget of insight for your consciousness to add to your growing wisdom.

Become addicted to the things that nature does. It is the only way we can make it through the things we choose to do and remain healthy. Nature is the only ‘straight edge’ when you are the architect of your blueprint for life………We only command nature when we obey her rules.

Enlightenment is a destructive process to the pessimist and a constructive one to the optimist. It has nothing and something to do with becoming better or happier. Enlightenment is the crumbling of a former untruth or the reveal of a new understanding of a former superposition. It is seeing through the facade of pretense. It is a changing of the future by a new perspective. It is the complete eradication of everything we imagined to be true……..it is truth set free by a new observation of thought. This is precisely the basis of Schrodinger’s live/dead cat paradox………in real life in case you do not realize it. Our observed reality and perception of things are colored by our perception and observation of it during the time we see it……..with time that perception can change and what we see no longer remains. This is the Ying and Yang of how human thought works in a quantum fashion.

The goal is optimizing health span and minimizing illness. That is what "Newbies" should expect at [www.jackkruse.com](http://www.jackkruse.com/)

The ultimate control of your life leads to optimal health and compliant design; autonomy leads to engagement and action.
**

Live until you die. Tap your enthusiasm today and every day from here on out. Act with more enthusiasm than you feel. Live your truth. Express love. Share enthusiasm. Take action towards your dreams. Walk the talk. Dance and sing to your music. Embrace blessings. Make today worth remembering. Share what you will learn here.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-low-body-temp.11879/post-143800>

still works. Why? Cold increase melatonin. melatonin removes bad water from your body and limits cytokine storms. This stimulates the choroid plexus to add iodine to your new water your drinking. Think.......when you get in cold don't you want to pee right away? This is why. You are getting rid of H20 that has poor hydrogen bonding network. The mainstream ancestral folks don't get it when it comes to things like iodine and T3 and T4. One of the great things about seafood is that it contains selenium which helps convert thyroid hormones to active T3. While you can eat more carbs to increase free T3 levels (a common paleo recommendation), this increases the need for iodine ([http://www.medical-hypotheses.com/.../S0306-9877.../abstract](http://www.medical-hypotheses.com/article/S0306-9877\(04\)00169-0/abstract)). Most of the T4 is stored in our gut, so by eating seafood, you allow form to meet function as the seafood can permeate the gut where majority of T4 is stored, allowing for the conversion of more T4 to T3. While DHA is pretty amazing, it needs to be paired with iodine (as that's the body's preferred antioxidant system) to protect DHA from oxidation. This is why TENSEGRITY 6 IS A CRITICAL BLOG. So, we've come full circle. You can either eat seafood and get a great evolutionary package of things like DHA, iodine and selenium, which will support thyroid health, or you can follow the traditional paleo path lacking in seafood DHA and iodine and continually up your carb intake, but this will trigger a need for more iodine and since you're not getting it, your body will divert to backup antioxidant systems like vitamin C, vitamin E and glutathione. Now you see why so many paleo folks eat lots of carbs and take supplements like vitamin C year-round. They're forcing their bodies to rely on a suboptimal pathway which requires this constant band-aid approach.

# Thread: Another Newbie in Need...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie-in-need.11881/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Athanay — 2014-10-13T05:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie-in-need.11881/post-143687>

Hello kind folks. I have been following Jack's work more recently and am brand new to the forum, this being my first post. I'm fully aware that posting my own problems and asking for suggestions may not be the best introduction to a community such as this. My hope in doing so, aside from getting advice and encouragement from a great forum, is to offer another thread of experience for others to possibly learn from. I have every intention of keeping progress reports along the way, as I continue along the healing path. This place is truly amazing and I hope to be able to contribute more and more as I continue to absorb all of this information. I apologize in advance for the length of this post, and will post the test results and current doctor recommended supplementation at the bottom, should you want to skip the trial and error portion of this essay.

My symptoms started with general digestion issues a few years ago. I had been a vegetarian for several years, and a lazy/uneducated one at times. I ate a lot of carbs in the form of all the bad things we know about these days. I also had stiff muscles and general aches and pains in day to day movement. A pretty intense chronic fatigue began to set in and lingers still, along with the aches and pains. My sleep was erratic at the time and I supplemented with a fair amount of coffee. I just kept crashing over and over again.

An early and limited blood panel (Page 3-5 on attached file '12' - LabCorp/Lauren Johnston), from an allopathic doctor, revealed only a Vit D deficiency, according to her. I have supplemented since, and levels were improved on more recent testing.

I eventually went to a naturopath (over 2 years ago at this point) who focused on chiropractic, nutrition and general detox. She used kinesiology (which never had me all that confident) to tell me I had all sorts of food allergies and leaky gut. She also ran a saliva hormone test (Page 3 on attached file '9' - Diagnos-Techs, Inc) and said I had high estrogen and adrenal exhaustion. This was all new to me so I just had to trust her for the most part. She gave me several supplements (fish oil, adrenal support, zypan and others) and by cutting out many trigger foods my digestion did improve, at least the major discomfort issues. After several months of expensive adjustments and continued fatigue I stopped going. She kept saying adrenal fatigue can take up to 18 months to recover from, but I just loss faith in her for one reason or another. All along I was not eating animal products (ethical reasons + 'supposed' dairy allergy) and was eating heavy grains and legumes, like, all the time.

I moved on to a chinese medicine and acupuncture clinic, but after a few months there was a petering out as well. When I consistently inquired about diet he always just said to eat 'good, nourishing' foods. Just wasn't getting results, aside from the relaxation that often comes from acupuncture.

I eventually started getting more into BulletProof and paleo related dieting and began to consume animal products about a year and half ago. I was hoping for some wow factor, where my shirt would burst open as I sprinted down the street with a grass-fed torch in my hand, no such luck. The transition didn't cause any bad reactions but they didn't seem to help either. After many trials with elimination diets (eggs, dairy, grains etc.) I still can't tell whether I actually have any real allergies/sensitivities. I never get obvious reactions that would indicate a specific allergy, but I have found that certain foods (eggs, salmon, some dairy) consistently make me feel crappy - I get tired and my body feels almost poisoned and tense. I never had food intolerances prior to all this. I eat completely BP/paleo, have been off all wheat/gluten/processed food/corn/soy etc for two years, but still I feel pretty crummy most of the time. Low energy, stiff, scaly skin, dry hair, dark circles under eyes, all the classics. The circles under my eyes in the morning are profound, and my once thick and flowing hair has been reduced to a dry, thin, and frizzy mess.

I started seeing a new naturopath in August of 2013. He did some blood work (Page 1-2 on attached file '12' - LabCorp) and noted that my iron levels were high. He thought this could be causing several of the problems and may be indicative of hemochromatosis. I tried to donate blood (only real way to rid the male body of iron) but had to wait a bit, based on some traveling I did in malaria zones earlier that year. He himself has hemochromatosis and said several of his symptoms were relieved when he began consistently donating blood. He gave me a couple types of digestive enzymes and had put me on pregnenolone to stimulate some basic hormone production, with little results after a few months of use. I have since donated blood a few times, but it hasn't resulted in any major shift in symptoms. I have felt a little better since lowering the iron (more recent tests have shown normal ranges) but again, no major recovery. Not sure what made it so high, but may have been reintroducing large amounts of meat after not eating any for 4-5 years.

I suspect my body might not be digesting fats particularly well, or that my gut is just depleted in general. I went from eating little fat for several years to eating a ton of it over the last year or so. I rarely, if ever, have any digestive discomfort (gas, bloating etc) but until recently I wasn't at all regular with bowel movements, and have had many periods where I only go every 2-3 days.

I used to get better sleep, but still never felt all that rested, but recently my sleep has gotten pretty bad. I can't fall asleep despite being tired and often wake early and can't fall back asleep.

There have been periods where I've had consistent muscle twitching, mostly in legs but other parts of the body as well. It mostly occurs when sitting and especially when laying. Circulation has been poor all along, with legs and arms falling asleep much easier than they used to. Back discomfort is an issue as well.

I have been seeing a new functional medicine doctor in Asheville for the last couple months and he ran a few tests after my evaluation (Page 1-2 on attached file '9' and Page 1-8 on attached file '1') Several B vitamins were quite low along with my DHEA. I was surprised that my cortisol pattern was in normal range, as I seem to go up and down so much with energy throughout the day, and never feel well rested in the morning. He has talked about running a heavy metals screening as well as a comprehensive stool analysis as well.

He has me on the following protocol daily:

\- B-Active
\- Acetyl L-Carnatine
\- DHEA (started with 25mg, now at 75mg)
\- Phenylalanine (started with 500mg, now at 1500mg)
\- A-Lipoic Acid (200-300mg)
\- American Ginseng, Elecampane
\- Probiotic, Digestive Enzymes
\- Magnesium Glycinate
\- Multi

I take/have taken on my own:

\- Krill Oil
\- L-Glutamine
\- Cod Liver Oil
\- Milk Thistle
\- Adaptogenic Herbs
\- Glutathione
\- Probiotics (High Quality)
\- Zinc
\- Manganese

Currently - I have had much more consistency with bowel movements for the last month or so, but still feel that my gut is sluggish. Large meals with protein always seem to bog me down, and eating in general rarely makes me feel better. I have made many a bone broth with mixed results, sometimes it feels soothing but often it makes me feel 'off'. I have tried incorporating much more seafood and feel that it has helped some with overall energy, especially after almost never eating any for many years. Oysters, mussels and a few types of fish have been a welcome addition (with the exception of salmon, which my body seems to reject, having tried multiple times).

I was relieved to hear Jack say that food and supplements aren't enough. I have eaten so well and tried so many products for over 3 years, and still I feel pretty awful most of the time. I'm still dialing in the diet to see what seems to help or hurt, and I feel like fats and proteins are a struggle for me. I was very low carb for awhile but that seemed to strain my body even more, so I have been eating a little (sweet potato mostly) with each meal.

Overall, the consistent feeling I have is that I have been poisoned in some way. No nausea, but my body feels tense and weak. My knees feel stiff and start to ache when I stand for an extended period of time. At times my energy is so low that just moving around feels taxing. I do yoga, bike as much as I can, but most strenuous exercise seems to make me feel worse. I'm trying to get more sun and have been grounding as much as possible, with some noticeable results. I wonder more and more about emf/dirty electricity. I live in a nice home, but I'm on the second floor and have many electronics in my room. I work on a computer most of the day and am starting to think it's stressing my system. I often feel a little better when get out of the house.

I am not all that famaliar with CT and am just now looking more into emf etc. I would very much appreciate anyone who can look at my labs and tell me if anything stands out, beyond what my doctors have noted. The Genova and Solstas tests were the most recent. Everyone has told me that thyroid and other common markers seem normal. The DHEA is definitely low, but so far the supplements recommended have only helped slightly.

I really appreciate all of your time, assuming you made it this far, and hope that some other folks may be able to see this and contribute to the cause. I'm happy to report on my progress and very much look froward to any and all feedback. Just let me know what I can do to help

Many thanks!

### Athanay — 2014-10-13T17:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-newbie-in-need.11881/post-143728>

Hi Caroline,

Thanks for the warm welcome!

Why do you think non-native EMF is a major issue for me?

I do drink R/O water and have for the past year or two, I sometimes drink mineral water as well.
I don't have the book and am trying to get caught up on the podcasts.
I will create a journal, and tried to post a pic but couldn't figure out how. When I click on the blank picture it says there is an error.

I work from home most of the time, so I spend a majority of my day in my room. It's on the second floor of a house built in the last 5-7 years. I've been living here for over a year but am moving out at the end of October. There are several outlets in the room (7 total), two of which are right next to my bed - I try to not plug anything into those. My workstation has a lot going on (desktop computer with large monitor, large speakers, and various audio equipment) I have a surge protector with 6 outlets, all of which are used, and the two wall outlets are on either side of me, both in full-time use. I spend a lot of time in front of the computer, as both my work and hobbies involve audio editing/recording, which require a computer. The wireless router and modem used to be in the room below me, but are now across the hall. The roommate below me has a very similar setup to me.

The house is nice, but that obviously doesn't mean the electricity was set up well.

I don't have a smart phone, btw, and I now turn my cell to airplane mode at night. I try and get to bed between 10-11, but have had a lot of trouble falling asleep lately. I'm going to really make an effort to get to sleep earlier, and wake up earlier as well.

One day a week I work at my bosses house. He lives in the country, but the room we work in has the wireless modem/router, a cordless phone and a couple computers. I almost always feel worse after working at his place for a few hours.

The house I lived in when I started feeling bad (3+ years ago) was much older and probably had the dirtiest of electricity. The router was in my room for 2-3 years and the there was some sort of electric component for the house right outside my room. It turned out to have some issues that required it to be completely replaced according to the city standards. Not sure if this played a role in my initial collapse, but it seems likely after reading up on emf.

I live in Asheville, NC - which is a beautiful and clean mountain town. I'm not sure about cell phone towers in my area, but am trying to find out. There are power lines running all through the neighborhood, and they converge at a spot that is level with, and not too far from (40 yards +/-) my bedroom.

I should note that I didn't spend quite so much time in front of the computer until I started my current job, roughly a year ago.

My family history has me on a pretty broad spectrum. My Mom's side is more type-A, and has had heart disease, diabetes and cancer. No cancer in my immediate family but my brother was recently diagnosed with diabetes. I'm much more like my Dad physiologically, and we have similar issues, with consistent depression being a common thread. He is not an alcoholic but both his parents were. His Mother and at least one of her sister's died of Alzheimer's. My father was diagnosed about a year ago. This is a scary thought for me, and I'm hoping to address the underlying issues sooner than later. I grew up on a pretty standard american diet, lots of fast food and carbs. I feel that my high carb intake was a big part of my initial crash, especially in relation to the digestive issues.

Please let me know if there is any other info that would be helpful.

### BTA — 2014-10-13T06:03:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-treatment-to-heal-your-gut.11882/post-143693>

New Treatment To Heal Your Gut -

Swallow poop - um? If you can get past the ick factor - this is the new treatment
Better than a fecal implants & faster than probiotics - bottoms up...........

### Alex Fergus — 2015-09-03T10:21:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bodybuilding.11883/post-176301>

Hey.

I know this is an old post, but I wanted to say hi.

I'm an ex Bodybuilder, I have also done a lot of powerlifting.

I still strength train, but my focus in health now (saying that I'm probably in better shape than my BBer days!).

I dieted down for my shows using a cyclic keto diet. It wasn't anything like it is today, but I was eating bacon and eggs for breakfast during my 12 week diet. I got down to ~5% and I won a national title.

I would be curious doing another comp using the tips and tricks (such as CT) i have learnt over the years. But I think BBing really messed me up. Years of overtraining, poor recovery, too many stimulants (I was all natural, but was taking far too much caffiene for example) and too much protein.
Anyway, I still train and coach Bodybuilders, so if you have any questions hit me up.

Cheers

### ssj3 — 2015-09-03T11:25:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bodybuilding.11883/post-176302>

Hi Alex,

Sometimes I think that our environment changed so much over the years that the constant beating we put on our bodies and the constant shovelling in of 6 meals a day along with out-of-season food intake that only a few would get through without health problems or injuries. I never made it to the stage, but I always think of how to make it happen combining what I learn on here, but I would have to completely overhaul my current life to balance that health tradeoff I now know comes with it.

My current plan which I am finding to work well is to run with a strength based program during the winter months (JoeD program) and then mountaindog during the months when I get amble sunshine to support increased carb intake and some intra-workout.

I am in Adelaide... do you run your own facility over in Sydney??

Rob

I was conscious this summer to add lots of calories, like the deer I plan to hunt here in a few weeks. I even did GOMAD (Gallon of Milk a Day) for 25 days and gained 15 lbs during that time. I took advantage of the long holiday weekend to go strict keto so I'm on day 3 now. Next August I'm moving to Hawaii so I'll have lots of carbs available and will of course eat more of them in Hawaii. Now I'm focused on mostly leafy greens for my carbs. I'm thinking I might want to match that 25 day GOMAD with 25 days keto and then see what my body composition is like.

Hi Jack,
Sounds good, Im still very new in regards to JK protocols so I don't really have much advice to give (I'm still learning!). But I can definitely help on the strength side of things. Heres my advice - don't overcomplicate things, use a basic strength program, focus on strength, size will come (with the help of the extra calories). And don't over do it. Personally I think 3 strength sessions a week, with a 4th HIIT conditioning day is all you need to put on size. Focus on recovery, sleep, and quality food while minimising stressors and you'll do great.

Be sure to keep us updated with your progress

### Alex Fergus — 2015-09-08T04:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bodybuilding.11883/post-176709>

Not a problem

I'm based in Sydney Australia.

Sounds like you have things all sorted then! Regarding your program, I would recommend doing more compound multi joint lifts (ie, squats, rows, chins, presses, deadlifts) and less isolation single joint lifts (biceps, calves, shoulder isolation exercises etc). And also look at doing these 2-3x a week (ie, theres no reason why you can't do pullups - which are great btw, I have them included in all of my clients strength/size programs - 3x a week, just make sure you have a day or two rest in between and you're good to go).

I say this to a lot of new lifters - don't train like a bodybuilder until you've been a powerlifter for a year or two. In essence what I'm saying is even if your goals are size, you are better off building a strength base before doing any advanced split routines. In fact, even guys that have a solid strength base still do better on a strength program if their goals are size. Maybe doing a few more reps/sets than a true powerlifting program, but I hope you get my jist. In summary - don't get caught up with fancy programs, just do the basics.

Bodybuilding is a great sport in the sense that you are so focused on achieving a great body - symmetrical, balanced, proportionate etc. And a lot of people associate health and bodybuilding, but this is not necessarily the case. Competitive BB'ing (whether natural or not) is horrible for your health. I'm still paying the price of pushing to hard for my years BBing. There's also the selfishness of the sport, it's all about you. It can be great for someone who is a horrible eater, and has no respect for their body, I've seen fat lazy kids change big time when they stumbled upon bodybuilding, but unfortunately it has a lot of costs. Excuse my profanity, but its a constant mindf&\*k. You are always doubting yourself, comparing yourself to others, never happy with what you have. But this is at a competitive level. Recreational bodybuilding can be great!

Anyway, that was a bit of a rant! Let me know if you have any more questions, especially in regards to training.

### Danco3636 — 2015-09-09T03:35:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-bodybuilding.11883/post-176826>

A few things of note.... Last week I did a medical exam/ surgery
Resting heart rate 42-45 - kept setting off the alarm on the machine.
Blood pressure was normal to low....
They took my temperature and thought the machine was broken, they got another and just figured mine was on the low side. I had CT'ed a few hours before going there but felt warm.
They started an IV and asked if I had been dehydrated. Nope I was around a 20 hour fast but had some water in the AM (I wasn't suppose to). My blood looked dark red and they said normally it is a bit clearer on people. I just said "I had rich blood".
After a 3-4 hour surgery and when the anesthesia wore off I had two things to say....... "Are we done?" They said "Yes". I then sad "Time for some salmon! Yep went home had some salmon and then a cool soak....
I opt out of any pain meds and antibiotics.... Just wasn't needed.

Just sharing and experience. - Can't wait to get back to lifting.....

# Thread: Athanay's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Athanay — 2014-10-13T21:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-143731>

Hello kind folks. I have been following Jack's work more recently and am brand new to the forum, this being my first post. I'm fully aware that posting my own problems and asking for suggestions may not be the best introduction to a community such as this. My hope in doing so, aside from getting advice and encouragement from a great forum, is to offer another thread of experience for others to possibly learn from. I have every intention of keeping progress reports along the way, as I continue along the healing path. This place is truly amazing and I hope to be able to contribute more and more as I continue to absorb all of this information. I apologize in advance for the length of this post, and will post the test results and current doctor recommended supplementation at the bottom, should you want to skip the trial and error portion of this essay.
My symptoms started with general digestion issues a few years ago. I had been a vegetarian for several years, and a lazy/uneducated one at times. I ate a lot of carbs in the form of all the bad things we know about these days. I also had stiff muscles and general aches and pains in day to day movement. A pretty intense chronic fatigue began to set in and lingers still, along with the aches and pains. My sleep was erratic at the time and I supplemented with a fair amount of coffee. I just kept crashing over and over again.

An early and limited blood panel (Page 3-5 on attached file '12' - LabCorp/Lauren Johnston), from an allopathic doctor, revealed only a Vit D deficiency, according to her. I have supplemented since, and levels were improved on more recent testing.

I eventually went to a naturopath (over 2 years ago at this point) who focused on chiropractic, nutrition and general detox. She used kinesiology (which never had me all that confident) to tell me I had all sorts of food allergies and leaky gut. She also ran a saliva hormone test (Page 3 on attached file '9' - Diagnos-Techs, Inc) and said I had high estrogen and adrenal exhaustion. This was all new to me so I just had to trust her for the most part. She gave me several supplements (fish oil, adrenal support, zypan and others) and by cutting out many trigger foods my digestion did improve, at least the major discomfort issues. After several months of expensive adjustments and continued fatigue I stopped going. She kept saying adrenal fatigue can take up to 18 months to recover from, but I just loss faith in her for one reason or another. All along I was not eating animal products (ethical reasons + 'supposed' dairy allergy) and was eating heavy grains and legumes, like, all the time.

I moved on to a chinese medicine and acupuncture clinic, but after a few months there was a petering out as well. When I consistently inquired about diet he always just said to eat 'good, nourishing' foods. Just wasn't getting results, aside from the relaxation that often comes from acupuncture.

I eventually started getting more into BulletProof and paleo related dieting and began to consume animal products about a year and half ago. I was hoping for some wow factor, where my shirt would burst open as I sprinted down the street with a grass-fed torch in my hand, no such luck. The transition didn't cause any bad reactions but they didn't seem to help either. After many trials with elimination diets (eggs, dairy, grains etc.) I still can't tell whether I actually have any real allergies/sensitivities. I never get obvious reactions that would indicate a specific allergy, but I have found that certain foods (eggs, salmon, some dairy) consistently make me feel crappy - I get tired and my body feels almost poisoned and tense. I never had food intolerances prior to all this. I eat completely BP/paleo, have been off all wheat/gluten/processed food/corn/soy etc for two years, but still I feel pretty crummy most of the time. Low energy, stiff, scaly skin, dry hair, dark circles under eyes, all the classics. The circles under my eyes in the morning are profound, and my once thick and flowing hair has been reduced to a dry, thin, and frizzy mess.

I started seeing a new naturopath in August of 2013. He did some blood work (Page 1-2 on attached file '12' - LabCorp) and noted that my iron levels were high. He thought this could be causing several of the problems and may be indicative of hemochromatosis. I tried to donate blood (only real way to rid the male body of iron) but had to wait a bit, based on some traveling I did in malaria zones earlier that year. He himself has hemochromatosis and said several of his symptoms were relieved when he began consistently donating blood. He gave me a couple types of digestive enzymes and had put me on pregnenolone to stimulate some basic hormone production, with little results after a few months of use. I have since donated blood a few times, but it hasn't resulted in any major shift in symptoms. I have felt a little better since lowering the iron (more recent tests have shown normal ranges) but again, no major recovery. Not sure what made it so high, but may have been reintroducing large amounts of meat after not eating any for 4-5 years.

I suspect my body might not be digesting fats particularly well, or that my gut is just depleted in general. I went from eating little fat for several years to eating a ton of it over the last year or so. I rarely, if ever, have any digestive discomfort (gas, bloating etc) but until recently I wasn't at all regular with bowel movements, and have had many periods where I only go every 2-3 days.

I used to get better sleep, but still never felt all that rested, but recently my sleep has gotten pretty bad. I can't fall asleep despite being tired and often wake early and can't fall back asleep.

There have been periods where I've had consistent muscle twitching, mostly in legs but other parts of the body as well. It mostly occurs when sitting and especially when laying. Circulation has been poor all along, with legs and arms falling asleep much easier than they used to. Back discomfort is an issue as well.

I have been seeing a new functional medicine doctor in Asheville for the last couple months and he ran a few tests after my evaluation (Page 1-2 on attached file '9' and Page 1-8 on attached file '1') Several B vitamins were quite low along with my DHEA. I was surprised that my cortisol pattern was in normal range, as I seem to go up and down so much with energy throughout the day, and never feel well rested in the morning. He has talked about running a heavy metals screening as well as a comprehensive stool analysis as well.

He has me on the following protocol daily:

\- B-Active
\- Acetyl L-Carnatine
\- DHEA (started with 25mg, now at 75mg)
\- Phenylalanine (started with 500mg, now at 1500mg)
\- A-Lipoic Acid (200-300mg)
\- American Ginseng, Elecampane
\- Probiotic, Digestive Enzymes
\- Magnesium Glycinate
\- Multi

I take/have taken on my own:

\- Krill Oil
\- L-Glutamine
\- Cod Liver Oil
\- Milk Thistle
\- Adaptogenic Herbs
\- Glutathione
\- Probiotics (High Quality)
\- Zinc
\- Manganese

Currently - I have had much more consistency with bowel movements for the last month or so, but still feel that my gut is sluggish. Large meals with protein always seem to bog me down, and eating in general rarely makes me feel better. I have made many a bone broth with mixed results, sometimes it feels soothing but often it makes me feel 'off'. I have tried incorporating much more seafood and feel that it has helped some with overall energy, especially after almost never eating any for many years. Oysters, mussels and a few types of fish have been a welcome addition (with the exception of salmon, which my body seems to reject, having tried multiple times).

I was relieved to hear Jack say that food and supplements aren't enough. I have eaten so well and tried so many products for over 3 years, and still I feel pretty awful most of the time. I'm still dialing in the diet to see what seems to help or hurt, and I feel like fats and proteins are a struggle for me. I was very low carb for awhile but that seemed to strain my body even more, so I have been eating a little (sweet potato mostly) with each meal.

Overall, the consistent feeling I have is that I have been poisoned in some way. No nausea, but my body feels tense and weak. My knees feel stiff and start to ache when I stand for an extended period of time. At times my energy is so low that just moving around feels taxing. I do yoga, bike as much as I can, but most strenuous exercise seems to make me feel worse. I'm trying to get more sun and have been grounding as much as possible, with some noticeable results. I wonder more and more about emf/dirty electricity. I live in a nice home, but I'm on the second floor and have many electronics in my room. I work on a computer most of the day and am starting to think it's stressing my system. I often feel a little better when get out of the house.

I am not all that famaliar with CT and am just now looking more into emf etc. I would very much appreciate anyone who can look at my labs and tell me if anything stands out, beyond what my doctors have noted. The Genova and Solstas tests were the most recent. Everyone has told me that thyroid and other common markers seem normal. The DHEA is definitely low, but so far the supplements recommended have only helped slightly.

I really appreciate all of your time, assuming you made it this far, and hope that some other folks may be able to see this and contribute to the cause. I'm happy to report on my progress and very much look froward to any and all feedback. Just let me know what I can do to help

Many thanks!

### Athanay — 2014-10-13T21:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-143732>

My family history has me on a pretty broad spectrum. My Mom's side is more type-A, and has had heart disease, diabetes and cancer. No cancer in my immediate family but my brother was recently diagnosed with diabetes. I'm much more like my Dad physiologically, and we have similar issues, with consistent depression being a common thread. He is not an alcoholic but both his parents were. His Mother and at least one of her sister's died of Alzheimer's. My father was diagnosed about a year ago. This is a scary thought for me, and I'm hoping to address the underlying issues sooner than later. I grew up on a pretty standard american diet, lots of fast food and carbs. I feel that my high carb intake was a big part of my initial crash, especially in relation to the digestive issues.

### Athanay — 2014-10-27T16:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-145302>

Just a quick update.

I have been grounding as much as possible in the morning and throughout the day. It always feels nice to touch the ground, and I feel it may be helping counter the all day computer cycle. I'm also eating quality fish daily, including oysters. I have felt a little 'lighter' in general, which is always a relief. Getting much more sun as well, which has been something I've lacked for a long time - again, always feels good to feel the sun on your skin.

My sleep is still not great, but has improved a little over the past couple weeks. I'm moving into a one story house this week, and will be curious to see if it makes a difference. I realized recently that every house I've lived in since moving to my current location has either had a basement, or I was on the second floor (sometimes with a basement, so technically third floor) Really hoping my new place will have me better grounded.

Any thoughts on sleeping outside for a few days? It's getting cold here, so may be a good way to get mild CT as well?

I'm looking to move more into CT, and also wondering about the leptin reset. Is this something everyone should do?

Where will I find the specific guidelines for CT and leptin rx etc?

Hope everyone is as good as they can be

### Athanay — 2015-02-09T19:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155143>

Long overdue update. Hope everyone is well!

I did the 23andme testing, and would highly recommend that others do the same. I discovered a few things that were definitely hindering the healing process. Apparently I burn through B12 quickly and so need a steady supply to keep the energy flowing. I was a vegetarian for many years, and was not supplementing B12 at all, so I imagine that did a number on my system. Also, I don't metabolize sulphur all that efficiently, so all those larger meals with heavy veggies were actually taxing my system a bit - which makes sense considering I almost always felt subdued after eating full meals. I have since spread out the servings, and along with Methyl B12 and Methyl Folate, I have seen an increase in overall energy. I was also encouraged to take SAMe for some serotonin issues related to MTHFR. All in all, it seems to be helping. The doctor I'm seeing is Dan Stickler, who has had Jack on his podcast (formerly 'Living the Optimized Life', now called 'Biohacking for Optimal Health' - lots of good information) so I feel good about his general approach to health. I had also been taking magnesium glycinate, but it turns out glycinate is not good for my system and have switched to malate. It's amazing how long it can take to fully comprehend N=1. I would try all these recs from different practitioners and despite feeling sometimes worse, would continue to try them, based on all the 'evidence' of their efficacy. I'm slowly but surely learning to really listen to my body, day to day, and to just be more patient with all this experimentation.

I'm currently working on stress reduction, using neurofeedback, and an Emwave 2 that I purchased. Lots of seafood down the hatch, but still feel that I have some gut issues, as I still rarely feel 'better' after eating. I'm doing low fodmap for the first time, along with some more elimination in the diet with suspect foods including eggs, all dairy, and unfortunately coffee. I really love coffee, but have found that almost every time I consume it, I eventually (within an hour or so) feel pretty gross. I get stiff and achey, and my energy tends to drop. I'm not sure what I'm reacting to there, or if it's just not in the cards for me for now.

My gut concerns have almost always been around constipation. I don't feel constipated, I'm just not going to the bathroom consistently - every couple days, sometimes longer, despite eating 2 square meals a day. I can tell there is just a lack of flow in my system, in general. I've been making bone broths and am about to start some more probiotics. I am also dabbling a bit with resistant starch, no real adverse effects so far. Maybe a little gas, but my dreams have been profoundly epic - the story lines seem to go on for ages, like one long dream, rather than several broken up pieces. I have heard of this occurrence, but am curious as to the mechanism, and whether it is something to view as positive/negative/who knows?

I feel that general dehydration may be a major issue for me, some of which is probably coming from consistent emf exposure (many hours a day on the computer, room close to refrigerator, roommate mostly unwilling to turn of wifi at night - the list goes on). My skin is profoundly dry, including scalp/hair. I drink RO water throughout the day, but am reading more into structured water, after hearing about dehydration occurring despite the amount of water you consume. I know it's a big part of the equation with Jack's work, but am struggling to make it work. One thing worth noting is that I rarely feel thirsty, and think I mostly drink water out of habit/protocol.

I have a had a lot of muscle twitching over the past year or so, but have seen a decline over the past couple months. My feeling is that more grounding and less technology has helped. I have a tri-meter and a stetzerizer meter, which has helped me steer clear of problem areas. My outlets show really high with the Stetzerizer, but I felt that using the filters was actually making it worse. The number dropped, but when using several filters in my bedroom, my body seemed to be pulsing a bit more, especially when trying to sleep. I have read that faulty wiring issues can be exacerbated by the filters, so I've been going without for the time being.

The past week or two has shown some improvement in sleep. For me it's falling asleep that is the issue, but lately I have increased exercise and have a nighttime routine that seems to be effective. I do think my room is a major source of emf. When I lay down at night, my eyes often water a bit, and they have clearly continued to do so throughout the night, as I often wake with dried tears around my eyes. I still never feel well rested, but am hoping consistent exercise will continue to assist in that arena.

I began CT about 3 weeks ago. I ordered replacement ice sheets for the ice vest and place them in between two compression shirts at night for about 45 minutes. It's not a challenge in the slightest, and I'm wondering when I should switch to the baths. I feel good about CT and have noticed that I'm the only person in my town cruis_ing_ through the icy cold mountains with my windows down, and I'm comfortable. Only other difference is that I can tell my car is a taxing source of emf. My leg muscles twitch after being in the car for a short period of time.

The best news I have involves my plans in the coming months. I am moving to Colorado for the spring/summer and will be staying in a small mountain town. I will be alone, and will have complete control of the variables in the immediate environment. I can shut off wifi at will, kill the breaker at night, sleep under the stars, climb mountains, live by candle light etc. etc. I think it will be the best thing I can do for my myself, and am really curious to see how this environment will affect my ability to heal. I've already worked it out with my local seafood/meat distributor to ship me whatever I may need. I'll still be working on a computer, but the overall environment should be much cleaner than what I'm dealing with now. Antenna search.com shows my current location having 26 Towers and 254 antennas within a 4 mile radius. The new location shows 3 towers and 4 antennas! I'm not expecting a miracle, but I always feel better when I spend even a short amount of time there.

In the mean time, I have the option to move into a new place for the next month or so, until I leave. I'd be on the second floor. but am considering the move, as I'm fairly convinced my current house is a stressor, emf, possible mold etc.

I welcome any comments or suggestions, especially in relation to increased CT, constipation, and housing. Thanks for all the support, hope you are all _improving in your own ways!_

Questions:

1\. Dehydration - how to experiment, drink more/less/different types of water?
2\. Anyone else had luck with resistant starch for improving digestion?
3\. Been using ice sheets for about 3 weeks, am I safe to start CT baths?
4\. Second-story living - or should I stay put for the next month or so?

### Martin — 2015-02-13T15:20:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155528>

9-14 milligauss is horrendous. But it will drop some as you back away.

Try holding the meter at the same distance as your body parts, like hands when you're on the keyboard, or your face when you are in a normal viewing position. That will give you the gauss level that you are chronically exposed to, as opposed to having your face right up on the screen. What are your readings when your body is in a normal position?

ex-screen licker,
NSH

### Martin — 2015-02-14T18:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-155612>

Let us know what setting your meter is on. Also, where are your power lines and circuit breaker panel? Is your place a house or apartment? Age of structure? The older they are, more of a chance someone's goofed the wiring up. Test your breaker panel from about a foot away, and more, to see how the readings fall off. Another experiment is to turn off the main breaker and THEN go through your place testing the fields. That could show you if it's coming from power lines or some other external source other than the house .

Tough call on the 4G and other stuff. Hard to tell without measuring with _accurate_ RF meter and gauss meter.

ex-panel box licker,
NSH

I am moving out of town at the end of March, but currently have the option to move in with a friend for the last month or so that I'm here. ### Martin — 2015-02-19T20:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/athanays-journal.11887/post-156076>

What does your bed read? You can position it to get some distance from the stronger fields if turning off the breakers isn't enough. In your potential new digs, check the floor of the 2nd story for fields. Often there are things like fluorescent lights on the ceiling of the 1st floor that can greatly affect the upper floor. And basements can house hot water heaters and lights that can have strong fields. Mag fields can go right through walls that are not steel, and even then can penetrate. Distance and metals, like nickel, are the two best options for reducing. Nickel sheet metal is off-the-damn-chart expensive! Thick sheets of steel (3/8"-1/2") can be somewhat effective, but hard to work with.

NSH

# Thread: Help with Deciphering Thyroid Study
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-deciphering-thyroid-study.11888/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### River — 2014-10-14T00:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-deciphering-thyroid-study.11888/post-143742>

I read this article with interest a few times and was hoping that some of you with more understanding of the terminology, content and context of the article could help extract the nuggets and boom moments (if any).

I know the studies were done on animal thyroid but from what Jack has told us regarding the evolution process much of this could apply to us as well.

As someone with thyroid issues for many years who monitors lab values of TSH, T3, T4, et.al., one of my take-aways (which may be wrong) was that the article seems to imply that TSH may be increased or decreased depending upon whether we are in a circadian long photoperiod or short photoperiod. The same with T3, and conversion from T4 to RT4 and from T3 to T2. I think they are saying their studies show it may change with the seasons.

For those taking NDT hormones to decrease TSH, this may put us in a permanent state of Fall/Autumn, even if we are honoring the natural light protocol per JK (minimal artificial light after sunset, and no blue light at all after dusk). Same with T3 and T4 levels.

I know that modern people today are living the majority of the day under unnatural light, but I'm thinking more about those of us here who are honoring length of day. I wonder if we should re-think thyroid labs. And I wonder if the labs should re-think the thyroid lab ranges, since they wouldn't apply to humans who live in the ancient pathway.

Sorry for my limited knowledge, but my inquisitiveness is genuine along with my desire to share.

Here is the link to the study:

[http://journal.frontiersin.org/Journal/10.3389/fendo.2014.00019/full](http://journal.frontiersin.org/Journal/10.3389/fendo.2014.00019/full)

### Grmoline — 2014-10-15T13:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-deciphering-thyroid-study.11888/post-143910>

This is a really interesting study, and will take me another read to completely digest it all. But it does demonstrate seasonal photoperiod-related changes in T3 and revT3 production in a number of vertebrate species, linked to melatonin and separate from the usual hypothalamic signalling. Two things that I found interesting. One is the statement about how genetic/molecular circadian clocks (peripheral clocks) are found in virtually every tissue, and that they rapidly become desynchronised and phase-shifted without regular resetting cues from the SCN (something JK has been telling us for years). And the other is how refractory states can be artificially induced (i.e., artificially inducing a particular season by manipulating light) and the effect it has on T3 production or repression. You've raised some really interesting questions for me also, as one who is treating Hashimoto's.

### Josh — 2014-10-15T21:09:20+0300

As I recall Jack's summary is that if we are LS and Cold-adapted, the brain bypasses the thyroid. # Thread: Ketochi's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketochis-journal.11889/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### ketochi — 2014-10-14T04:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketochis-journal.11889/post-143765>

Hi All,

Where to begin? 35 year old male that has been dealing with tremor and neuropathy along with mood/sleep issues for roughly 15 years. Conventional neurology is now telling me I might have MS although I currently show no lesions. The unofficial MS diagonosis led me to Dr Terry Wahls and her Wahls protocol which I started the ketogenic verison of 2 weeks ago. I've also been to see a functional Dr who ran several tests of which the following appears noteworthy:

-   Heterozygous for MTHFR C677T and A1298C - since started supplementing with methylfolate and methyl b12
-   high cortisol levels at night - I was doing shift work at the time of this test and my sleep cycle was a bit messed up. I'm back on a normal schedule so I plan to retest this soon to see if anything has changed
-   High levels mercury/copper - I took the heavy metals test fully expecting the results to come back normal. No clue how I would have aquired high levels of these metals although I've since decided on an oral chelation protocol and with the new diet/methylation support hopefully these levels come down.
-   Food allergies - seems to be the usual culprits - eggs, soy, dairy, etc...eliminated these from my diet
-   High testosterone levels - like really high. 1450 on a 200-1200 scale. No clue what to do about this or even if should do anything. The endo that ran the tests really has no explanation.

I've been in research mode focusing on the ketogenic diet mainly which in turn led me to Dr Kruse. I recently watched an interview of his with Primal Edge Health and have since read the book and been all over the website. I can say for certain at this point that Dr Kruse is a total nut and I mean that as the highest compliment . I like people that think outside the box and realize I need to start thinking this way if I am ever to get my health back. To be honest, Dr K's food pyramid scares me quite a bit. Since I supposedly have high levels of mercury I've been avoiding seafood (not hard as I've never been a seafood eater). I've decided to add some sockeye salmon to my diet and see how it goes. Since I've started my detox I been doing 4 sauna sesssions a week. How well it helps with the detox is up for debate but it's relaxing for me so I'll keep at it. What I've always had issues with is the cold and so I'm excited about CT. Hoping that becoming cold adapted will help with the adrenals and sleep. Time will tell.

I look forward to reading and contributing as I go.

Poster child eh? That doesn't sound good...

[http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-10-multiple-sclerosis/](http://jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-10-multiple-sclerosis/)

_The missing link to her protocol is DHA and restoring normal circadian signaling of real sunlight. The secondary issue is limiting transition metal exposure and fake light. Without massive infusion of DHA and proper circadian balance, Multiple Sclerosis will be improved somewhat, but it can not fully reverse the symptoms associated with it._

So it appears I'm doing some things right by following the Wahls protocol and detoxing the metals. I guess the missing links for me are lack of DHA from food sources (I supplement fish oil although sounds like you are against this), limiting exposure to fake light (I do where blue light blocker glasses at night starting around 8pm), and proper circadian rythms which appears to come from sunlight in the am and darkness in the pm.

2 questions:
1) Short of turning off the lights how do you limit your exposure to fake light? Are there certain bulbs I can use in my house?
2) Would a magnetico pad help reduce the effects on non native EMF?

[http://jackkruse.com/why-leaky-guts-lead-to-ms/](http://jackkruse.com/why-leaky-guts-lead-to-ms/)

I'm aware of the issues with leaky gut. I've never been tested for it in part because I don't trust the test and in larger part because I'm already taking steps to heal it if it is an issue (coconut oil/bone broth..will add seafood broth).

[http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/](http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/)

I've eliminated all inflammatory foods from diet and added probiotics and fermented foods. I'd love to be able to consume some raw dairy and even though my source is from Jersey Cows I have no idea whether it's A1 or A2.

Lots of good information to digest and I'm sure it will take time to process it all. I enjoy the scientific nature of your writing even though most of it is over my head.

The other interesting thing she talked about was Visible Light Transmission - using light instead of wireless... but then she wondered if you had to take into account the blue/green light that was hitting people's eyes... hmm... would that have an effect? She didn't know...

Why can't they just used wires? Or fiber optics?

Lots of info on the above webinar folks - well worth the hour spent - take notes on the names of scientists she gives - like Barry Trower who worked at MI5 - and she talks about the EMF spectrum and what it can do to a person psychologically - you can't believe how your government is wasting your $$$...

They know. They've always known. They just don't want \*us\* to know...

# Thread: Stridar's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stridar — 2014-10-15T02:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/post-143857>

Hi all!

My name is Stridar and I am a 25 year old female living down in Sydney. I've been following Jack's work for a while and it's only just begun to sink in, and I'm really excited to be a part of this community! While I’m not obese, I do have a pot belly and my goal is to slim it right down. I’m 163 cm and 59 kilos (130 lb) and I want to drop approximately 5 kilos (11 lb). I also have PCOS, which I also want to treat and get my labs optimal in general.

I fall asleep within five minutes of going to bed, sleep peacefully without waking up and I don’t feel fatigued waking up or during the day. However I suspect my life-long pattern of getting 5 hours of sleep contributed to my muffin top; that and my prior addiction to instant noodles, rice and overall poor diet.

I have a computer desk job which exposes me to EMF during the daytime, which means I’ll need to be stricter with my environment control. Luckily, I live in the outer suburbs of Sydney and not directly within the city where I work.

I’ll be following the leptin prescription, epi-paleo diet and getting my circadian cycle normalised to the best of my ability to lose my gut and counter my PCOS. Here are the main changes I’ve been implementing:

\- I’ve been using my Lumie clock for about three months. I plan on weaning off using my Lumie eventually, because I don’t like the potential risk of EMF. But we’ll see.

\- I’ve skipped breakfast for the majority of my life. Now, I’ve started off the day drinking grass-fed protein shake to start the day. I am working on getting 6 oysters and eggs for breakfast, but meanwhile I’ll have at least a protein shake for breakfast.

\- I’m packing vegetable soup with canned sardines for work every day. Sometimes I order sashimi instead of packing soup and sardines.

\- After sunset, I wear my blue blocker glasses and have my Himalayan salt lamp as the only source of light, to keep my room as dark as possible.

\- Been doing CT with my Cool Fat Burner for an hour almost every night. I try to take bitter melon extract before the CT session but sometimes I forget. Been drinking glasses of cold water to amp the CT. All my showers are in cold water too.

\- I’ve ceased using my computer from 9:00pm onwards. Instead of wasting time on Facebook, I’m now drawing or reading until bedtime which is 10:30-11:00pm, with a 6:30am start.

\- I’ve been eating two dozen oysters over the weekend. Sometimes four dozen when I can afford it (is this an excessive amount to consume over 2-3 days?)

\- I’ve switched from regular fluoride water to RO bottled water, however the RO water is sourced from Australian waters which is not ideal. I am currently searching for an affordable brand/supplier of bottled spring water from the northern hemisphere, any Aussies here know of any brands?

\- I’ve been trying to get as much sun as possible. I sit outside in the sun for lunches at work with my barefeet on the grass and for a bit longer on the weekends. However I am concerned about the donut hole UV, so I’m not sure how much sun I really should be getting or limiting. I’ve begun taking 5000 UI vitamin D from Life Extension everyday for extra precaution. Easier than gulping five 1000 UI tablets, that’s for sure.

\- I’m in the progress of reducing EMF in my household too, one step at a time.

\- I know you’re not supposed to exercise while on the leptin reset, but I just began doing HIIT on my treadmill for 20 minutes, once a week in the late afternoon. Surely this cannot hurt?

\- I’m using a wet sauna for 15 minutes twice a week. Just started last week and I love it.

\- I don’t feel stressed at the moment. Sometimes I feel stressed to meet a work deadline but I don’t believe it’s anything too strenuous. I typically use my EmWave daily. I also meditate on weekends, though I’ll be working to factor meditation every morning fairly soon.

Lab results:

DHEAS: 5.2 umol/L (I have no idea how to convert this number to compare it to Jack’s Optimizing Labs PDF)

Total Protein: 77g/L

Vitamin D: 104 nmol/L

Glucose: 4.3 mmol/L

Globuline: 32 g/L

Creatinine: 65 umol/L

Neutrophils: 1.73

Highly Sensitive CRP: <0.4 mg/L

Triglycerides: 0.7 mmol/L

HDL Chol: 1.8 mmol/L

LDL Chol: 5.2 mmol/L

Cholesterol: 7.3 mmol/L

My cholesterol is my biggest concern because it’s risen quite a bit, it was only 4.9 last year prior to going paleo/epi-paleo. My doctor believes the rise to be due to the grass-fed butter and coconut oil I’ve been taking over the year, but honestly I do not believe I consume an excessive amount. I put a little butter in my green tea in the morning, eat duck fat on the weekends and use coconut oil in my cooking but all in moderate amounts. What should I do to lower my cholesterol, and should I even be worried?

I need more labs, I know. My doctor does not believe I require detailed blood results because I’m not seriously ill, so I’m looking for a place in Sydney that offers comprehensive lab results for an affordable price; if anyone recommends a place let me know.

P.S If you read through that essay and made it through, I applaud you!

### caroline — 2014-10-15T02:45:24+0300

You can buy bottled imported water at Aldi ...from Italy. around $1.49 for 1.5L \[blue bottle\]

rethink the Vit D .....
your hsCRP is good

follow Yew's journal .... he used to live in Melbourne ...and has just moved to Cairns to see how that will impact his health. He is doing better

Hey thanks guys! I implemented these changes recently, so I'll have to wait for next round of labs to see exactly how much of an impact it's had. I have read Dr Jack Kruse's book but it took several tries to understand the content ('cause I am noob); I'm beginning to understand it now though.

### Stridar — 2014-10-15T05:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/post-143887>

I don't have a very good view from my house. I've been waking up at 6ish pretty regularly now, sometimes I just lie in bed peacefully before the alarm goes off. Instead of lying in bed, I may start forcing myself to go outside and sit on my front lawn grass barefooted to gaze at the sunrise/sky/whatever-I-can-get.

It's kinda weird, I don't konk out as quickly as I did before, which was as soon as my head hit my pillow. Now it takes 10-15 minutes to fall asleep. So I think I've been sleep deprived my entire life but didn't feel the symptoms and therefore didn't pay it any attention. I used to sleep through the night without waking but now I typically wake up at 4am in a half sleepy/dazed state for half a minute and return to sleep. Not sure what's going on there but I'm not too worried.

I am really enjoying every chance to ground now, except when I get attacked by thorns and weeds.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-20T03:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stridars-journal.11897/post-144359>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=143929)
>
> Welcome from me too, nice to have you here
>
> I have high cholesterol too but I am not one bit worried

When calcium levels are altered in the CSF and the blood, cholesterol in the basal skin cannot be sulfated from cysteine stores. Sulfur stores come from things I mentioned in the EPCOT Rx, seafood and pork. Cholesterol sulfate is manufactured by a combination of specific frequencies of

(1) ultraviolet radiation

(2) visible light radiation

(3) infrared radiation

(4) and a range of oxygen saturation

The sulfate actually comes from homocysteine in RBC’s within hemoglobin. This is why elevated homocysteine and low vitamin D levels are a non specific risk factors for heart disease and stroke. Homocysteine is the source of sulfate. You have to consume “some energy” to produce a sulfate from homocysteine, and the red blood cells actually supply the ATP to the plaque. ATP withdraws electrons from homocysteine to sulfate the cholesterol in blood. Many people forget that RBC have no nuclei or mitochondria so you might be wondering where the ATP comes from? Because of the lack of nuclei and mitochondria, mature red blood cells are incapable of generating energy via the Krebs cycle. Instead, erythrocytes depend on the anaerobic conversion of glucose by the Embden-Meyerhof pathway for the generation and storage of high-energy phosphates. Moreover, erythrocytes possess a unique glycolytic bypass for the production of 2,3-bisphosphoglycerate (2,3-DPG), the Rapoport-Luebering shunt. This shunt bypasses the phosphoglycerate kinase (PGK) step and accounts for the synthesis and regulation of 2,3-DPG levels that decrease hemoglobin's affinity for oxygen. This works in concert with the magnetic flux forces that allow paramagnetic diatomic oxygen to be drawn to mitochondria. In this way, 2,3-DPG constitutes a quantum energy buffer to catalyze this reaction. This frees sulfate to bind with cholesterol in blood plasma. When your LDL cholesterol is elevated it is a sign your skin is lacking sunlight in these frequencies. These frequencies occur 10-12 noon in most places on the globe.

When you sulfate cholesterol, you turn it into both a water-soluble and a fat-soluble molecule. This allows it to get into places using water and fat pathways in cells. It doesn't have to be packaged up inside a low-density lipoprotein (LDL) particle to be used by cells. When sunlight interacts with eNOS and cholesterol some quantum magic happens in skin. So what is the key to high LDL cholesterol? CALCIUM EFFLUX.
