### Josh — 2014-10-18T03:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-144147>

> **Abstract**
> The formation of amyloid fibrils proceeds via a nucleation-dependent mechanism in which nucleation phase is generally associated with a high free energy resulting in the rate-limiting step. On the basis of this kinetic feature, the nucleation is one of the most crucial phases controlling the pathogenesis of amyloidoses, but little is known about the details of how protein molecules and surrounding environment vary at this stage. Here, we applied near infrared (NIR) spectral monitoring of water structural changes in real time during the nucleation-dependent fibrillation of insulin. Whilst multivariate spectral analysis in the 2050–2350 nm spectral region indicated cross-β formation, characteristic transformations of water structure have been detected in the spectral region 1300–1600 nm corresponding to the first overtone of water OH stretching vibrations. Furthermore, specific water spectral patterns (aquagrams) related to different water molecular conformations have been found along the course of protein nucleation and aggregation. Right in the beginning, dissociation of hydrogen-bonded network in bulk water and coinstantaneous protein and ion hydration were observed, followed by water hydrogen-bonded networks development, presumably forcing the nucleation. These specific transformations of water spectral pattern could be used further as a biomarker for early non-invasive diagnosis of amyloidoses prior to explosive amplification and deposits of amyloid fibrils.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Schematic illustration representing multi-step transformation of water structures during the fibril formation.**

> **Figure S6**
> **The structural transformation of water molecules as estimated by PLS concentration regression analysis.** The analysis was performed with a dataset of NIR spectra in the region of 1300–1600 nm monitored at four different concentrations of insulin protein; 0.5, 1, 3, and 5 mg/ml. (A) Regression vector obtained as a model for the prediction of insulin concentration. The regression vector was based on the spectra at 6–10 min. The most prominent absorbance band was exhibited at around 1455 nm (assigned to S2), and additionally, positive peaks at 1365 nm (water solvation shell) and 1381 nm (OH−·(H2O)5 and/or O2−·(H2O)4), and negative peaks at 1421 nm (hydration), and 1437 nm (H+·(H2O)2) were observed, suggesting that hydrogen-bonded water structures are involved strongly with the formation of amyloid fibrils. (B) Time-dependent change in the SEP of protein concentration. In this analysis, the spectra at 6–10 min and 1–60 min were used for the model and test datasets, respectively. Although a markedly larger value at 1–5 min might indicate spectral change associated with temperature jump, the gradual increase in SEP values in the range of 11 min to 30 min coincides roughly with the elongation phase, verifying the change in amount of S2 water species. (C) Time-dependent change of the ratio of absorbance at 1460 nm (assigned to hydrogen-bonded water, S2) and that at 1410 nm (assigned to free water, S0). The result of protein solution is plotted by closed circles and that of solvent (25 mM HCl containing 100 mM NaCl) is also shown by open circles for reference. Immediately after starting the monitoring, because of the temperature increase and adjustment linked to increase of less hydrogen bonded water (S0), the overall ratio decreased. After 5 min, the value of absorbance ratio seemed to increased, which agreed very well with the observed 2 stages in the nucleation stage ([Figure 4](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/figure/pone-0101997-g004/)). Further on, in the next 2–3 minutes, it decreased very slightly and increased again showing details of the elongation phase (phase II). With this, we show that the changes in the initial stage of the fibril formation reaction are in accordance with the PCA and PLS regression results for protein concentration measurement.

(TIF)

> If it is assumed that the organized hydrogen-bonded water network is not restricted within the proximal region but extended to considerably distal ones, it is likely that the water network contributes to interplay among an ensemble of protein molecules regulating well-ordered assembling. In light of a novel concept which has been recently proposed on the basis of explicit-water molecular dynamics simulations[\[13\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/#pone.0101997-Chong1), water-mediated attraction between protein molecules contributes to directional adjustment towards a stable associated structure, which will lead to nucleation in the present case. After the nucleation, the hydrogen-bonded water conformations seemed to be attenuated along with abrupt amplification of fibrils during the elongation phase, and again developed after reaching the equilibrium phase.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4094474/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-18T15:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-144170>

I have mentioned many times on my blog and forums and on our FB groups that the mainstream ancestral people don't get the " atomic scale argument" when it comes to things like iodine and T3 and T4. One of the great things about seafood is that it contains selenium which helps convert thyroid hormones to active T3. While you can eat more carbs to increase free T3 levels (a common paleo recommendation), this increases the need for iodine even more. This creates a huge issue chronically especially in the CSF. ([http://www.medical-hypotheses....](http://www.medical-hypotheses.com/.../S0306-9877.../abstract\)). Most of the T4 is stored in our gut, so by eating seafood, you allow form to meet function as the seafood can permeate the gut where majority of T4 is stored, allowing for the conversion of more T4 to T3." While DHA has pretty amazing quantum effects, as Bill has talked about here before, it needs to be paired with iodine (as that's the HUMAN body's preferred antioxidant system) to protect DHA from oxidation. This occurs in synapses due to this Grotthuss mechanism especially for heat transfers. So, we've come full circle. You can either eat seafood and get a great evolutionary package of things like DHA, iodine and selenium, which will support thyroid health, or you can follow the traditional paleo path lacking in seafood DHA and iodine and continually up your carb intake to offset this loss. The "leadership of ancestral health" needs to tell people this will trigger a need for more iodine from their diet and since you're not getting it in "their" diet, your body will divert to backup antioxidant systems like vitamin C, vitamin E and glutathione. NO one is saying this because it hurts their business models. Now you see why so many young paleo folks follow "their thought leaders" eat lots of carbs and take supplements like glutathione and vitamin C year-round. They're forcing their bodies to rely on a suboptimal pathway which requires this constant band-aid approach.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-21T02:17:07+0300

> Symmetry is broken by any phase transition in chemistry. Any time symmetry is broken, **energy and information transfers must occur by nature’s laws.** This occurs many times in biochemical reaction of cells. And as such, all breaks of symmetry require a transfer of energy by the laws of physics to satisfy the Second Law of Thermodynamics. Symmetry is also broken any time temperature rises or falls or when electrons or protons are moving in any biochemical reaction. Any transfer of energy has the potential to break symmetry and therefore to give rise to emergent properties in the protein polymers or products of these reactions.

This part seems very important. Anytime we CT, we cause phase transitions, break symmetry, transfer energy and information. And we see emergent properties in our proteins, like helix formation perhaps.

> A Jew went to a whorehouse and told the madam that he wanted the cheapest girl in the place.
> 'We have a black girl for ten dollars,' she told him.
> 'But I only have four dollars,' he protested.
> After a long argument the madam consented to take him on herself for four dollars.
> The Jew did not return to the whorehouse for another ten years. When he did, the madam greeted him cordially and told him that as a result of their relationship years ago, he had a nine-year-old son. She called the boy out and introduced him to his father.
> 'So you are my dad,' said the boy. 'You know, ever since I was born I've wondered what my last name was. Tell me, dad, what is my last name?'
> 'Goldberg,' the father replied.
> 'My god!' exclaimed the boy, 'you mean I am jewish?'
> 'Don't knock it, boy,' he replied. 'If I had had six dollars more ten years ago you would have been black too.'

> A pregnant jewish girl asked her doctor what position she would have to lie in to give birth to her baby.
> 'The same position you were in when you started it,' the doctor told her.
> 'My god!' she exclaimed. 'Do you mean I will have to drive around Berlin in a taxi for two hours with my feet hanging out the window?'

### PaulG — 2014-10-29T04:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tensegrity-6.11899/post-145506>

Anyone interested in how H+ ions work in the mitochondria should read carefully Ling's 1981 68 page paper "[Oxidative Phosphorylation and Mitochondrial Physiology: A Critical Review of Chemiosmotic Theory, and Reinterpretation by the Association-Induction Hypothesis](http://www.physiologicalchemistryandphysics.com/pdf/PCP13-29_ling.pdf)"

Like most of Ling, it is not a quick and easy read but well worth persevering and plenty of nuggets (proton gradients, Mg2\+ and Ca2 vs swelling/shrinkage, mitochondrial electrical potential) in there which I am sure @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) will circle back to down the pike...

> SUBJECT OUTLINE
> I. INTRODUCTION
> 11\. THE CHEMIOSMOTIC HYPOTHESIS
> A. Generation of an electrical potential difference by postulated electrogenic pump vs. law of macroscopic electroneutrality
> B. Proton gradient
> C. Electrical potential gradient
> 1\. In vivo measurements
> a. Membrane disruption
> b. Electrode misplacement in the intermembrane space
> c. Inpocketing of mitochondrial membrane at microelectrode tip
> 2\. Insitu measurements
> 3\. Effect of valinomycin on resistance
> D. Is the mitochondrial inner membrane impermeable?
> 1\. Is there enough phospholipid in the inner membrane to form a continuous bilayer barrier?
> 2\. Is the mitochondrial inner membrane virtually impermeable to ions in general and H+ in particular?
> a. H+ permeability
> b. Cation impermeability
> (I) Inner membrane permeability to K+ and Nu+. (2) Inner membrane permeability to divalent cations.
> c. Anion impermeability
> E. Functions of uncoupling agents and ionophores
> F. ATP synthesis
> G. Conclusions regarding the chemiosmotic hypothesis
> 111\. THEORY OF THE LIVING CELL
> A. Basic features of the membrane-pump theory
> (I) Water. (2) Ions. (3) Cell surface barrier. (4) Membrane pumps. (5) Cell volume.
> (6) Resting potential.
> B. Basic features of the association-induction hypothesis
> (I) Water. (2) Ions. (3) Cell surface barrier. (4) Pumps and solute exclusion.
> (5) Cell volume. (6) Resting potential.
> C. Discriminatory experimental evidence
> 1\. Energy requirements of pumps
> 2\. Membrane vs. cytoplasm as the seat of discrimination in solute distribution
> 3\. The adsorbed state of K+
> 4\. Consequences of K+ binding in living cells
> IV. THE SOURCE OF ENERGY FOR ATP SYNTHESIS
> V. PROTEINS ACCORDING TO THE A1 HYPOTHESIS : HIGH- AND LOW-ENERGY STATES OF PROTEIN-ION-WATER SYSTEMS. PRIMARY INDUCTIVE EFFECT
> A. Inductive effect as the basis of energy and information transfer over distance
> 1\. Universal applicability of the inductive effect
> 2\. Target groups of the inductive effect
> a. The acid dissociation constant (or pK,)
> b. H-bonding strength
> c. Oxidation-reduction potential
> 3\. The inducing "groups"
> 4\. Additivity and reversibility of the inductive effect
> B. c -Value concept, linear model, and theoretically calculated change in preference for K+, Na+, H+, and NH4+
> C. Complex interaction between biologically active agents
> D. Cooperative behavior of living cells: interpretation based on the A1 hypothesis
> 1\. Cooperative adsorption isotherm of Yang and Ling
> 2\. Oxygen dissociation of hemoglobin and related phenomena
> a. Empirical analysis (Hill's equation)
> b. The AAKM theories
> c. The Yang-Ling model: comparison with other models
> 3\. Three types of autocooperative adsorption on proteins
> E. Autocooperativity in selective solute accumulation in living cells
> F. Control by cardinal adsorbents of shifts between two cooperative states
> VI. TENTATIVE MODEL OF ASSOCIATIVE-INDUCTIVE COUPLING MECHANISM FOR ELECTRON TRANSPORT AND OXIDATIVE PHOSPHORYLATION
> A. The coupling mechanism hypothesis
> B. Comparison with other models
> 1\. Heme-heme interaction and the Bohr effect
> 2\. Oxidation-reduction controlled autocooperative ion adsorption shifts
> a. Cytochrome c
> b. Hemoglobin
> c. Pyridine nucleotides
> VII. INTERPRETATION OF OTHER MITOCHONDRIAL PROPERTIES UNDER THE A1 HYPOTHESIS
> A. Additional basic A1 concepts
> 1\. Physical state of the bulk of cell water in living cells
> 2\. Swelling and shrinking
> 3\. Polarized water rather than membrane-lipid as seat of selective permeability
> 4\. Resting potential as surface adsorption potential
> B. Important findings in mitochondrial physiology and some new interpretations
> 1\. Swelling and its reversal by ATP
> a. Type I. Simple swelling and shrinkage
> b. Type 11. Dissociative swelling
> c. Type 111. Depolarizing swelling
> 2\. Reappraisal of previous reports according to the A1 hypothesis
> a. ATP and swelling/shrinkage of fetal and adult rat liver mitochondria
> b. Mg+ and Caw vs. swelling/shrinkage
> c. Passive osmotic swelling
> 3\. Ion and substrate transport
> 4\. Uncouplers, ionophores, Ca+, Mg+, ATP, and other agents as cardinal adsorbents
> a. Mechanism of action of valinomycin on mitochondrial K+ accumulation
> b. Ionophores, uncouplers, and other cardinal adsorbents: induction of c-value change as basis of their action on mitochondrial ion distribution
> (I) Respiration and anaerobiosis. (2) "Ionophores." (3) Uncouplers (4) Thiol reagents. (5) ATP and ADP.
> 5\. Synchronous oscillatory changes in mitochondrial swelling, ion uptake, and other properties
> 6\. Mitochondria1 electrical potential

# Thread: Newbie from the UK!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthyuk — 2014-10-15T13:38:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143914>

Hi All
Have been reading the forum for quite a while but have only just been able to post because of problems with the forum. I am trying to upload a pic but keep getting an error message, so will contact support.
I have started reading all the blogs - great reading but a lot to take in  Eat seafood twice a day everyday. Try to ground for 1 hr everyday and limit blue light & have switched off the wifi! And walk in the local woods most days.

So i have a few questions please if anyone can help would be very appreciated!

1\. I would like to download the Epcot rx, is this still up to date/current? Or is their a later version?

2\. I eat this trout [http://www.abelandcole.co.uk/rainbow-trout-fillets-200g](http://www.abelandcole.co.uk/rainbow-trout-fillets-200g)
and this salmon [http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=279468791](http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=279468791)
Can i eat these raw?

3\. This is the water i drink all the time [http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=259614228](http://www.tesco.com/groceries/product/details/?id=259614228)
Is this brand okay or are their better options.

4\. Can someone please point me in the right direction of the best place for someone in the UK to purchase a magnetico? Benefits or any particular type to buy?

5\. I am thinking of removing fat from my diet and eating solely fish. Is this a good idea or do i need to keep with the fat please? Can you live only on seafood?

6\. I have an electric meter in the bedroom and am currently in the process of getting it moved outside. I assume this will bring great benefits when done

7\. Would feeding my pets (cats & dogs) a seafood heavy diet give them the same benefits as us (humans)

8\. What is the one most important part of Jacks protocols? Grounding? CT? Removal of blue light? or something else?

Sorry for all the questions & hopefully some of you wont mind answering them

Thankyou!
HealthyUK

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-15T14:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-the-uk.11903/post-143920>

1\. Epcot Rx is fine.
2\. You could but you don't have to.
3\. Its fine if you get it in glass. If not stick to spring water or RO water in glass.
4\. No idea. I say craig-list/ebay of contact magnetico directly.
5\. Extremely bad idea.
6\. That is really bad.........it should never be in your house of directly outside of it.
7\. Yes. Both are carnivores.
8\. The most important thing is limiting blue light, avoiding non native EMF and adding back enough DHA to offset your losses from the non native EMF and blue light

# Thread: Another Blow To Genetic Research

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-10-16T04:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-blow-to-genetic-research.11907/post-143983>

A revolutionary new study reveals that the core tenet of classical genetics is actually false, overturning the entrenched view that hereditary information can only move from genes to body cells, and not the other way around, and by implication, proving that what we do in this life - our diet, mindset and chemical exposures - can directly impact the DNA and health of future generations.

[http://wakeup-world.com/2014/10/15/...irectly-into-sperm-cells-amazing-study-finds/](http://wakeup-world.com/2014/10/15/body-cells-transfer-genetic-info-directly-into-sperm-cells-amazing-study-finds/)

# Thread: Hair Loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melanie Procter — 2014-10-16T11:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-143996>

I am seeing so much hair loss in women these days. I just spent some time in Dubai and was doing work for the royal family. One of the princesses is 24 years old and is losing her hair. You can imagine the money she has spent trying to get to the bottom of it. I started searching the forum for a blog for her. I just stumbled across his recent blog about men's hair loss. I found this quote absolutely radical and thought I would repost it here.

"My reading is now focusing in on teleromase shortening of the hair follicles and early cell death.......and lack of replacement stemcells in the hair follicle......and its the MSH stem cells particularly of the hair follicle. It appears teleromase inhibitors can make hair grow and turn grey hair back to what it was in youth! The effect on Mice is off the chain. Bald Mice becoem shaggadelic pretty fast. But mice have long telomeres to begin with......we dont.

I have on older man (80) who is taking TA 65 and he has peach fuzz all over his head now and his hair is no longer grey. He went completely bald at 55. He is the one that got me thinking about a lot of stuff. Not sure this answer is helping you.....but the standard answers of hair docs is all about DHT modulation. After speaking to Bauman.....I am not a buyer of his science. Its not up to date.

I think guys who go bald early is because they are genetically limited in their MSH cells in the hair follicle and they undergo senescense earlier because their mitochondria have a faulty telomerase......or their is a signalling issue with it. It appears DHT brings the effect on quicker because DHT stimulates hair follicles to divide quicker so if you got genetically faulty mitochondria....and DHT makes them divide quicker.......well you exhaust your Hayflick number faster and you go bald as a young man. I think the whole blocking the 5 alpha reductase pathways partially with Propecia or totally with Avodart is playing Russian roulette with your pituitary and your sexual performance and ability to get a stiffy. There was just an article in one of the sex journals that completely under cut Bauman assertion that he has never seen anyones' pituitary gets french fried for a long time and never recover. Well there is a ton of them out there......I know cause I see them. Me, Id rather have my Johnson working and be bald. We know that when you screw with reproductive ability the body senses this and tries to take you out........its a signal for apoptosis. Cell death via suicide and cancer rates go up because of mTor and activation of NF kappa beta......my advice is stay bald and get a transplant........based on all I know currently based upon the latest science. But I have a feeling this story is going to evolve with telomerase drugs now hitting the mainstream anti aging scene."

### Shijin13 — 2014-10-16T13:33:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-144003>

Melanie

I have seasonal hair loss. mostly in the fall. I've linked mine to seasonal changes. Its finally starting to slow down....

### nicld — 2014-10-16T16:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-144009>

^^^^^ Go to know. I am "shedding" too.

### cantweight — 2014-10-16T16:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-144010>

This is the first fall I am not shedding and having major scalp issues....I am cautiously optimistic. My vit A cravings are not in even poking their heads out yet. Something is profoundly different in my world....and as usual I have no idea what...just goin with the flow.

### Shijin13 — 2014-10-16T17:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-144012>

### dhruv.d — 2014-12-23T13:57:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-loss.11910/post-150845>

I also suffer from Hair fall in winter. kindly give me some suggest for it.

# Thread: Newbie Alert - Hello
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-16T12:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-143997>

Hi all, just wanted to introduce myself \*wavingexcitedly

I do not have any thing in particular that I am trying to treat health wise, of course I have or have had ailments of various kinds throughout my life for one reason or another and I would like to address these with the advice & knowledge I gain from the site. I am well aware that people come to accept these niggles of poor health, rather than strive for optimal health and it is for this reason really that I have stumbled upon the amazing work of DrJK. After years of various reading and making changes here and there, I now feel like I have actually happened across something that will really make a difference and be life changing for not only myself but anyone around me that I can help and who is willing to listen. I am not a creative type rather than academic so I am aware the reading material may be a challenge for me. I have been listening to podcasts over the past couple of days, as I am on the road with my job quite a lot & I have found this to be a really great way of gaining some insight and understanding. I look forward to getting stuck into the forum in a few days and really getting my head into this new and exciting journey!!!!

Thanks, Sarah

### fitness@home — 2014-10-16T12:56:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-143999>

Welcome Sarah!

The reading matetial is very challenging to me on a regular basis...and I've been here a long time . What's nice is that when you go back and read older blogs, over time you have learned and they actually become easier to understand.

Hang in there, ask questions and and read, read, read.

### Sarah Hodgson — 2014-10-16T13:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144002>

Thank you ladies. Yes, it feels great when something suddenly makes sense so I guess we just need to accept that it is going to take time, and perseverance is key. I am currently listening to podcasts again as I work... I am looking forward to starting CT once I have had the chance to read exactly what I need to do. I doubt I will feel as excited when I am about to plunge myself into cold water teehee. But hey, in for a penny in for a pound!

### caroline — 2014-10-17T01:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-alert-hello.11911/post-144063>

Welcome Sarah! yes indeed .... in for a penny - in for a pound!
are you grounding? do you have blue blockers?

Please start a journal in the journal section .... it really helps to keep track of stuff and for motivation ....and fun to look back over at a later date and see our journey to our own n=1

# Thread: Self Actualize's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-actualizes-journal.11918/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Self Actualize — 2014-10-16T23:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/self-actualizes-journal.11918/post-144042>

Hello everyone, my name is Dave and I'm 20 years old. I'm Canadian, originally from Winnipeg, Manitoba but I've recently moved to Toronto, Ontario a few months ago(yay, more EMF).

I'm 5'11" and just under 160lbs, I've always been pretty skinny.

So, I'll start with recounting my life and what led me to have the current health issues I can, to the best of my ability.

I'm the youngest of four children and my mother had me when she was 36 years old (I think specifying age matters), she's been a smoker non-stop to varying degrees since she was 13.

When I was young, I was a child who was really hyper and had high amounts of energy.

My dad got sick with brain cancer when I was 5 and eventually passed away when I was 8. I was sad for a little bit, but I couldn't quite grasp the permanence of death at that young of an age. I now think this hampered my development as a man at least to some degree. This may or may not be useful, but I decided to add it in just to give as much insight as possible into my health.

With my dad out of the picture, my mother was left with working full-time and attempting to raise four kids on her own while coping with the loss of her spouse.

Because of all the things on my mom's plate, she didn't have time to cook home meals and a lot of the time they'd be frozen ready made things. She also couldn't say no so I could pretty much eat whatever I wanted in between meals which would usually be sweets or baked goods of some kind, and as much as I wanted.

Fast forward to junior high and highschool. I was always well liked and popular, but I got more and more shy, nervous, anxious, depressed as time went on. Leading me to isolate my self more and more. Most of my time I preferred to play video games and surf the internet. A habit that I'm still trying to break.

Since I was obsessed with the way people thought of me and how I was perceived, I started to care about my nutrition in a way that was motivated by vanity. In grade 10 to the middle of grade 12 I started following a high-carb, low fat diet that included processed grains, dairy, factory meats etc. In attempts to achieve a ripped body. I mostly did cardio with a bit of weights but I wasn't motivated enough to achieve much. I was basically treading water in highschool trying to stay afloat. At the same time, I started developing either psoriasis or eczema all over my body that kept getting worse and worse. Bags under my eyes were always there too and caused me a lot of grief. I used to eat a lot of sandwiches, I'm talking one ever two hours throughout the day with smoked chicken breast deli meat and miracle whip mayonnaise. Gross.

I also started reading up on conspiracy theories etc which made my depression and apathy worse. I would tell these things to my mom which made her want me to go on medication. I refused, as it never felt right to me. I'm glad I made that decision.

One thing led to another and I eventually started seeing a Naturopath. During the last two years, I received an allergy test, a male hormone panel and a blood test for thyroid hormones. I still have the papers for the allergy test and the male hormone panel, but I can't find the thyroid test. I can say that she did diagnose me with borderline hyperthyroidism (I remember reading about the inaccuracy of the tests and how it could be either hypo or hyper and I personally thought it was hypo as I could barely walk up the stairs at my workplace and generally felt like death. It improved a bit when I went on a supplement called Thyrosmart.) She advised that I follow a moderate diet that consisted of lots of mostly green vegetables, lean protein from non read meat, fruits and gluten free grains, with no processed sugar, alcohol, gluten, dairy, caffeine, corn, nightshades. This probably would've been pretty effective but I couldn't stick with it for over a week and would end up binging on baked goods and breads.

My allergy test was too big of a file and I'm not savvy enough with computers to figure out how to shrink it. The jist of it was that everything came back negative except for a moderate reaction to cows milk and whey and a very low reaction to egg yolk. It was performed in January of 2012.

My male hormone panel is attached below.

I've started to eat more seafood, no shellfish yet, I'm trying to eat up all my foods I bought before I found this site.

From reading this site, I get the importance of EMF to our functioning. I'm trying to cut down my use of the computer and am attempting to completely quit video games. I do live with two roommates who don't follow any of these rules so completely cutting it out isn't really possible. Plus, I work until 10:30 at night at a grocery store sometimes and don't get home until 11:30, whilst walking in artificial light from the street lamps and cars all the way home. What are some steps I can take to limit it as best as I can?

In closing, I've been trying to solve all these problems for years but I've been unable to stick to anything for very long due to a compromised mental state.

My problems in a easy to read list
\-brain fog (can't think clearly, focus for long periods and retain information very well)
\-anxiety
\-apathetic depression
\-trouble falling asleep most nights
\-frequent urination
\-small, not well formed and frequent bowel movements
\-skin problems (eczema all over my body and really purple bags under my eyes)

Also, is there anyone who lives in canada or ontario that could point me to a good list of seasonal fruits and vegetables. I've found some by googling but I'm not sure if they're technically the right ranges as there is probably some artificial things being done to extend certain seasons of fruits and vegetables.

I don't have a whole lot of money and resources to sink into a whole bunch of tests. If there's some that are absolutely needed, I'll do what I got to do, as health is the most important thing, but I'd prefer it if I could just spend that money on good quality foods.

I hope this is enough information and I hope you can navigate through my verbal diarrhea. If there's anything I should be doing specific to my case just let me know.

Thanks for reading,

David

### NeilBB — 2014-10-18T01:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/joan-rivers-cause-of-death-released.11920/post-144134>

Use of propofol anesthesia for surgical procedures, ICU sedation, and to stop intractible seizures is very standard in all hospitals today. What was unusual about MJ was that he was using the drug at home just to sleep. Anesthesia does carry risks, but what happened to Rivers and what happened to Jackson are far from same thing IMO...

Hope, thanks for your thoughts. I am using it now for limited time during sunrise and sunset. I think this way it makes me really good. I have let my kids to trya nd to decide whether they want. So, the experiment goes they ask for it just before bedtime and both of them know how to switch it off and they do switch it off every day a bit more later (I am secretly measuring the time). I gave it to my husband one morning still sleeping, so he did not know, full load 25 min and he ended up with massive headache that same day, whoch i treated in aprox 2 hours with charcoal. So for my experiments. I am pretty sure it makes a lot of effect. I started to use it for pains in the muscles I am slowly starting to get as the uterus is expanding (i had it with my first child quite a lot). i just leave it ont he ar which is in pain and something like spasm and take some extra magnesium, it is gone usually within 5 min, that is a real wonder!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-17T18:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-electromagnetic-organs.11921/post-144114>

DHA turns light into an electrical signal. When DHA is missing the electrical signal is missing in CM and tissues. What are the implication of this? Mitochondria have two CM and a matrix filled with H+. This is where mass charges are saved. Without DHA you can no longer use them as a super capacitor of electric charges. Why is this big? Without a charge is alters the masses of the subatomic particles within mitochondria. This is physics 101. This also points out why modern obesity researchers fundamentally clueless about fat. None of them are aware that according to Einstein's mass equivalence equation (E=mc2) that the mass of subatomic particles is an energenic variable that is subject to the electric charge of the surrounding environment; that is why the universal constant for gravitation has been shown to consistently inconstant by physics experiments when they have decided to measured it in space. Well that science scales to the surrounding environment in our mitochondria too. These laws are universal. No one seems to realize it. This implies that as our mitochondria lose their electric charge because DHA and iodine are missing in action, masses of all subatomic particles in mitochondria increases as well. At a macroscopic level where biology scale resides, we observe increases in our weight gain because we are losing energy from the interior of the mitochondrial matrix as ECT slows. As ECT slows electric charge drops. Hyperlipid has been pounding this point in his proton series for months. No one gets the implications. This is why we humans get fat fundamentally.

The less ideal your mitochondria hold their electric charges, the higher the fat version of the keto diet has to be. This is why Jimmy Moore can't handle carbs but most of the rest of us can. I would remind you all how does Jimmy make his $$$? TECHNOLOGY. He can never access autophagy to recycle his shot mitochondria. Guys like Ben Greenfield has bad mitochondria because of excessive endurance exercise but he can lower his fat content and use carbs because he can recycle mitochondria with a lack of electric charge. As your mitonchondria improve, the lower the fat content needs to be; as the protein levels can increase........as you improve further the protein levels in your diet should decrease because your ubiquination pathways improve so you don't not need to replace a lot of protein in your tissues. Bill has pointed out that people who are big time exerciser's don't need a lot of protein. This is 180 degree opposite the paleo idea. They love bacon and crossfitting. As you improve further, you begin to be able to exercise more, building your muscle mass as well and then you can add "seasonal carbs" back to your template with out risk. This is the part the low carb people do not understand. The more you exercise the less protein you actually need. This is another thing that the paleo kids do not understand because their "leaders" have no idea how ubiquination rates are tied to the amount of exercise you do. Ask Bill Lagakos if this is bad advice. I do not want anyone buying what I am selling. I want you to all know why I say what I do. My understanding is based upon a different "scale of science" than paleo biochemistry.

Moreover, this explains the mTOR controversy in aging too.

### BTA — 2014-10-17T22:25:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-electromagnetic-organs.11921/post-144123>

consuming a high fat diet overactives the mTOR pathway? Leading to a wide range of health problems and less muscle growth?

A study by Rivas et al.7 confirmed that consuming a high-fat diet triggers insulin insensitivity, yielding diminished mTOR signaling and muscle protein synthesis. mTOR inhibition stems from negative feedback within the insulin signaling pathway. Chronic consumption of a high-fat diet overactivates mTOR, which in turn heavily activates the molecule S6K. A hyperactive S6K produces inactivating signals within the insulin-signaling cascade, thus diminishing sensitivity to insulin along with mTOR activation and protein synthesis.

1\. Hawley JA, Gibala MJ, et al. J Sports Sci 2007; 25 Suppl 1, S115-124.2. Walker DK, Dickinson JIM, et al. Med Sci Sports Exerc 2011; 43(12), 2249-2258,3. Wilson GJ, Layman DK, et al. Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 2011; 301(6), E1236-1242.4. Pasiakos SM, McClung HL, et al. Am J Clin Nutr 2011; 94(3), 809-818.5. Gingras AA, White PJ, et al. J Physiol 2007; 579(Pt 1), 269-284.6. Olefsky JM and Glass CK. Annu Rev Physiol 2010; 72, 219-246.7. Rivas DA,Yaspelkis BB, et al. J Endocrinol 2009; 202(3), 441-451.8. McDaniel SS, Rensing NR, et al. Epilepsia 2011; 52(3), e7-11.9. Bielohuby M, Sawitzky M, et al. Endocrinology 2011; 152(5), 1948-1960.

Young people have bursts of energy and get the job done before someone tells them how impossible it will be. Older people may not have the same kind of energy but we have the stamina to keep going. We all have energy ... we are energy; we just have to plug into the right natural outlets to power up our lives for health. When we plug into the wrong outlets we come closer to the 6th extinction.

And BTW, don't need to obsess over raw vs cooked. So long as you're not using super high heat methods of cooking like deep frying, you'll still be getting lots of DHA (thanks to iodine for protecting it). Quantity would trump quality in many cases.

I'd think eating 200g of cooked mackerel would still deliver more DHA over 100g raw. (though my favourite method of cooking is definitely briefly seared on the outside while leaving the inside raw).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-144302>

### JMO — 2014-10-23T03:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-144785>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=144224)
>
> And BTW, don't need to obsess over raw vs cooked. So long as you're not using super high heat methods of cooking like deep frying, you'll still be getting lots of DHA (thanks to iodine for protecting it). Quantity would trump quality in many cases.
>
> I'd think eating 200g of cooked mackerel would still deliver more DHA over 100g raw. (though my favourite method of cooking is definitely briefly seared on the outside while leaving the inside raw).

Followed a link over the weekend that took me to a Q&A on DHA ...it stated that 40% of DHA was lost if it was frozen. I thgt it also had some cooking methods also lost DHA. Does anyone know the link....can't find it now?

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-23T06:27:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-144804>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=144785)
>
> Followed a link over the weekend that took me to a Q&A on DHA ...it stated that 40% of DHA was lost if it was frozen. I thgt it also had some cooking methods also lost DHA. Does anyone know the link....can't find it now?

This paper shows that pan frying salmon to 70C  reduced the DHA content by 40% -- [http://www.researchgate.net/publica...\_EPA\_and\_DHA\_loss/file/9fcfd50046720d9a70.pdf](http://www.researchgate.net/publication/223442449_Pan-frying_salmon_in_an_eicosapentaenoic_acid_\(EPA\)_and_docosahexaenoic_acid_\(DHA\)_enriched_margarine_prevents_EPA_and_DHA_loss/file/9fcfd50046720d9a70.pdf)

Specifically, the DHA concentration dropped from 581mg/100g when raw, to 351mg/100g when pan fried (to 70C/158F).

Firstly, salmon is better raw anyway , and even if you cook it thoroughly, I doubt you would want to cook it to 70C anyway (tastes lousy). Even most paranoid people would only cook it to 60-62C (145F). Or you can do a salmon aburi, and only sear the outside while leaving the inside raw, creating a tasty yet mostly raw piece of fish.

The C-O covalent bond between the glycerol backbone an the fatty acid is pretty strong, and is not likely to dissociate by cooking your fish to 70C. This just means that DHA that is in the sn-2 position stays in that position despite cooking.

So if you could only stomach 100g raw, but 200g cooked, you'll be getting more DHA by going the cooked route. (at least with salmon)

\---

As for freezing, you shouldn't freeze fish for that long, but I wouldn't be too concerned if you keep the freezing period at 3 months or under.

See this paper on Caspian white fish fillets being frozen at -18C -- [http://www.davidpublishing.com/davidpublishing/Upfile/5/8/2012/2012050812006534.pdf](http://www.davidpublishing.com/davidpublishing/Upfile/5/8/2012/2012050812006534.pdf) . This species of fish is pretty low fat, so that may skew the results, but still, with an original 15.07g/100g fat of DHA content in the fresh fillets, we see that drop to 14.73g/100g fat in 1 month, and then 14.05g/100g fat at 3 months, and then 6.58g/100g fat at 6 months. Even at 3 months freezing, the fish had only lost 8% of its DHA.

Another similar study from caspian sea fish being frozen at -24C -- [http://en.journals.sid.ir/ViewPaper.aspx?ID=179943](http://en.journals.sid.ir/ViewPaper.aspx?ID=179943) . We see that the rate of DHA loss differs based on the fish. Pike lost 21% of its DHA in 3 months, and Kilka lost 22% of its DHA in 3 months.

Another study on refrigeration of Caspian Kutum roe, which has a very high concentration of DHA -- [http://www.idosi.org/wjfms/wjfms4(5)12/19.pdf](http://www.idosi.org/wjfms/wjfms4\(5\)12/19.pdf) . Vacuum seal + refrigeration => 7.26g of DHA at 5 days, 5.81g of DHA at 120days (4 months). So in 4 months, the DHA content decreased by 20%.

Again, I don't view this as significant. If being able to buy in bulk and easily access twice the amount of fish, you are ahead in the DHA game. Just eat as much as you can

### ssj3 — 2014-10-23T09:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-144816>

You could always invest in a fishing rod???

Here herring are known as tommy ruffs... and they are awesome fresh, but dont freeze well. Vacuum Sealing them does make them last significantly longer.

### Inger — 2014-10-30T21:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-145676>

I make my herring like this
slice the herring, put in a bowl. Slice onion, thin, add. Grate fresh ginger, salt, ACV, brown pepper, garlic... and if doing carbs... a tiny bit of raw honey... just a touch.
Mix it all and let stay over night. Just made a huge bowl of it... I eat from the bowl several days in a row. So convenient and yummy! I never get tired of it

### Inger — 2015-01-21T19:02:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-153541>

Haaa... I did it    I spilled tea on my screen and now 30% from the right is blacked out.. the screen has a weird pale color all over
and i cant reply cause the button for
that is on the right side of the screen... but i just clicked all over in the dark and then i hit it  I was at the internet cafe today....
but now i can reply to you with this damaged computer yay
to read your posts i need to copy it into my word...lol or i can see only 70% of it..... haha... very complicted but i have figured out a way
there is always a way     and i bet a bit hard work helps to heal a broken heart

Ok... so.. to your questions.....

I buy my herrings Fhursday or Friday... make the "chevice" on Saturday normally.... i prefer whole herring they taste better
and i get the innards and heads too. Fillets tend to oxidize faster..
so i eat from the bowl many days until.. normally Wednesday. Today I ate the rest of the marinated herring. Tasted great still!

That said... I am not picky... when it tastes a tiny bit old i just eat it anyways. But I try to keep the fish on the collest place in my fridge.. it helps.
If you take out the innards and the herring is whole you could easily keep it for a couple of days.... if it is fresh.
My herring is not always that fresh when i buy it...... you can see it on the eyes if they are clear... if red, and not clear the fish is not so fresh
and also the older the fish... the softer it gets. Hard herring with clear eyes is very fresh. Same goes for mackerel.
I think mackerel and herring keeps about the same way... mackerel mis maybe a bit faster spoiled.
I have eaten pretty old herring and mackerel too.. no problem.. even if this is not ideal for sure. The fresher the better.. and especially
the taste!
You can freeze it immediately after taking out the innards. That way you will have it freshest. Mackerel and herring, both.

I usually take the fish milk (the white male stuff) and the roe (female) and the heads, and make small bags with a few heads, some roe, some fish milk.
That is for one fish head smoothie. And i make a few of these bags and put them in my freezer.
Then i leave a few heads and fishmilk and roe in a bowl in my fridge for a few days supply of fish head smoothie.
It will stink a little after a few days but if I have forgot to use them i use it anyways...hehe.... I just hold my nose and get it down....
Then i freeze bags with a few herring in each, usually 4 whole, that is a meal for me........

now let see if i can post this... darn have to blind search for the button again.....

### Veg — 2015-01-25T08:21:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-153796>

Update:
6 kg of fish (already gutted) seems to be too much. I have frozen 3 kg out of it. Rest 3 kg went into ACV.
Now I am eating it. First day flesh was not so tender. But second day it is tasting much better, but bones are making it difficult for me to enjoy them.
Therefore, I am wondering if there is any way of deboning the fish? I am new to fish and know very little about it.
Last experiment also showed me that I am not good in the kitchen and with cutting I need practice too and perhaps I have to buy a new sharp knife.

Yesterday, I have found another shop where I can buy Mackerel (whole) and may be herrings too. Trick about telling them about MEDICINE really worked here (otherwise they initially offered the same thing, that is mackerel gutted and without head).

I have never seen mackerel in my life before. Is it the same as herring? Inger, which one you prefer personally?

### Veg — 2015-01-25T13:02:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-153802>

Update:

It is the 3rd day morning, and the fish has become really tasty. Earlier it was little pink in color, but now the color has changed to white. With pink color it tasted raw, but with white color it does not tastes raw any more. Even the onions have started tasting great too.

### Inger — 2015-01-25T14:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring.11931/post-153805>

Veg, that is so cool.. I like it better when it sit a bit longer in the fridge too

I made it the other day and measured what i put in. That is what came out;

0,9 liters of loosely packed herring fillet pieces (readily cut into slices)
1 dl of ACV
1 teaspoon of seasalt
1 big onion, sliced
1 spoonful of Piment (whole brown pepper)

That was it, about  This is a small amount, I usually make about the double, so double all the amounts and you get what is in the herring bowl I eat from a few days / week

### Inger — 2015-01-25T14:16:11+0200

I debone my herrings all the time! Just fillet them..like any other fish. I have a video about how I fillet mackerel.. you can watch that? Google Ingerliese..mackerel sushi, on Youtube

I actually prefer mackerel to herring  it is even more delicious to me..... the mackerel heads are a bit harder and bigger so the smoothie will be a bit yuckier if you do not like pieces in it
but the meat is even softer and fattier which I like
Here I more often get herring this time of year. I guess mackerel is more of a season fish here. Herring I get all the time!

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=153806)

I am glad to know about deboning, as this is the only factor which was disturbing me. There is a black membrane inside which neither looks appetizing nor tastes good. It seems that after deboning I will also get rid of this black membrane too along with the bones.

And I made a mistake while searching for your video and wrote "Ingerlise mackerel sushi". It gave no results to me. Problem was this that I made a mistake in your name which is "Ingerliese" and also it was Makrelen (german word for mackerel) and also "Sashimi" instead of sushi. Nevertheless, I accidently found you while watching other mackerel filleting videos. I found it to be a very helpful fish Video by a pretty woman with wonderful feminine voice.

> I actually prefer mackerel to herring  it is even more delicious to me..... the mackerel heads are a bit harder and bigger so the smoothie will be a bit yuckier if you do not like pieces in it
> but the meat is even softer and fattier which I like
> Here I more often get herring this time of year. I guess mackerel is more of a season fish here. Herring I get all the time!

Challenge for drinking heads smoothie remains there and I am still fully pumped up to win this challenge.
Initially I thought 3 kg of herrings is too much, but I have almost finished them. "You have become a new fish eating Monster", I was told this by my little niece .

# Thread: Golden Tumeric Paste
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-tumeric-paste.11933/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-10-18T19:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-tumeric-paste.11933/post-144197>

Not my recipe...it is named Lily's Goldern Tumeric Paste.

I was looking for a way to get more tumeric into my daily life for the anti inflammatory and antiparasitic properties. This fits the bill. I can add to tea or just eat a spoonful of it.

\- 1/2 cup organic tumeric
\- 1 cup water, plus 1/2 cup
\- 1/4 cup raw honey
\- 1/2 cup organic coconut oil
\- 1/2 to 1 tsp black pepper
\- 1-2 tsp combined spices: nutmeg, garam masala, cinnamon, cardomom, etc to taste
\- a pinch of salt

How to make:

Here is where is experimented. I simmered one cup of water with grated ginger root, peppercorns, and cloves for about 10 minutes and then filtered out the water for use in recipe. Added enough water to it to make one cup.

Bring to boil and then add tumeric and whisk. Add spices and continue whisking. Add more water as needed. It will thicken.

Remove from heat after 5-7 minutes. Whisk in coconut oil. Add raw honey and stir until incorporated. I also added a big pinch of red pepper.

Pour into clean, sterile jars and cool. Store in refrigerator.

Now I have enough for at work and home.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### fitness@home — 2015-02-25T23:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/golden-tumeric-paste.11933/post-156606>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=156598)
>
> How long does it 'keep' in the fridge, do you know? A week or two?

I used sterilized canning jars when preparing this and stored them in the fridge. Those three jars lasted me about two months. Actually, I liked taking it straight off the spoon with a chaser of warm green tea. It had a very light sweetness to it. You could drop the raw honey, if you prefer a more savory flavor.

### fitness@home — 2014-10-19T02:16:35+0300

### Rain — 2014-10-19T00:22:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144219>

I apologize in advance for what will surely be a hard to read long email, but believe me it's the condensed version.

I was born by cesarean, to a stressed out mother because she was insane and my father was having an affair.

I nearly died after being born, they didn't know what was wrong with me, i was left to cry in my crib instead of bond with my insanely evil mother

I was sick and abused my whole life, sexually, emotionally, and physically abused at home, and as you may know you carry this 'energy' with you everywhere and the pattern continues forever.

I lost my health utterly by the time I was in my early 20's... I was seeing a doctor who later got kicked out of the medical profession for ruining so many peoples' lives, including mine.

She refused to treat HORRIBLE candida, my hmmhmmm was cut and bleeding and she still wouldn't treat it, she would just say things like 'do you have asthma yet?' and i would have it a few weeks later... the day she said 'ok it's time to test you for MS' i left her office and never went back

I live in canada, and monistat was not available otc at the time, but my brother started going over to border once a week to get me a box, the first box i got i had such a herx reaction i thought i was going to die.

I was so fat and sick by then that i found the 'yeast connection' diet and followed it with weekly monistat until my body gave out, the yeast became monistat resistent, my kidneys gave out, soon i had the worst case of what they called 'Chronic Fatigue Syndrome'... i had lost physics to mini strokes when i was younger and had become a computer programmer by the skin of my teeth on the yeast diet and monistat.

i lost my career, moved out to the coast so the cold didn't hurt so much, and the heat too.... and thought a juice fast would cure me, but no food regime has ever done a thing for my health ever since.

I have no gallbladder, I got most of my stomach cut out, i have IBS, i have horrible PTSD... i was bed ridden for years, diagnosed with CFS, Fibromyalgia, possible MS, Chronic Pain Syndrome that left me screaming in the emergency room every week, now i have either Rheumatoid Arthritis or Lupus, but refused to get tested as i don't want the drugs, oh and i also broke my back when i was 15 years old, a compression fracture of the 3rd and 4th lumbar vertebrae, if you can avoid such a thing i recommend you do.

I don't sleep at night for over 25 yrs, i went on a ketogenic diet 7 - 8 weeks ago and the diet, which by conventional thought should have killed the yeast beast dead, made it RAGE and was so bad i really feel as tho i may die if i don't do something fast.

i found ray peat 3 days ago, and added some sugar slowly, the yeast calmed down some, but the sugar is making me feverish and sick... i'm so sick right now, i'm going back to keto and asking Jack for help...

i am 52 years old now, have been fighting for my health for 30 years, to no avail. you are my last hope, i've tried water fasting because everything i ate, including a raw vegan diet of mostly fruit and carrot juice made me sicker, in fact it's why I ended up with RA or lupus.

on the keto diet i started having weird hypoglycemic comas, and i often feel dizzy and feverish now, and maybe the keto diet made me diabetic, because i didn't crave sugar once and the candida went off the charts, so i can only assume that i had a super high blood glucose level.

i'm fat from psych meds years ago, when i went without sleep for too long and had a nervous breakdown, i was terrified, and got PTSD...

my highest weight was 276 lbs and i'm only 5'5'' i weigh about 200 now, and i'm sick as a dog

i'm terrified because candida stole my life, and now it's back with a vengence.

i need your help, i'm mostly drinking my food, because eating and i don't really get along

being canadian, doctors only prescribe things that will hurt, not things that will help... plus they always say there's nothing wrong with me, of course, just all the mysterious illness catch all phrases.

my mother before me was fat and sick and tried her whole life to get well, but never did and died of pancreatic cancer in the end.

can you tell me what to do? help me, please, for the love of all that's good and holy

i'm sure i've forgotten a lot but i'm in so much pain from typing this that i'll stop here.

I'm really sorry to bother you with this impossible case.... ps, the doctor says there's nothing wrong with my thyroid, but my thyroid actually HURTS, is that normal? i doubt it is

also, all my fat is in my belly from a life of horrible stress from the moment i was conceived.

ok i'll stop now

thank you... help me?

When this happens to the microbiome, and Vitamin D levels drop. When they drop the gut flora simplifies more and it also decreases the resistance to other infections like candida. It is something called the Grotthuss mechanism. Realize that supplemental DHA does NOT FIX this because you need the other parts like iodine and selenium at the same time. Since you live in Canada this will be easy to do. Get naked in a warm spring or hot tub and at 100-104 degrees and then leave your top half out to the environmental cold while drinking cold water. This will improve the mitochondrial function in your brain and heart and help you feel better.
6\. The stressors in your life constantly destroy the amount of DHA in your mitochondria and tissues naturally, so the more stress the more seafood you will need. 7\. There is a particular flora that produces adiposity and obesity in humans. These bacteria make something called FIAF ([Fasting induced adipose factor](http://gordonlab.wustl.edu/PublicationPDFs/344_BackhedPNAS04.pdf)) that control this process. This factor blocks lipoprotein lipase (LPL) in fat cells. LPL allows us to convert dietary Free Fatty Acids carried in lipoproteins into neutral fats that are stored in adipocytes. This is why you have always been heavy. The Fournier effect will help you mobilize that fat.
8\. Your mitochondria wont be ready for these fats right away but if you keep up the Fournier effect they will rather quickly.
9\. Crabtree effect explanation:
Chronic elevated blood sugars induces something called the Crabtree effect. This mechanism down regulates mitochondrial biogenesis chronically leading to a state of low sex steroid hormones and infertility. When your hormones drop the candida has a field day in your microbiome. This is happening in you. The results will be seen in poor sleep. This is a sign of mitochondria that cannot make any superoxide and have to rely only on ketone derived acetyl-CoA. The end result on a macroscopic biochemical level is phosphorylation of the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex in mitochondria. This shutters ALL input to the Kreb cycle from glucose no matter what you eat. It leaves you with the sense you cannot eat carbs at all, otherwise your health goes to shit further. Mind you, this is actually a false belief, when reality is, it is your TOXIC environment driving the action on ECT! Oh the irony. If you keep missing the boat (most do), on the scale of actions occurring on your neurons, eventually you become unable to even use lactate as a back up system, and you will still get ill eating a high fat low carb diet. That is not good news for many. You become left with the belief you must sustain yourself with nutritional ketosis because you can only access ECT via ketone derived acetyl-CoA inputs to the mitochondria. That is why it happens. This calcium thing is a big deal with non native EMF. It links to most diseases at a very small scale called the quantum level of electrons and protons.
11\. Everything comes back to calcium efflux when your environment is the real problem.......it rarely is food. Hard for most to fathom but it is true. I spoke about this in Energy and epigenetics blog 3 and 4 which are free and you can google them.
12\. The FIAF is made by our liver, muscles, and our small bowel wall when food sources are in short supply. This is the signal to stop storing fat in our fat cells when food is scarce. What is not well appreciated by many is that the FIAF in our intestinal wall is controlled 100% by our gut flora. When we have a simplified gut flora it favors fat cell creation. When our gut flora is [complex and healthy](http://www.sciencefriday.com/segment/06/15/2012/mapping-the-microbial-make-up-of-healthy-humans.html), it has 100 trillion cells with 250 species. The gut flora’s action is directly signaled to the brain via the afferent nerve fibers in the vagus nerve. This nerve links your gut to your brain. This is why your brain is not working so well. It is not your fault. It is tied to the entire unwinding of this process.
13\. In those newly created fat cells, a protein called **leptin** is also produced, and acts as a score keeper for the brain of how much fat is stored in the body. This messenger is sent to the brain around midnight when we are sleeping allowing the brain to assess total energy balance in the body. When leptin is not working our brains have no way to tell how much energy is present. think of it like a car without a gas gauge. When you cant tell how much fuel you have you feel the need to eat a lot more often then you should. Eating many meals a day also blocks your mitochondria from recycling to get better.

You need to become cold dark and boring!

this is what your day should look like .....
when you rise - go outside barefoot and look at the sun - with as little clothes as possible

eat a BAB - using an epi paleo template

drink bottled water

no fake lite after sunset .... use candles etc.

get your computer wired in .... do away with wifi. No ipad, iphone, flat screen TV etc ...or very limited

get everything out of your bedroom ....alarm clock, TV, iphone, ipad ...no wifi!!!

make your bedroom as dark as possible

so ....when the sun sets - so should you!

what else do you want to know?????

Good morning Rain,
Don't think about the impossible,think about the possible,even if it is small.
Is there a way to curtain off the area where you sleep#? If not,can you partition it off a bit with existing
furniture?You could try sleeping with a sleepmask to get at least darkness for the eyes. Could you buy one of them freezable gel eyemasks ,combjne iy with a blackout one over it maybe? Dont know jf that works but you could have a look if you can make them stay together. There is a thread about good sleep and what to do that started recently. One of the things the doc said. Is to breathe jn the cold air from the freezer while yawning.
Food: As much seafood as you can . With you stomach issues could you manage tuna? Can gou get oysters? Oysters would probably help most as they come in a conveniently small package.
Are you mobile enough to go outside and find a park or a patch of grass to enjoy theautumn sun while sityjng barefoot on the ground in cotton clothes? I think as you can only do small thigs you need to do a lot of them,even if they are not 100% optjmal but better than nought,right?
Now about that speed dial, forget about it....
Thinking like that traps you into a dopamine steal syndrome and I woild hazard a guess that you may be deficient in that. You are probably thinkjing "easier said than done" buy think that this is cumulative. Every minute you manage to thjnk "pretty" is a bit of charge jn the ole battery. Music,especially opera fires me up.
There are days when I can almost"eat"
it. Sounds silly? Don't be afraid to be. Hum when you feel despair. It is easy,it
is free and may help.

Hugs from the other end of the continent

I am busy on the job and cannot take the time to really explain much, but I know that my reading one book 7 years ago had me eating differently, feeling better, and searching for why that was, and ultimately to more and more understanding as I kept following those rabbit holes. This is amazing how Jack reached out to Elaine and offered some simple solutions to get started right now.
For instance I pointed a woman to a book 2 weeks ago, and she contacted me yesterday and told me her diabetic sugars were better and she lost 7+ lbs already. When she is ready I will point out another book. I did tell her I eat fish once a day and how important that was.

Elaine embrace your discomfort. it is the force that is going to heal you even though I know that sounds and may feel a bit counterintuitive to you right now. Trust me, I've been there and done it. I know how you're feeling. There is a light and hope. But now you MUST run to it no matter how bad you feel currently. True character is revealed in the choices a human being makes under pressure and you are under that stress now - the greater the pressure, the deeper the revelation becomes. This is what you will find when you get well. Pressure can be a privileges when it is directed. Remember, no pressure, no diamonds in life. As you begin to aim higher in life it seems as though you have more responsibility. This responsibility creates stress and pressure in you and your environment. Without these guiding factors, one can not grow and achieve the greatness you seek. Stay focused and channel your efforts via a "tunneled vision", and you will marvel at the amount of pressure you create in the creation of that vision. It's not what you want or think that you can achieve, it's what you can accomplish that really matters. The more prepared your wisdom is, the less pressure you feel in any task you take. You will become prepared by following those 31 points.

In the swollen mitochondria, evident shrinkage may be induced by adding Mg2+ ions in the absence of exogenous ATP. This is why taking Mg2+ supplements without knowing the context is a big error most make. Guys like the Magnesium man don't get this scale of action. You must. When you are suffering in an environment of high non native EMF you must realize the environment must be hacked before your diet or any supplements. ### nicld — 2014-10-19T19:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144301>

Welcome Elaine and you are in the right spot.

Jack is right on, embrace the discomfort.

With a small stomach, maybe puree the oysters and drink with seafood broth. I do not have access to fresh oysters so it is canned. I have can to a can and a half every morning for me along with salmon and it has made a huge difference in the mental clarity. Check out Inger's fish head smoothies. I destroyed my health by working nights and going back to college. I lost alot of my smarts but it is coming back.

Toss a towel over the alarm clock. That's what I do with the one of the hubbies side. I have a battery operated one emits no light. Maybe look into getting one of those for him. I have this mask and it works pretty good. [http://www.amazon.com/dp/B000CCI4YU/ref=sr\_ph?ie=UTF8&qid=1413735709&sr=1&keywords=sleep+mask](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B000CCI4YU/ref=sr_ph?ie=UTF8&qid=1413735709&sr=1&keywords=sleep+mask)

Also get a pair of these ASAP.
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox\_sc\_sfl\_title\_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox_sc_sfl_title_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER) These have made the biggest difference for me in helping get the sleep better. I used to get tired, crawl in bed, lay there for an hour or two, sleep maybe one cycle, wake back up for a couple of more hours and if I was lucky, get one more cycle in before needing to get up for work and felt like crap. Now, I put these on as soon as it get dark, and can feel the melatonin increase (I get cold when that does) and can fall asleep fairly soon and the best part, stay asleep.

Oh and i think CT rocks and LOVE my deep CT. I use the snow to get my baths really really cold in the winter.

There is hope but it will take alot of work and maybe some pretty hard decisions.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-19T20:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144306>

[Candida proteins resemble gluten](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=hwp1+protein+gluten) proteins so they all cause a leaky gut. A leaky gut is on that leaks too much light from the microbiome to your enterocytes. This light destroys the amount of Vitamin D in enterocytes. This decreases the activation of the immune system in the GALT which is right behind the gut lining. This is why some people who have tried the recent paleo craze of resistant starch have contacted me about their recent spikes in their autoimmune conditions. CONTEXT IS KEY and I pointed that out in the January 2014 webinar on my RS bio hack. Once an immune reaction against candida commences, patients will generally develop frank celiac or severe gluten intolerance/hypersensitivity. Celiac disease can be created by this mechanism using mis folded proteins that alter the polarized light signal to T regulator cells if certain HLA DQ2/8 immune cells are present. The lower your vitamin D level is the more likely they will be present. The immune system will begin to slowly attack foods with wheat, barley, rye and oats with a vigorously because these contain or have proteins antigens that simulate gluten when macrophages present the antigen to the T regulator cells. This causes a loss of iodine and electrons in proteins making them more hydrophobic and less hydrophilic meaning theire will be less H+ in the enterocytes and the GALT in which to turn of the switch. This has massive effects on T4 and T3 production in the gut too. Stopping gluten in foods helps........but it is only a very small part of the Rx for fixing this night mare. I personally believe this is how Hashimoto's has exploded in our modern world.

> [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox\_sc\_sfl\_title\_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000NP6NG2/ref=ox_sc_sfl_title_5?ie=UTF8&psc=1&smid=ATVPDKIKX0DER) These have made the biggest difference for me in helping get the sleep better. I used to get tired, crawl in bed, lay there for an hour or two, sleep maybe one cycle, wake back up for a couple of more hours and if I was lucky, get one more cycle in before needing to get up for work and felt like crap. Now, I put these on as soon as it get dark, and can feel the melatonin increase (I get cold when that does) and can fall asleep fairly soon and the best part, stay asleep.

> Oh and i think CT rocks and LOVE my deep CT. I use the snow to get my baths really really cold in the winter.
>
> There is hope but it will take alot of work and maybe some pretty hard decisions.

I don't think my whole house is grounded but I have a personal grounding strap hooked up now. I have a spot in my basement with open ground (I have an old old house). To this, I have another copper wire attached and have attached a grounding wrist strap to my arm. Oh, I also have a pretty inexpensive volt meter that I am using to test everything, borrowed it from work since it has been sitting at my desk for a couple of years.

### Justfourme — 2014-10-20T01:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-for-calcium-help-its-elaine.11935/post-144334>

rain - when I read through your posts, I am amazed by your strength and determination. Dr Kruse has given you a very precious gift, read and re-read often.
As Caroline suggested, a journal is a good idea, not only for communication with others, but you can reference back to it to remind yourself of the milestones you have already achieved.
Most of us here are not afraid to think outside of the box, you need to challenge yourself to do the same.
You have an RV you can no longer use, can you sell or rent it out? Could be an income stream that you could use to buy a different RV, away from EMF, out by a nice cold stream in the woods...
No tub for CT....and Jack recommends a hot tub with cold air....but you have a shower - so buy a big plastic tote that will fit in the shower, fill with warm/hot water and stand or kneel in that with ice packs on your top half. Zip lock baggies tied on with towels would be a cheap ice vest.
Make sure along with the seafood, you are eating the best quality organic food you can. Yes, organic can be expensive so spend $ wisely. Start with organic berries, apples and lettuce.
And bone broth - very healing and would be easy on your stomach.
Remember you didn't get this way overnight, so you won't be fixed overnight. Baby steps for now.
And as far as your hubby - he has stuck with you thru all the ups and downs. He will be amazed at how you will be able to turn things around.
We are all here with different challenges and will be here to support and guide.
Welcome!!
