# Thread: Ebola Airborne
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ebola-airborne.11959/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-10-21T18:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ebola-airborne.11959/post-144604>

Interesting to see more than 25 years ago the Military discovered Ebola can be transmitted by air if it's cold. Cold temperatures cause viruses to be more virulent. Must be a tie into how it helps humans and has an effect on viruses?

**Ebola can spread by air in cold, dry weather common to the U.S. but not West Africa, presenting a “possible, serious threat” to the public, according to two studies by U.S. Army scientists.**

After successfully exposing monkeys to airborne Ebola, which “caused a rapidly fatal disease in 4-5 days,” scientists with the _U.S. Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases_ (USAMRIID) concluded Ebola can spread through air but likely hasn’t in Equatorial Africa because the region is too warm, with temperatures rarely dropping below 65°F.
“We… demonstrated aerosol transmission of Ebola virus at lower temperature and humidity than that normally present in sub-Saharan Africa,” the 1995 study entitled [_Lethal Experimental Infections of Rhesus Monkeys by Aerosolized Ebola Virus_](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1997182/) reported. “Ebola virus sensitivity to the high temperatures and humidity in the thatched, mud, and wattle huts shared by infected family members in southern Sudan and northern Zaire may have been a factor limiting aerosol transmission of Ebola virus in the African epidemics.”
“Both elevated temperature and relative humidity have been shown to reduce the aerosol stability of viruses.”

The study also referred to the 1989 Ebola outbreak at a primate quarantine facility in Reston, Va., in which the virus rapidly spread between unconnected rooms.
“While infections in adjacent cages may have occurred by droplet contact, infections in distant cages suggests aerosol transmission, as evidence of direct physical contact with an infected source could not be established,” the study added.
It is interesting to note this outbreak occurred in [December 1989](http://www.foxnews.com/science/2014/09/19/how-virginia-suburb-became-ebola-epicenter/), when [temperatures in Reston were usually below freezing](http://www.wunderground.com/history/airport/KIAD/1989/12/15/MonthlyHistory.html), and it’s unlikely the indoor temperature in the vast quarantine facility was much higher.

A 2012 study also by the USAMRIID, which exposed monkeys to an airborne filovirus similar to Ebola, reached a similar conclusion to the 1995 study.
“There is no strong evidence of secondary transmission by the aerosol route in African filovirus outbreaks; however, aerosol transmission is thought to be possible and may occur in conditions of lower temperature and humidity which may not have been factors in outbreaks in warmer climates,” the study entitled [_A Characterization of Aerosolized Sudan Virus Infection in African Green Monkeys, Cynomologus Macaques and Rhesus Macaques_](http://www.mdpi.com/1999-4915/4/10/2115/pdf) stated.
The study pointed out that filoviruses, which include Ebola and the Sudan virus used in this particular study, have stability in aerosol form comparable to influenza.
“Filoviruses in aerosol form are therefore considered a possible, serious threat to the health and safety of the public,” it added.

And the Pentagon took this threat of airborne filoviruses so seriously that it organized a [_Filovirus Medical Countermeasures Workshop_](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4113787/) with the Department of Health and Human Services in 2013.
“The DoD seeks a trivalent filovirus vaccine that is effective against aerosol exposure and protective against filovirus disease for at least one year,” the [executive summary of the workshop](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4113787/) stated.
The Pentagon’s concern with airborne Ebola runs contrary to health officials who claim the disease can’t spread through coughing and sneezing, but according to the Army studies, that may only be true in tropical climates.
“How much airborne transmission will occur will be a function of how well Ebola induces coughing and sneezing in its victims in cold weather climates,” the web site [potlblog.com](http://pissinontheroses.blogspot.com/2014/09/us-army-says-ebola-flu-in-airborne.html) suggested. “Coughing and nasal bleeding are both reported symptoms in Africa, so the worst should be expected.”

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-10T05:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-iv-ozone-for-ebola-or-any-other-infectious-disease-an-idea-whose-time-has-come.11961/post-146728>

_**The Chemistry of Ozone**_

The O2 molecule is not the only elemental form of oxygen. In the presence of lightning or another source of a spark, O2 molecules dissociate to form oxygen atoms.

spark
O2(_g_) { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

2 O(_g_)
These O atoms can react with O2 molecules to form ozone, O3,

O2(_g_)+O(_g_)

O3(_g_)
whose Lewis structure is shown in the figure below.

Oxygen (O2) and ozone (O3) are examples of **allotropes** (from the Greek meaning "in another manner"). By definition, allotropes are different forms of an element. Because they have different structures, allotropes have different chemical and physical properties (see table below).

_Properties of Allotropes of Oxygen_

_Oxygen (O2 )_ _Ozone (O3 )_
Melting Point -218.75oC -192.5oC
Boiling Point -182.96oC -110.5oC
Density (at 20oC) 1.331 g/L 1.998 g/L
O-O bond order 2 1.5
O-O bond length 0.1207 nm 0.1278 nm
Ozone is an unstable compound with a sharp, pungent odor that slowly decomposes to oxygen.

3 O3(_g_)

3 O2(_g_)
At low concentrations, ozone can be relatively pleasant. (The characteristic clean odor associated with summer thunderstorms is due to the formation of small amounts of O3.)

Exposure to O3 at higher concentrations leads to coughing, rapid beating of the heart, chest pain, and general body pain. At concentrations above 1 ppm, ozone is toxic.

One of the characteristic properties of ozone is its ability to absorb radiation in the ultraviolet portion of the spectrum ( > 300 nm), thereby providing a filter that protects us from exposure to high-energy ultraviolet radiation emitted by the sun. We can understand the importance of this filter if we think about what happens when radiation from the sun is absorbed by our skin.

Electromagnetic radiation in the infrared, visible, and low-energy portions of the ultraviolet spectrum ( < 300 nm) carries enough energy to excite an electron in a molecule into a higher energy orbital. This electron eventually falls back into the orbital from which it was excited and energy is given off to the surrounding tissue in the form of heat. Anyone who has suffered from a sunburn can appreciate the painful consequences of excessive amounts of this radiation.

Radiation in the high-energy portion of the ultraviolet spectrum ( 300 nm) has a different effect when it is absorbed. This radiation carries enough energy to ionize atoms or molecules. The ions formed in these reactions have an odd number of electrons and are extremely reactive. They can cause permanent damage to the cell tissue and induce processes that eventually result in skin cancer. Relatively small amounts of this radiation can therefore have drastic effects on living tissue.

In 1974 Molina and Rowland pointed out that chlorofluorocarbons, such as CFCl3 and CF2Cl2, which had been used as refrigerants and as propellants in aerosol cans, were beginning to accumulate in the atmosphere. In the stratosphere, at altitudes of 10 to 50 km above the earth's surface, chlorofluorocarbons decompose to form Cl atoms and chlorine oxides such as ClO when they absorb sunlight. Cl atoms and ClO molecules have an odd number of electrons, as shown in the figure below.

As a result, these substances are unusually reactive. In the atmosphere, they react with ozone or with the oxygen atoms that are needed to form ozone.

Cl+O3

ClO+O2
ClO+O

Cl+O2
Molina and Rowland postulated that these substances would eventually deplete the ozone shield in the stratosphere, with dangerous implications for biological systems that would be exposed to increased levels of high-energy ultraviolet radiation.

UV light kills everything except at one frequency. 380 nm.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-22T04:39:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-aspect-to-daily-life-dark-circles.11962/post-144675>

OK...........I thought about this because I think it is interesting and important clinical sign. Dark Circles are tied to melanin and alpha MSH and POMC.
Sulfur plays an important part, as a carrier of **_reducing hydrogen and its electrons_**, for cellular repair of oxidation. This changes hydrogen's abilities to to things. Reduced [glutathione](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glutathione), a sulfur-containing tripeptide, is a reducing agent through its sulfhydryl (-SH) moiety derived from [cysteine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cysteine). The [thioredoxins](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thioredoxin), a class of small protein essential to all known life, using neighboring pairs of reduced cysteines to act as general protein reducing agents, to similar effect.

One specific feature of skin cells is that the skin stores sulfate ions attached to molecules that are universally present in the intracellular matrix, such as heparan sulfate, chondroitin sulfate, and keratin sulfate \[Milstone-1994\]. In the research it has been shown that exposure of the melanocytes to molecules containing _reduced_ sulfur (-2) leads to _suppression_ of melanin synthesis \[Chu2009\]. This will lead to vitiligo like syndromes. Now when we consider dark circles we need oxidized sulfur anions to get dark skin. Keratocytes exposure to molecules like chondroitin sulfate that contain _oxidized_ sulfur (+6) should lead to enhancement of melanin synthesis \[Katz 1976\]. The body sacrifices +6 S for −2 sulfur in weird way to protect its stores of sulfur, so it sacrifices +6 sulfur to the sun god. Very few foods have +6 sulfur and I think this is why it is sacrificed because sulfur containing amino acids are rare in the food chain. Dark rings are seen in adrenal fatigue and I think this is because we are trying to reserve the −2 sulfur in good foods.
Melanin is a potent UV-light absorber, and it would compete with reduced sulfur (-2) for the opportunity to become oxidized. So when sulfur is reduced and filled with electrons, melanin synthesis is likely suppressed, so that sulfur acts as sponge for UVB light and can absorb the solar energy and convert it to very useful chemical bonds in the sulfate ion. So what is around the eyes that has a very high metabolic rate? The extraocular eye muscles. In these muscles, sulfate ions would eventually be converted back to sulfide by a eye muscle cell's. Why? This action would allow for a perfect coupling cycle. It would allow for simultaneous recovery of the energy to fuel the cell by unlocking oxygen in sulfate ion. Remember when sulfate (-2) is low so is Vitamin D sulfate and this up regulates glucose metabolism and AMPK pathways. This would support aerobic metabolism of glucose, and the cycle would continually repeat further darkening you eye skin above this coupling.

# Thread: 21 cm Hydrogen line?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-cm-hydrogen-line.11963/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2014-10-22T12:48:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/21-cm-hydrogen-line.11963/post-144705>

I was watching [Brian Cox's Human Universe](http://www.bbc.co.uk/iplayer/episode/p0276pxp/human-universe-3-are-we-alone) last night and he was talking about searching for life on other planets. He said something about electromagnetic frequencies being emitted 21 cm from hydrogen atoms. Is this something we should be thinking about in biology?

> The **hydrogen line**, **21 centimeter line** or **HI line** refers to the [electromagnetic radiation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electromagnetic_radiation) [spectral line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spectral_line) that is created by a change in the energy state of neutral [hydrogen](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen) atoms. This electromagnetic radiation is at the precise frequency of 1420.40575177 [MHz](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megahertz), which is equivalent to the [vacuum wavelength](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wavelength#in_non-vacuum_media) of 21.10611405413 [cm](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centimeter) in [free space](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Free_space). This wavelength or frequency falls within the [microwave](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Microwave) [radio](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radio) region of the [electromagnetic spectrum](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electromagnetic_spectrum), and it is observed frequently in [radio astronomy](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radio_astronomy), since those [radio waves](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radio_wave) can penetrate the large clouds of interstellar [cosmic dust](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cosmic_dust) that are [opaque](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opacity_%28optics%29) to [visible light](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visible_light).
>
> The microwaves of the hydrogen line come from the atomic transition between the two [hyperfine levels](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperfine_levels) of the hydrogen [1](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Principle_quantum_number)[s](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Azimuthal_quantum_number) [ground state](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ground_state) with an energy difference of 5.87433 µeV.[\[1\]](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen_line#cite_note-Hyperphysics-1) The frequency of the [quanta](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum) that are emitted by this transition between two different energy levels is given by [Planck's equation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Planck%27s_constant).
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen\_line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrogen_line)

# Thread: Ebola & Non-native EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ebola-non-native-emf.11967/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BTA — 2014-10-23T00:02:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ebola-non-native-emf.11967/post-144766>

Interesting to see the research on Ebola & genetic snips. Humans use to make a certain enzyme that would destroy viruses like Ebola, AIDS, ect... However, genetic snips have been found which means humans stop making the enzyme. The research doesn't point to non-native EMF but I'm thinking the environment has allowed viruses to run loose?

**High-Dose Mannose-Binding Lectin Therapy for Ebola Virus Infection**

**[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3071052/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3071052/)**

Persons with mutations of both MBL2 alleles constitute between 1 and 65 percent (median 5 percent) of the population, depending upon the ethnic group. Heterozygosity, or the presence of mutations in one MBL2 allele, is found in more than 30 percent of the population in most ethnic groups evaluated. Thus, up to 40 percent of most populations may have mutations in one or both MBL2 alleles (See ["Mannose-binding lectin", section on 'The MBL gene'](http://forum.jackkruse.com/contents/mannose-binding-lectin?source=see_link&anchor=H2#H2).)

Several common mutations of the MBL2 gene can lead to a condition called mannose-binding lectin deficiency. People with this condition have low levels of mannose-binding lectin and may be susceptible to recurrent infections. Several of the disease-associated mutations occur in a region of the MBL2 gene known as exon 1 and result in a change to single protein building blocks (amino acids) in the mannose-binding lectin subunit. Other mutations occur in an area of DNA near the MBL2 gene called the promoter region, which helps control the production of the mannose-binding lectin subunit.

Functional mannose-binding lectins are made up of two to six protein groups called trimers, which are each composed of three of the protein pieces (subunits) produced from the MBL2 gene. This protein complex plays an important role in the immune system's response to foreign invaders (pathogens).

Mannose-binding lectin recognizes and attaches (binds) to sugars, such as mannose, fucose, and glucose, that are found on the surface of bacteria, viruses, and yeast. This binding turns on (activates) the complement system, which is a group of immune system proteins that work together to destroy pathogens, trigger inflammation, and remove debris from cells and tissues. Attachment of mannose-binding lectin also targets the pathogen to be engulfed and broken down by special immune cells. Recognition of foreign invaders by mannose-binding lectin provides one of the body's first lines of defense against infection.

Cytogenetic Location: 10q11.2
Molecular Location on chromosome 10: base pairs 52,760,859 to 52,772,817

### HoneyChild — 2014-10-23T15:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-shells.11970/post-144845>

Re: soil, I've been reading recently that chitin from exoskeletons are amazing. The earthworms produce chitinase in order to digest the chitin. The plants then can use the chitinase to destroy the exoskeletons of insects which are trying to eat the plants. (I hope I've understood that correctly anyway.) Apparently you can also buy crab shell fertilizer. My plan is to add whatever exoskeletons I can get into my compost bin and start growing a wide variety of veggies next year (I've already planted a single clove of garlic I bought from the supermarket!)

> **Insect Repellency**
>
> Testing has shown that several microorganisms found in worm castings work as effective repellants for a large array of insects. **A key element for insect repellency is the level of chitinase-producing organisms.** Chitinase is an enzyme that will dissolve chitin. The exoskeleton of bugs is made of chitin \[so obviously they don't want to hang out where their skeletons will get dissolved!\]. Bugs have various detection mechanisms to determine the level of chitinase in plants and soil. Once the chitinase level is high enough, they will leave the area. **Worm castings test with a very high level of chitinase-producing organisms.**
>
> Also, some elements in worm castings are able to activate the chitinase-producing organisms found inside plants. Once triggered, these organisms multiply to a level that can be detected by the insects. The repellency effectiveness has been tested and confirmed for white fly, aphids, spider mites and various other bugs. Twenty various plants have been tested showing effectiveness. The rate of repellency is in direct relation to the size of the plant. Spider mites will leave houseplants in about 2 weeks, aphids leave roses in about 6 weeks, and white flies will leave full sized hibiscus in about 3 months.
>
> In November 2002, California Vermiculture was granted a U.S. Patent for the use of worm castings as an insect repellant, and they in the process of obtaining EPA approval to label their worm castings as an insect repellant. Currently, Wormgold® castings are being tested for [use against the pine bark beetle](http://www.organicrosecare.org/articles/worm_bark_beetle.php).
>
> [http://www.organicrosecare.org/articles/worm\_castings.php](http://www.organicrosecare.org/articles/worm_castings.php)

### Penny — 2014-10-26T04:39:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-shells.11970/post-145188>

I take one chitosan every night

**The antimicrobial action of chitosan, low molar mass chitosan, and chitooligosaccharides on human colonic bacteria.**

[Simůnek J](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Sim%C5%AFnek%20J%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22528310)1, [Brandysová V](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Brandysov%C3%A1%20V%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22528310), [Koppová I](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Koppov%C3%A1%20I%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22528310), [Simůnek J Jr](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Sim%C5%AFnek%20J%20Jr%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22528310).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22528310#)**

**Abstract**
Antibacterial effect of chitooligosaccharides (COS) and low molar mass chitosans (LMWC) is considered as one of the most important characteristics of chitosan (CS) hydrolysates. Here, we show the in vitro effect of different COS, LMWC, and CS on representative anaerobic bacteria isolated from human colon as a possibility of targeting modification of colonic microflora composition by supplementation of dietary CS products by humans. Specific growth rate of seven selected nonpathogenic anaerobic bacterial strains (Clostridium paraputrificum, Clostridium beijerinckii, Roseburia intestinalis, Bacteroides vulgatus, Bacteriodes thetaiotaomicron, Faecalibacterium prausnitzii and Blautia coccoides) was determined in the presence of 0.25 and 0.5% COS (2, 3, and 6 kDa), 0.025 and 0.05% of LMWC (10 and 16 kDa), and 0.025 and 0.1% of CS in vitro. The growth rate decreased in all strains in the presence of COS and LMWC in higher concentrations in comparison to control incubations. A relatively higher resistance to CS hydrolyzates was detected in R. intestinalis and F. prausnitzii, and more susceptible were bacteria belonging to Bacteoides sp. and Clostridium sp. The antimicrobial activity, minimum inhibitory concentrations (MIC), and minimal bactericidal concentrations (MBC) were determined. The antimicrobial activity increased with the degree of polymerization (DP). MIC ranged from 0.25 to 4.5% in dependence on bacterial strain and DP of CS/LMWC. MBC also decreased with DP. The most effective antimicrobial action was detected in LMWC with 16 kDa and CS. Weak antimicrobial activity was found in COS with small molecules (2 and 3 kDa).

### Sue-UK — 2014-10-26T11:31:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-shells.11970/post-145206>

My bio hack is without supplements (about to make an exception with enzymes after 10 months) but I'm wary of chitosan as a supplement as its marketed as a fat magnet .... (but timing and context I know , taking at night different to taking with food ......) .....Intuitively a bit of exoskeleton going down as a lump with the seafood giving the good bacteria a work out draws me more......

I've ordered a bag of fine oyster shell grit, I'm going to put some in the outside CT bath with a couple of largish pieces of seaweed .... Give the water information ..... Then maybe mix a little oyster grit into a glass of well water, sieve if necessary ... drink on the basis that in addition to any conventionally detectable nutrients, the liquid that comes from an oyster is also informed water... (Nicole , maybe you are doing this intuitively with your ground up shrimp shells, informing the next dish? )

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-23T15:05:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-the-vaccine-or-is-it-something-ancient.11972/post-144833>

What is else differs that might explain the difference? Have you ever stopped to ask yourself that? Pertussis toxin is released from B. pertussis in an inactive form. Following the toxin binding to a cell membrane receptor, it is taken up in an endosome, after which it undergoes retrograde transport to the trans-Golgi network and endoplasmic reticulum. What keeps the toxin out of the cell membrane? Sulfate in the blood plasma. Why? Sulfur can go from +6 to −2 in its forms. This means there is a lot of electrons (that can carry a lot more of photons) to activate T regulators cells in immunity. Sulfate in blood carries more electrons. because of this abiity. Sunlight releases more sulfate in our skin and gut to protect all barriers. What is the difference between Colorado and Mississippi again? Sulfate production occurs due to UVB and IR light naturally. You stop making sulfates in your skin on August 31 and begin to have to use nitrates to protect your barriers. Nitrogen protection goes from −2 to 0. This means **less electrons** (which carry photonic energy) to activate T regulator cells of both arms of immunity. You don't stop making it in Mississippi until early Jan. Where you live might be the real answer to this question.............and have nothing to do with what you currently believe. Become aware of things you might not know.........they may trump what you believe.

Notice the difference in the colors of Mississippi and Colorado.

Time to upgrade your beliefs maybe?

### kovita — 2014-10-23T15:56:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-the-vaccine-or-is-it-something-ancient.11972/post-144843>

this brings back the question, what are the best places to live? I have lived in subtropics, tropics and 4 seasons places. If I would go my guts, I would choose something matching aprox mediterranean zone - warm and sunny summers and cooler winters, but without freezing. So yearlong sun protects you in many ways by raising sulfates in your blood, cold protects by improving oxygenation and paramagnetism of your blood and seems to protect better against EMF. My experience with the life in tropics is that it is a real disease paradise. My kids were nonstop bringing new and new viral infections. Well, myself, my lyme seemed to be on halt while in tropics and blew fully after moving out of tropics. I t would be ideal to identify a place with a lot sunshine hours athorough the whole year and the same time cold winter ;-)

as I've been digesting Tensgerity 6 & 7 I've been thinking about sulfates. especially as its getting darker earlier and the sun is rising later. what coincided with this seasonal change has been the onset again of my cold/burning/tingling toes. I've been grounding as much as possible (even standing outside in the rain) and CTing and noshing raw oysters at least 3-4x/week for lunch. yet the toes are back.... your comment on the FB Quantum Health group got me thinking.... that my sulfur veggie consumption has gone down in coordination with this seasonal change. I know taking methylfolate helps my symptoms... but its not just the methylfolate...the sulfur is the key. for me I need more sulfur. and it also explains why my preferred veggies have always been kale, collards, broccoli, and cauliflower. as well as just eating the coconut flakes.... as well as trying to find a tanning salon along my commute that has a IR booth... or swing by @[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) 's to sneak into her IR Sauna....

Related Question : Viral Entry Mechanics

I found lots of papers on Heparan Sulfate and Chondroitin Sulfate as viral attachment and entry factors. It seems that the success of entry of many viruses is enhanced by Heparan Sulfate, but not by Heparan itself.

Obviously the sulfate is doing something to aid in viral entry into a cell, but I haven't found a mechanistic explanation for why this happens.

-   is there electron transfer between sulfated Heparin and a virus?
-   is H+ involved in this at all? I see many negative dangling O- in the heparan sulfate molecule below. Viral H+ bonding to these negatively charged zones?

Heparan Sulfate subunit (image from Wiki -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heparan\_sulfate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heparan_sulfate))

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-25T00:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-the-vaccine-or-is-it-something-ancient.11972/post-145003>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=144843)

Well, myself, my lyme seemed to be on halt while in tropics and blew fully after moving out of tropics. I t would be ideal to identify a place with a lot sunshine hours athorough the whole year and the same time cold winter ;-)

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=144887)
>
> My best health was in Guam. I'd go back in a heart beat if I could get DH to go... but its probably an EMF nightmare now... but all that volcanic activity from sitting off the Deepest trench in the world's got some serious nnEMF mitigation going for it...
>
> That said we moved a bit west of DC and ended up in a great location.... good water, access to fresh seafood and we still have jobs...

Reminder: 3-legged stool -- light, water, magnetism. Try to get as much of the 3 as possible.

Personally, I think a year-round 12-hr light cycle in a warmer climate gives you the most control over the environment.

Temperature modulation can be done pretty easily given today's technology. It's only a matter of being able to afford say getting the cooling equipment needed to have your own private ice bath. (or if you're Tony Robbins, you have you own cryotherapy chamber in Florida -- [http://fourhourworkweek.com/2014/10/15/money-master-the-game/](http://fourhourworkweek.com/2014/10/15/money-master-the-game/) ). You can buy ice vests, cold tubs, air-conditioning, etc ....

Sidenote: Tim Ferriss interviewed Tony Robbins in the link I just listed. Robbins' morning routine involves getting up, eating a fish and vegetable breakfast (every single day), going out to his private beach, feeling grateful, jumping into his 57F cold tub, jumping into his sauna (hot), and then mentally priming his brain for success afterward. He also does cryotherapy 3 times a week, and is perpetually on a low-carb diet. He lives off the Florida coast, so light cycles are pretty consistent. Despite his crazy travel schedule, he has managed not to break down. He's practicing some key Quantum Health principles without knowing it.

(The interview is pretty good in general BTW)​

Seafood is going to be location dependent, as would water, so we'll have to check those out based on the specific location.

Consistent light cycles allow for a more flexible diet in my mind. If you so choose, you can modulate carbs in and out of the diet so long as you manage the relative oxidation and reduction rates in your body. (we're probably designed to be more oxidised during spring and summertime. This may have implications for fertility -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/human-year-round-mating-season-implications.11016/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/human-year-round-mating-season-implications.11016/)).

One downside of living at the lower latitudes is that the natural magnetic field is weaker in general. That would not be true for regions where there are geological stressors that "open up the earth". The American gulf region is one that Jack has talked a lot about (thanks to the 65 m.y.a asteroid). I don't know how an active Volcanic region like Hawaii would fare. My guess is that the magnetic field is stronger (plus you get sulfur rich soils)

I would think Guam fits the bill, as would many of the islands in the pacific. The phillipines would also be decent.

\-----

I'm in Australia (donut hole!), I'll share my thought process when I decided to move from Melbourne to Cairns about 1.5 months ago.

Cairns is about 17 deg South of the equator compared to Melbourne at 37 deg south. The light cycle is 13hrs at the end of December, and 11hrs at the end of June, which is not too big a variation. Average temperatures range from 10-26C (40-79F) in July, to 24-32C (75-90F) in January.

I personally choose to follow a high-fat ketogenic diet year-round despite the longer light cycles of my location. While carbs should be used sometimes for the ROS burst to stimulate autophagy, I choose to use other methods of stimulating ROS production at the right time of the year. eg: chew on some nicotine gum and then go for a hypoxic bike ride during the longer light cycles, maybe just chill out with some easy swimming in the sea during the shorter light cycles (when the sea is cooler and there are no jellyfish anyway).

I like the political situation here, still being part of the Australia, and benefitting from all the infrastructure benefits that you'd get from. eg: I could get a huge cryotherapy chamber shipped to by a land courier service if I so wanted to.

It's pretty small population wise. Maybe 160,000 people spread over a 50km by 10km rectangle. Most of that population is in the center and inner south. My suburb is maybe 2km by 2km in area, and houses just under 5,000 people. Should I choose, I can get even further north to the more rural parts of the region to setup a homestead.

I like the variation in terrain. I'm living 200m from the beach. If I go 2km inland I'm in a rainforest. If I go 1km north I'm in a mangrove swamp. If I go 30km south-east I'm on the Atherton Tablelands (up to 1200m elevation with cool winters in low 40s fahrenheit / single digit celsius). Environment modulation is just another way of controlling how my brain senses the environment should I need to shock it out of something.

Queensland's seafood catch is pretty decent. It's always famous for prawns. And while it costs more, we get stuff shipped in from the rest of Australia as well. I'll still take my farmed atlantic salmon at $27/kg (thank you Roy Palmer).

Water is decent. No fluoridation in Cairns, and for 4 months of the year we get northern hemisphere water from the Indo-Australian monsoon. Can't do much about the ozone hole,

I like the fact that it has an international airport, so if I want to get to the US to meet up with Krusers in the future, it's only a 30 min tax ride and a (long) flight. Of the major East-cost cities in Australia, Brisbane is a 1hr flight away, Sydney about 2.5hrs, and Melbourne about 3.5hrs.

Hopefully some of that helps to get people thinking about how to optimise their own environment to fit the lifestyle they want

Hmm, viruses are paramagnetic?

So it seems like a "good immune response" means that your the cells responsible for the immune response are more strongly magnetic (have more electrons) than the cells that you want to protect. Viruses would be drawn to these immune cells instead of your regular cells and be destroyed.

Now it's time to study immune cell function and the types of compounds that are present in them to perform this function.

It's also time to look at the changes in unhealthy cells that make more susceptible to viral entry (or less? depending on the virus?).

yew, that is a great summary. For me there is another important point which is not having any direct health impact, but having lived in SE Asia, Middle East and central africa i know it matters to me. It is whether the culture and social acpects of the place you live in fits with your own values.I have to say, I am pretty loner and so is my husband I never look for much friends, but the place you live can squeeze you or inspire you. Again, I am my own biggest inspiration and it is me who can create flowers in the middle of the dessert, but still I reached a point where I am sure I need this place to be in Europe (ironical beacause when |I left Europe 10 years back I hoped and wished I would never come back).
Australia was always on my mind just because of what you said - places with very mild climate and long light cycles. I knew nothing about how the donut hole could affect the health that time, but it was as far as living on the moon, so we never gave it a try. I know there are similar places around the gulf, thought I would prefer Yucatan since the culture is on emotional level perfect match for me. I could easily go and live in latin america, but it is just not possible right now.

In Europe, I guess one can think only about the south. I find the best the atlantic coast because of the long light cycles and seafood and wild sea, but the magnetic part could be weak. Some islands around Italy and Italy itself could be better, it is after all what we call the cradle of the european culture and there are for sure natural reasons. we were looking into Italy as well, at this very moment this seems very complicated ;-)

### prAna303 — 2014-10-26T10:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-the-vaccine-or-is-it-something-ancient.11972/post-145198>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=145195)
>
> In Europe, I guess one can think only about the south. I find the best the atlantic coast because of the long light cycles and seafood and wild sea, but the magnetic part could be weak. Some islands around Italy and Italy itself could be better, it is after all what we call the cradle of the european culture and there are for sure natural reasons. we were looking into Italy as well, at this very moment this seems very complicated ;-)

Just my take on this because i never felt like i been in the wrong spot, never.

One of the biggest things is to avoid big places that are manmade, always search for a place where you have nature, nextdoors. If nature is 100% life, city is 0% life... Be a constant being that always connects with nature, then you adapt to that nature because we are flexible. Where i live, almost everyone is getting ill from almost no sun for five months now, i do not. People get depressed when it is dark, i feel alive and relaxed. Everyone is bulking up with comfort, i challenge myself every single day in nature.

The key is nature, you and me, we are able with good magnetic sense to live on these rules, use nature to feed us and save us. When we get connected we also feel at an instant that modern comfort is killing us, then we make changes.

I think we can find places nearby if we are willing to change how we look upon "lifestyle".

# Thread: Cold packs versus baths
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LisaLearning — 2014-10-23T15:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/post-144837>

I've been making radical improvements to my diet, and working my way into CT. I've tried a couple of baths, but I am finding that getting in a tepid bath, cooling it down enough, etc., and keeping it separated, time-wise, from my normal slightly-warm shower, is eating up a lot of time that I don't have due to some additional commuting I have to do.

So I thinking about multi-tasking. I've used several frozen cold packs on my abdomen, lower tummy and tops of thighs when I am at my desk, or sitting in the living room, and I can get a nice long exposure that way, and my skin gets cold and numb, and no signs of any damage to epidermis.

I am thinking it is not as good as cold water immersion, but I am wondering how inferior it is. Is what I am doing good enough, if I am doing it every day? I seem to remember there were comments about using the cold in order to trigger skin receptors, but then I saw comments as well about cooling near the shallowest parts of the femoral artery, which would seem to indicate systemic cooling.

Is cooling with the ice packs daily (long-term) of significant value, or am I barely making a dent compared to immersion?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-23T15:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/post-144838>

water around your skin transmits cold 24 times better than air does. Spot CT is helpful in many cases but the best way to reverse a problem or mitigate nnEMF is total immersion

### LisaLearning — 2014-10-23T15:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/post-144840>

Thanks for the quick reply, Jack.

### nicld — 2014-10-23T16:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/post-144849>

I just started doing spot CT when I don't have enough time at night to do a full bath. Like you targeting the thighs and belly. Sometimes I even do it when commuting to work but just have to be careful with the pack since the "leakproof" ones I got leak.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-24T03:41:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-packs-versus-baths.11973/post-144915>

Welcome Lisa! Glad you are making a time investment in your health.

# Thread: Skin violett at hands
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-10-23T19:02:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144863>

My skin at my knuckles is getting a violett colour when they are getting cold. Is it a sign for something? Also my knuckles get a little dry.

### Penny — 2014-10-23T19:12:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144865>

I thought people covered up their hands and head when they did CT? And they used a compression shirt? That doesn't sound too good... not an expert BTW...

### nicld — 2014-10-23T19:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144870>

My hands turn purple all the time when they get cold. I assume this is due to reducing the blood flow to the extremities to keep the heat in closer to the body. There are even times my knees seem to turn orange when cold. But I am really fair skinned and for me, is normal but not sure if it is normal

I don't usually wear a hat and gloves when doing CT.

### Butters — 2014-10-23T20:01:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144871>

I don't speak about CT Sessions. I speak of being outside in the cold. There it happens. My first thought was about the restricted blood flow too, I am not alone  ...

### kovita — 2014-10-23T21:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144878>

raynaud's syndrome? My fingers would get white sometimes when i start to feel very cold but never during CT. I have found a quick remedy in a acupressure on my feet. Must be tensegrity principle. When at home I use acupressure mat and while outside i just go barefoot and find grass lane or much better tiny stones, it works like a magic for me and even if i do it on the snow or on a very cold day, I feel warm quickly and the fingers come back to it's colour.

### nicld — 2014-10-24T03:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144913>

Oh, if it is a bit cold at work the hands turn purple there.

### Butters — 2014-10-24T13:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skin-violett-at-hands.11975/post-144942>

Yeah. I think vasoconstriction. As soon as I put them in my Pocket they turn normal

I told you hydrogen can also act as a group 7 halogen in Tensegrity 6. It means it can gain electrons to become a non metal. When hydrogen does this in water when it is associated with iodine it forms an ionic liquid. Ionic liquids are now receiving special attention in science, owing to their unique properties such as high ionic conductivity, non-volatility and non-flammability. This ability makes these fluids versatile alternatives to conventional solvent-based systems used to make batteries, fuel cells, and super capacitors that hold large charges. Fluoride blocks the ability to hold charges........and we lose energy and leak light from our mitochondria. That light is IR in thyroid mitochondrial cells.

Fluoride blocks the ability to hold charges........and we lose energy and leak light from our mitochondria. That light is IR in thyroid mitochondrial cells.

Iodine displacement of fluoride from thyroid gland is **_activated_** by IR-A morning sunrise light.

A comparative study of fluoride ingestion levels, serum thyroid hormone & TSH level derangements, dental fluorosis status among school children from endemic and non-endemic fluorosis areas - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3890436/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3890436/)

[https://pleijsalon.com/enhance-thyroid-gland-health-with-red-and-near-infrared-light-therapy/](https://pleijsalon.com/enhance-thyroid-gland-health-with-red-and-near-infrared-light-therapy/)

Thyroid Peroxidase (TPOAb) antibodies decline from near infrared therapy - [https://pleijsalon.com/enhance-thyroid-gland-health-with-red-and-near-infrared-light-therapy/](https://pleijsalon.com/enhance-thyroid-gland-health-with-red-and-near-infrared-light-therapy/)
Safety and Efficacy of Low-Level Laser Therapy in Autoimmune Thyroiditis - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6247385/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6247385/)

The _general idea_ of what "we know" is the effects of infrared light come from the interaction with at least two main photoacceptors, including cytochrome c oxidase (unit IV in the mitochondrial respiratory chain) and intracellular water. The photons dissociate inhibitory nitric oxide from the cytochrome c oxidase, which promotes increases in electron transport, mitochondrial membrane potential, and adenosine triphosphate (ATP) concentration. There are many signaling pathways are activated through Ca2+ release from intracellular stores, which increases the concentrations of cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP). These factors can induce increased expression of genes related to the synthesis or release of many molecules, such as growth factors, interleukins, cytokines, anti-apoptotic proteins, and antioxidant enzymes. These molecules are responsible for cell proliferation/differentiation, regeneration of several types of tissues, and modulation of immunological responses.

However, _fluoride toxicity_ can not be over come by IR-A light ->
A few examples:
Fluoride hazard quotient and medical geological health implications in divergent human populace -
[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-019-52812-3](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-019-52812-3)

Groundwater contamination is an enormous problem - [https://www.downtoearth.org.in/coverage/indias-groundwater-is-flooded-with-fluoride-12676](https://www.downtoearth.org.in/coverage/indias-groundwater-is-flooded-with-fluoride-12676)

As Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) has said -> It's your _zip-code_ which makes the largest environmental difference on your health's potential outcome.

In Ban Mae Toen is a small rural village in the Thai, this women has no electricity, no cell phone, no plumbing, the out-house is outside; she's a gardener. Her children experience brain damage and deaf-mutism.
[https://www.vice.com/en/article/3b74gw/a-contaminated-lake-is-poisoning-a-thai-village-855](https://www.vice.com/en/article/3b74gw/a-contaminated-lake-is-poisoning-a-thai-village-855)
The picture Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) posted at the top of this thread is the women poisoned.

_**From a Thermodynamic perpective** ->_
Enzymatic dehalogenation reactions - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5575885/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5575885/)

Glutathione _S_\-transferase-like reductive dehalogenases

An additional well-studied class of reductive dehalogenase is a glutathione _S_\-transferase (GST) superfamily protein capable of dehalogenating the toxic intermediate tetrachlorohydroquinone (TCHQ)

Halogen atoms like (fluorine, bromine) can reap havoc on our health. Dehalogenating enzymes are best known for removing halogen atoms from man-made organohalogens.

Using a thermodynamics point of view as to why interspecies hydrogen transfer -
Thermodynamic Cycle That Relates the Equilibrium and Kinetic Parameters for the Enzymic Reaction

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4477011/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4477011/)

Balancing Kinetics and Thermodynamics -> the energy to achieve a nearly thermoneutral reaction in the enzyme is about 290 kcal/mol relative to the gas-phase reaction.

[https://www.researchgate.net/publication/40795347\_Thermodynamic\_Considerations\_for\_Dehalogenation](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/40795347_Thermodynamic_Considerations_for_Dehalogenation)
The hehydrodehalogenation pathway uses nitrate or sulfate with hydrogen in reducing biochemical reactions.

A Biochemical Study of a Cataboic Reductive Dehalogenase – [https://www.research.manchester.ac.uk/portal/files/85715519/FULL\_TEXT.PDF](https://www.research.manchester.ac.uk/portal/files/85715519/FULL_TEXT.PDF)
_**NAD**_ H-driven organohalide reduction by a catabolic reductive dehalogenase

**_From a Mitochondrial Perspective ->_**
Is it possible to evaluate the productive energy potential of damaged mitochondria? - Not just for cellular survival but for recovery from debilitating environmental toxins? _like the example of fluoride.
_
Since we know the environment (zip code) has layed its impact, removal from the environment where the patient got sick maybe step one.
However, the damage is done. Scaring of system may have found a new homeostasis for survival of the human organism (patient).
Now what?
We know the mitochondria need a Thermodynamic energy boost. NAD is used multiple times at each step throughout the ATP cycle. Its depletion is most likely.
Should a physician consider NAD+ supplementation just to help the flailing beaten Citric Cycles' respiration?

Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) says,

"Normally K+ is more polarizable than water, so polarization is added to the Coulomb energy in a cell. But, when fluoride or bromide are added to a cell for any reason, dielectric collapse occurs. This is precisely how the dielectric constant in water is destroyed in “floxed patients”.

The reality is that life is really a continuum of physics built upon the sliding scale of geometry on our respiratory proteins, to create amazing diversity. Ling was the first to realize K+ ions were linked water chemistry and eventually to the ATPase. This is the sliding scale a “floxed patient” needs to pay attention too. When the ATPase slows proteins remain unfolded and water cannot bind to them to transfer energy throughout the cell.

So when you have altered poor K+ ion and you’re dehydrated by our modern world, your cell’s interior becomes pseudo-hypoxic and NAD+ levels drop. This cause mitochondria to swell and calcium homeostasis is lost. Cells begin to leak electrons and light in the form of ELF-UV light.

This causes a redox shift of the mitochondria cytochromes and increases the distance between the respiratory proteins. In other words, your mitochondria down shift electron tunneling speeds, by slowing their ECT flow, to protect itself from short-circuiting.

They do this because of the lack of electrons and water. This is why ketosis is a partial fix in “floxed” patients. This becomes energy costly to a cell.

Every stressed cell releases ELF-UV light. When this happens chronically in many tissues sequentially because of how drugs are prescribed your cells that need energy are starving for it.​

[https://www.myquinstory.info/dr-jack-kruse-talks-about-fluoroquinolone-toxicity/](https://www.myquinstory.info/dr-jack-kruse-talks-about-fluoroquinolone-toxicity/)​

_
Bottomline: How are you hydrating yourself?_

### John Schumacher — 2021-09-16T20:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-fluoride-does-to-the-thyroid-gland-in-pictures.11977/post-303808>

Fluoride Exposure Induces Inhibition of Sodium-and Potassium-Activated Adenosine Triphosphatase (Na+, K+-ATPase) Enzyme Activity -

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6518254/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6518254/)​

Schematic representation of the molecular mechanisms and biological pathways by which fluoride inhibits Na+, K+-ATPase activity on plasma membranes and in thyroid follicular cells.

Key molecular mechanisms by which fluoride inhibits Na+, K+-ATPase activity. Arrows refer to increases (↑) or decreases (↓) in regulation or expression by fluoride.

Abbreviations: NKA: Na+, K+-ATPase; PKC: Protein kinase C; ATP: Adenosine-triphosphate; cAMP: cyclic adenosine-monophosphate monophosphate; Cn: Calcineurin; CaM: Calmodulin; Mn2+: Magnesium; cGMP: Cyclic guanosine monophosphate; ENO1: Enolase; NO: Nitric oxide; Pi: Inorganic phosphate; ALP: Alkaline phosphatase; TGF-β1: Transforming growth factor β 1; CT: Calcitonin; DA: Dopamine; PTH: Parathyroid hormone; PLA2: Phospholipase A2; AA: Arachidonic Acid; Prostaglandin E2; BgL: Blood glucose; Glc: Glucose; RAGE: Receptors for advanced glycation end products; OC: Osteocalcin; DA: Dopamine; PTH: Parathyroid hormone; INS: Insulin;. TSH: Thyroid stimulating hormone; TRH: Thyroid-releasing hormone.

F− inhibition of NKA activity is complex and multifactorial. In summary, evidence is provided to show that activation of PKC, cAMP, cGMP, NO, Pi, PLA2, AA and PGE2 inhibit NKA activity and that F− upregulates PKC, cAMP, cGMP, NO, PLA2, AA, PGE2 and enhances Pi levels in systemic circulation. Furthermore, evidence is presented to show that Cn regulates NKA activity and that F− inhibits CN activity, in part, by regulating the bioavailability of manganese and Pi, as well as by altering the phosphorylation activity of CaM. In addition, evidence is provided to show that F− enhances the activation of adenylate cyclase by CaM, leading to increased levels of cAMP and further evidence is provided to show that F− inhibits the activation of phosphodiesterase by CaM, which is responsible for the breakdown of cAMP.

Further evidence is presented to show that NKA activity is inhibited by dopamine, PTH and glucose. In addition, evidence is presented demonstrating that F− upregulates dopamine, glucose and PTH activity. Further analysis reveals that the mechanism of dopamine inhibition of NKA is via increased production of cAMP. It has also been elucidated in this study that NKA inhibition by PTH and hyperglycaemia is regulated by activation of PLA2, AA and PGE2. In addition, there is evidence demonstrating that stimulation of AGEs inhibits NKA activity and that F− inhibition of enolase results in the formation of AGEs. Again, the mechanistic pathway by which AGEs inhibit NKA enzyme activity is via activation of the PLA2 pathway leading to enhanced expression of AA and PGE2.​

As highlighted above, there is also an association between hypothyroidism and loss of NKA activity, suggesting a negative feedback mechanism that may further decrease enzyme activity. As elucidated in this current study, this mechanism appears to be driven by **_increased TRH secretion and DA release_** which leads to inhibition of enzyme activity.

# Thread: Could I get away with not eating as much seafood in this situation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-i-get-away-with-not-eating-as-much-seafood-in-this-situation.11981/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Self Actualize — 2014-10-24T17:18:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-i-get-away-with-not-eating-as-much-seafood-in-this-situation.11981/post-144950>

So I just had a brilliant idea.

I'm currently living in Toronto, Ontario, aka an EMF hellhole. However, my family owns a cabin in Northwestern Ontario which is two hours away from where they all live.

I'm thinking about eventually moving there and living year round. Working a 9-5 job has never appealed to me and I've recently developed an interest in self-sustainable living. I haven't pursued it very much because of my sub-optimal health and mental health make everything without a instant gratifying award hard to motivate myself towards.

This area has a lot of pros in that it's near a lot of lakes with great freshwater fishing, hunting, I can garden, ground myself to the earth, it gets really cold in the winters so cold thermogenesis etc etc.

The only issue, which may not even be an issue in this situation, is that there will be less access to seafood. My question is, will my living situation offset the need for a really high DHA component in the diet?

If so, I could always drive into Winnipeg, Manitoba which is two hours away and purchase seafood if needed.

Could I get by with Wild game and freshwater fish and shellfish? (deer, duck, walleye, pike, bass etc, mussels and crayfish?)

I'm thinking I can but I would love some more educated insight.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-i-get-away-with-not-eating-as-much-seafood-in-this-situation.11981/post-144951>

# Thread: Pushing the envelope: Quantum-Astro Philosophy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Konish — 2014-10-25T01:28:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145017>

Hi Jack and All,

Maybe stretching the construct of the forum with these two questions!

Once aspect of delving into the quantum world is the ability to question why do we need optimal well-being in the first place.

1\. What's your perspective of the purpose of evolving conciousness i.e. what is the human race moving towards over the next 1000 years?
2\. From your accumulation of knowledge how to do you think the universe was born? i.e: something from nothing

LOL if possible a response in English not Math

### Konish — 2014-10-25T01:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145018>

I'm aware people spend there entire lives searching for the above answers!

### Da-mo — 2014-10-25T01:57:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145020>

Could it be that optimal wellbeing sets the stage for evolutionary innovation?

> Damo (Tat Choon)
> Damo introduced Qi-Gong exercises to the Chinese monks and it is from Damo that the breathing techniques of Wuzu are derived. An Indian monk who travelled from India to Guangzhou (Canton) in 520, and thence by foot to Song Mountain Henan, where he settled in the Shaolin Temple (then hardly a century old): Damo (Bodhidarma) was the 28th Patriarch of Buddhism and the First Patriarch of Chan (Zen) Buddhism.
> At the Temple he found the monks in poor health, hardly able to carry out their important spiritual duties. At the end of his famous nine year long meditation (carried out in a cave to the rear of the Temple) he realised that his long period of sitting had atrophied his leg muscles to the point of immobility. He sought a remedy to this problem (and fortuitously to that of the health condition of his fellow monks as well) through specially devised breath control exercises, combined with meditation (mind-intent).
> Through these excercises his mobility was progressively regained and his vitality greatly increased, for he had generated and directed his qi through the qi-gong exercises he had discovered. These, he taught the monks, who were not only swiftly restored to good health and gained great powers of concentration, but also found aspects of the system could be used in self-defence.
> Damo is reputedly the creator of the 18 Lohan Hand Forms. He pursued his travels once again having taught the monks Ch’an Buddhism. He inspired the writing of two famous books, The ‘I Chin Ching/ I Jin Jing’ (The Sinew Change Classic), and the ‘Sui King Chin/Sui King Jin’ (Washing Marrow Classic): both teach the meditation and respiratory techniques for developing the Qi and hand movements.

[http://www.qi-chinamartialarts.com/damo.html](http://www.qi-chinamartialarts.com/damo.html)

### shah78 — 2014-10-25T02:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145022>

answer #1 Humans will do in the next 1000 much the same as did for the last 1000 years. Google: Monty Python, Life of Bryan answer #2 The Buddha told his followers not to bother with such questions. Though not "technically" a Buddhist, I follow the Buddha's teachings on this subject. It is not a subject fit for human contemplation.......... It will lead to eating low -fat diets!

### Da-mo — 2014-10-25T02:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145025>

The search for meaning is an emergent property of a consciousness that has evolved language and the ability to think in the abstract. (see "The Hidden Connections" - Fritjof Capra")

This ability to imagine and plan helped us survive against more deadly predators - we could imagine the danger in the water or in the tall grass and plan scenarios of how to mitigate them. Today there generally aren't those sorts of dangers in most of our lives but that part of the mind continues to create fear and doubt - largely around the issues of social acceptance or rejection (which does still tie back to survival in the tribal sense and in the herd sense).

It seems the mind/brain prioritises stimulii via DIPI - dangerous, important, pleasurable, interesting. This is a priority list linked with survival - as is the tenedency to try to attach meaning (purpose) to stimulii so that scenarios can be created in abstract thought which will enable us to react in appropriate ways.

Attempting to attach meaning to abstract concepts can be thought of as building illusions (as in life is an illusion) or visionary if others agree with you. Hence imagination can be our most powerful gift and/or our downfall.

I think of conciousness in fractal terms - in the same way one of our cells is not aware of our larger being in the same way we are - perhaps we are not aware of some larger existence we are intrinsic to in the way that existence might percieve. Perhaps we are the mitochondria powering something on a different scale - hence if we start breaking bad . . . . maybe that is as good a reason as any to move towards optimal - we send and recieve and interpret signalling more accurately, efficiently and appropriately.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-25T15:22:32+0300

Today's reality is a construct of the parts and bit we have and know. We are designed to collect and assimilate electrons to increase our electric charge and magnetic sense. The purpose is to control the flow of light to the proper levels to un condense our matter in order to see many hidden truths in front of our eyes. The more we collect and touch the more clear our vision becomes so that we can innovate from there. Consciousness is just a stream of electrons. It is our job to put that stream to uses. In my view optimal comes from using that said stream to make your mitochondria a perfect self sustaining nuclear reactor. And I mean this literally and figuratively as you will eventually find out. I have already hinted that the sun and a mitochondria serve exactly the same purpose..........and they do. Consciousness is the solar wind that connect the sun to the mitochondria. By itself it tells you nothing. But when it is connected from sun to Earth all energies and information are transferred giving you a constant steady stream with which you can innovate, create, emerge.

The philosophy of man for the next 1000 years should be to persevere what we have done to our blue dot. Right now, I am painting a bleak picture, yet, I am intensely optimistic for our species. We have the power to destroy nature............but we also have this amazing ability to change transform and collectively come together to do amazing things when we choose too. For the last 5000 yrs man has slowly come together. We call it civilization. It has brought many benefits but todays's society and culture is tearing us apart from one another. Civilization is now about interfacing with machines and not people; This inadvertently is forcing us to withdraw from one another and disconnect from nature. When we allow this consistently, we become sick and die sooner. Man must innovate that solution the next 1000 years. Man must fight for it and persevere.

We are all fighting odds, swimming upstream. It's the evolutionary game plan. Success is not where we are now, it's what we went through to get where we're at. It does not matter how slowly you go on the path to optimal, as long as you do not stop. Things always seem impossible until they are completed. We must never give in or up in life, you persevere it. You mustn't confuse a single defeat with a final defeat ever. Don't shelter your life from dangers; become fearless in facing them. Continuous effort is how we build our successes and unlock our potential.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-26T04:02:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145185>

Photorepair is about specific frequency of light causing amazing regeneration and healing. Becker found some amazing things about the neuroepithelial junction. He just never linked it all together. That frequency can stimulate RNA to make proteins to repair all the damage by turning differentiated cells to multipotent cells to rebuild missing parts. Mammals have lost this ability because they can't generate enough DC current in the proper time frame of wound healing. They use their excess DC current in the massive brains. This is a trade off salamanders and flatworms do not face. The two places it still works in us is finger tips replacement and in bone regeneration. In both cases........RBC are the key yet again.
There is a deep reason in utero human surgery leaves no scars on the fetus.........fetal RBC's have some nucleus left and the UV light can activate the photorepair mechanism...........adult RBC's have no nuclei or mitochondria.........so they can't use it.......and scars are the result.
You need skin to transform the light signal to a specific voltage to get the effect.

It is well known from biological laboratory experiments that if you blast a cell with UV light so that 99 per cent of the cell, including its DNA, is destroyed, you can almost entirely repair the damage in a **single day** just by illuminating the cell with the same wavelength but at a much weaker intensity. To this day, scientists don't understand this phenomenon, called _photorepair_, but no one has disputed it exists. I believe Becker found it in salamanders. You need the skin to transmute the UV light to affect the injured tissue. You simultaneously need the DC electric current outside of axons but below the myelin layer to electrify the tissues back to health.

Fritz Popp took the science deep. He also knew that patients with _xeroderma pigmentosum_ \[**right**\] eventually die of skin cancer because their _photorepair_ system can't repair sun damage. He was also struck by the fact that _photorepair_ works most efficiently at 380 nm -- the same frequency that the cancer-causing compounds react to and scramble. That frequency corresponds the a specific voltage that repairs cells found in Becker's work.

I spoke about light in electromagnetic coupling in the blog long ago. I even mentioned the names Cosic and Veljkovic. Both of their experiments show light frequencies drive biochemistry by electric processes. We live in an electric universe and no one seems to want to accept it. What their work showed us is that somehow each molecule sends out a unique electromagnetic field. That field is imprinted in cell water and this creates a quantum sense or memory. The field can begin to "sense" the field of the complimentary molecule in this fashion. It's as if there is a " quantum electrochemical dance" in the cellular terroir and the molecules move to the rythmn. The music is supplied by the biophoton or light in the system. Light is the key.

**"Veljkovic and Cosic proposed that molecular interactions are electrical in nature, and they take place over distances that are large compared with the size of molecules. Cosic later introduced the idea of dynamic electromagnetic field interactions, that molecules recognize their particular targets and vice versa by electromagnetic resonance. In other words, the molecules send out specific frequencies of electromagnetic waves which not only enable them to 'see' and 'hear' each other, as both photon and phonon modes exist for electromagnetic waves, but also to influence each other at a distance and become ineluctably drawn to each other if vibrating out of phase (in a complementary way)."** -- The Real Bioinformatics Revolution: Proteins and Nucleic Acids Singing to One Another?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-27T17:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145310>

Excessive exposure to the lowest wavelengths of UV light, also called UV-C, (180-290 nm) can cause damage to the cornea as well as the lens. These wavelengths are not common in nature, since they are absorbed by the upper atmosphere.

They but are present in in some industrial environments, such as electric arch welding. The mid UV wavelengths, called UV-B, (290-320 nm) can cause damage to the lens as well as cause welders eye (feels like sand in the eye). Mid UV light is present both in sunlight and in some industrial environments. The _high UV wavelengths_, also called **UV-A, (320-380 nm)** are present in **all outdoor environments**. Excessive exposure of UV-A can cause fatigue or snow blindness. they key is 320-379 nm wavelengths cause problems...........but 380 nm is quite special

Foods emit biophotons........and this light is paid attention to by your gut enterocytes. This alters their Vitamin A and D3 sulfates cycles. People need to read Popp's work carefully. Quorum sensing is quite complicated because it is a fiberoptic/quantum opticophotonics/ condensed matter process between prokaryotes that emit light and eukaryotes that can change that signal into an electrical response.

Popp also showed in detail in his research that the light emissions of healthy people follow a set biological rhythm for day and night and also by week and month, as though they are connected to biorhythms of the earth, as well. This emission is tied to the flora and also the biophotons in foods. GMO foods all have lower biophotons emissions. People don't seem to realize it. Man made processed foods do as well. This is what simplifies the gut flora and in size and species diversity. Then we have introduced the process of FIAF.

This is what causes us to get fat and alter the Vitamin A and D3 sulfated levels are altered in gut enterocytes and the GALT right underneath the brush border. Those electrons are what activate or deactivate the T regulator cells that affect both arms of the immune system.

>

Quorum sensing is quite complicated because it is a fiberoptic/quantum opticophotonics/ condensed matter process between prokaryotes that emit light and eukaryotes that can change that signal into an electrical response.
>

This emission is tied to the flora and also the biophotons in foods. GMO foods all have lower biophotons emissions. People don't seem to realize it. Man made processed foods do as well.
