We all need to remember at ground level sunlight is 44% visible light, 3% ultraviolet with the sun at its zenith (highest point in sky), and the remainder is all infrared light. This is why water and IR magic is key. The Earth's atmosphere blocks about 77% of the sun's UV, almost entirely in the shorter UV wavelengths, when the Sun is at its zenith. Of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the Earth's surface, more than 95% is the longer wavelengths of UVA, with the small remainder UVB. The fraction of UVB which remains in UV light after passing through the atmosphere is heavily dependent on cloud cover and atmospheric conditions. This means it fluctuates huge for the people and animals below. This should also make you realize that life also uses very specific parts of the spectrum of light to work its quantum magic. But UV light in placental mammals has a very bizarre twist. Photolyase absorbs blue light and employs the energy to remove UV-induced DNA damage, cyclobutane pyrimidine dimers, or pyrimidine pyrimidone lesions. These enzymes have been found in many living organisms ranging from bacteria to aplacental mammals, but their photoreactivation effect, such as survival increase of UV-irradiated cells by light-illumination, has not been identified in eutherian placental mammals, including humans.

CITES:
1\. [http://www.newport.com/Introduction-to-Solar-Radiation/411919/1033/content.aspx2](http://www.newport.com/Introduction-to-Solar-Radiation/411919/1033/content.aspx2). [http://rredc.nrel.gov/solar/spectra/am1.5/](http://rredc.nrel.gov/solar/spectra/am1.5/)
3.[http://www.skincancer.org/prevention/uva-and-uvb/understanding-uva-and-uvb](http://www.skincancer.org/prevention/uva-and-uvb/understanding-uva-and-uvb)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T01:25:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145359>

No one seems to know what makes chemicals carcinogenic and others benign, even when they are chemically similar. In 1970, Fritz-Albert Popp, a German theoretical biophysicist, discovered that benzo(a)pyrene, (a potent carcinogen) , absorbs ultraviolet light at one specific wavelength and emits it at another. Yet, benzo\[e\]pyrene,a benign polycyclic hydrocarbon known to be one of the most lethal carcinogens to humans, and its twin (save for one small alteration in its atomic molecular makeup). Benzo\[e\]pyrene is nearly identical twin to benzo\[a\]pyrene, absorbs and reemits the same light at its original wavelength. They key Popp found was in the atomic lattice and how light was altered by the atoms. Small changes in the action of light lead to massive changes in observed reality. Again, we see thermodynamics entering the picture via E=mc2. Popp tested 37 different chemicals in total. The carcinogens "scrambled" light with a wavelength of 380 nanometers. The benign chemicals did not.

Popp later learned that 380 nanometers, the wavelength altered by carcinogens, is also the wavelength that cells prefer to use to photorepair themselves. After exposure to intense UV light, cells quickly self-repair when they are exposed to very weak UV light, particularly that with a wavelength of 380 nanometers. Popp hypothesized that cancer results from a disruption of cells' photorepair system for some atomic reason. **_His hypothesis raised a very fundamental question:_** what in the body produced this very weak light that powered the repair system? We still do not know.........I have a hunch it maybe a release of this 380 nm light at night from specific parts of the mitochondrial cytochromes when FFA are broken down to electrons while we liberate CO2 and make water to surround the mitochondria during autophagy.

Popp and his student Bernard Ruth found that all living systems store light energy (photons) acquired from the sun and from plants consumed as food (photosynthesis), in nucleic acids like DNA. Ironically, he also found that prokaryotes release way more light than eukaryotes but he never tested to see of mitochondrial DNA holds more light than eukaryotic nuclear DNA. I have a big hunch it does and this is why we have two mitochondrial membranes to hold massive electric and magnetic charges to contain that light.

When we consume vegetables, for example, and digest them to electrons in mitochondria, it is metabolized into carbon dioxide (CO2) and water. On the back of the electrons from the vegetables comes seasonal information of the the light used to make these plants grow. Mitochondria have a way to harvest light very similar to the reaction centers in plants. Electrons are very capable of being excited by the sun's photons and we then store the light photons from the sun while letting the "harvested electrons" pass on to reduce oxygen. During this process we extract the CO2 and use the water in many ways, but the light, an EM wave, must be stored by the cell.

Link here to get that story:[http://www.theguardian.com/science/2014/oct/26/youre-powered-by-quantum-mechanics-biology](http://www.theguardian.com/science/2014/oct/26/youre-powered-by-quantum-mechanics-biology)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T01:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145361>

Popp chose to work specifically with UV light because of the experiments of a Russian biologist named Alexander Gurwitsch who, while working with onions (loaded with sulfur) in 1923, discovered that roots could stimulate a neighboring plant's roots if the two adjacent plants were in quartz ( a piezoelectric crystal) glass pots but not if they were in silicon (not piezoelectric) glass pots. The only difference being that the silicon filtered UV wavelengths of light while the quartz did not. Gurwitsch theorized that onion roots could communicate with each other by ultraviolet light.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T01:43:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145362>

Might we do the same?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T01:58:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145363>

So how does a mitochondria recapitulate the sun?

It uses the photoelectric effect. The only two things that can contain light is strong electric and magnetic fields.

Electrons taken in by the body by any means, becomes the the energy currency of life. It is on the back of these electrons that photons are carried to mitochondria. The photons are released and dissipated in a very precise why controlled by nucleic conductors and they become distributed over piezoelectric and superconducting cables in cells. They span the entire spectrum of electromagnetic frequencies, from the lowest to the highest in the spectrum of light energies.

Dr. Veljko Veljkovic who heads the Center for Multidisciplinary Research and Engineering, Institute of Nuclear Sciences Vinca thinks we are beings of light who connect frequencies of light in biologic fiberoptic cables. I agree with her. I think this is how the human brain works at a fundamental level.

Popp showed in his experiments that these weak light emissions were sufficient to orchestrate the body's repairs. They key metric in this idea is the ability to turn light into an electric signal in the brain that then can be changed to an electrochemical signal and back to light or another signal. DHA is the only lipid to do it. I have a sense sulfated D3 and DHA with Vitamin A select for a specific protein in our cell membranes that activate the 380 nm photorepair system. Without that specific protein.......I think cancer becomes more likely. What chemical in the lipid raft do I think is the main target of UV light communication and photorepair? Sulfated cholesterol. Why? You need sulfated D3 to make sulfate cholesterol........and sulfated D3 only comes from UVB sunlight. It also explains why low cholesterol levels are constantly associated with oncogenesis when it is studied. The mitotic spindles cannot work properly without a sulfated cholesterol level over 200. Mitotic spindles are associated with nucleic acids.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T03:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145374>

Now for the next level jump in wisdom........When subatomic particles whiz around in spacetime or within a mitochondria, very close to the speed of light, they live a longer existence both in a particle accelerator and in you. This is how time is created, but it must merge with space, to become one. When you separate the idea of space and time it is like watching a play by only looking at the shadows cast by the spotlights on the stage. The real business of life involves 3D actors moving around within the light, while shadows only capture the 2 D projection of the play. When you see this vision merge in your mind, you become able to lift your eyes from the shadows of biology to the light of quantum biophysics.

### DavidL — 2014-10-28T03:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145381>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=145362)
>
> Might we do the same?

Does this explain how Reiki works?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T04:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145384>

It explains a lot more. It is how all things communicate in life.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T04:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145387>

The energy in the mass of one single H+ proton approaches 1 billion times what is liberated in a biochemical reaction. The problem is it is hard to destroy proton mass to get that energy. In fact, we see Kleiber's law show up in the sun compared to mitochondria. The sun is several thousand times less efficient than the human body at converting mass to energy. One kilogram of the sun generates only 1/5000th of a watt of power on average, whereas your mitochondria typically generates usually more than 1 watt per kilogram. The sun is much bigger than our mitochondria which makes up for this inefficiency. This is the basis of Kleiber's law. This is why Elephants are bigger than mice, but it is also why bright hot stars die sooner than cool dull ones. It is also why sick mitochondria leak more light than mitochondria that are well. Here Kleber's law crashes into Dr. Fritz Popp's work.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-28T04:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pushing-the-envelope-quantum-astro-philosophy.11985/post-145388>

Nature is symmetrical, but life is not............

aloha all,
i had some labs done on 10/4. cycle day 20. (i have also been suffering from a sinus infection since july, which now appears-fingers crossed-to be 99% gone. so, i think that chronic infection influenced my labs.)
any thoughts hugely appreciated.

HgBA1C 5.1
Bun 9
Creatinine .61
ratio= 14.8:1
GFR >60
Total Iron 123
TIBC 352
RBC 3.95 3.9-4.5
WBC 13.8 5.0-7.5
Hemoglobin 12.7 13.5-14.5
Hematocrit 37.4 37-44
Ferritin 18
MCV 94.6 82-89.9
MCH 32.1 28-31.9
MCHC 33.9 32-35
Platelets 376 (150-385x1000)
RDW 12.7 <13
Neutrophils 81 40-60%
Lymphs 14 24-44%
Monocytes 1 0-7%
Eosinophils 4 0-3%
Basophils 0 0-1%
Free T4 .7
FreeT3 3.1
TSH 2.95
RT3 19
Thyroid peroxidase Ab 40
DHEA sulfate 118
Estriol, serum <0.10
Progesterone 16.25 (luteal phase ref range 1.55-27.91)
Estrone 106 (luteal phase ref range 16-173)
Estradiol 169 (luteal phase ref range 49-291)

so...bun/creatinine ratio is less than optimal
ferritin is in the toilet

help!!

looks like i need more b12. more oysters?!

interesting this is i was supplementing w/iodine prior to this...i then decreased my dosage and BAM, sick w/sinus infection and now this.

### Rain — 2014-10-26T08:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-antibiotic-resistant-skin-infection.11993/post-145194>

Jack if you see this, or anybody else want to chime in.... i need help fast

here's what happened, i had such bad candida that i ordered threelac, i ended up in bed for days, i could do nothing but sleep, with sporadic waking for the needful things

i broke out in a HORRIBLE skin rash, first itching, then BAD BURNING, then PAIN, then all three

Penny wrote me the following in my other thread to point out that it is a bad skin infection, but i have been flox'd and can't take strong antibiotics, and i don't know what to do? JACK, QUANTUM LEVEL TREATMENT QUICKLY PLEEEEEASE????

ok Penny wrote

**Verified Purchase**([What's this?](http://www.amazon.com/gp/community-help/amazon-verified-purchase/175-5491908-7031623))
**This review is from: Threelac Probiotic Dietary Supplement, Natural Lemon Flavor, Includes 60 .053-Ounce Packets (Health and Beauty)**
I just stopped taking ThreeLac because I developed a severe skin infection with a burning rash. At first I did not connect the ThreeLac to the rash. After several days it became worse and I saw a doctor who prescribed anti-biotics for the skin infection. I stopped taking the ThreeLac since I was now taking anti-biotics and decided to do an internet search for other probiotics that I could take while on the antibiotic. I came across a Holistic health care provider's website where she described her reaction to ThreeLac and many other people left comments about their experiences...most negative. I was surprised since I had read so many positive reviews and I thought that probiotics could do no harm - they would either help or do nothing so I thought. After reading through those experiences I felt weak and panicky because I realized that all my current symptoms were due to the release of a pathogenic bacteria into my bloodstream which is the last ingredient in ThreeLac: enterococcus faecalis. It is a bacteria that normally resides in our intestines, but if you have too much, of have leaky gut syndrome (as most Candida sufferers do), it can enter you bloodstream and cause all kinds of infections. Many people reported getting urinary tract infections which led to kidney infections, and most people reported feeling weak, tired, dizzy, and having other related problems. I too feel sick and weak and horrible with this rash - all my symptoms meet the those of an infection with enterococcus faecalis.

I am not sure if ThreeLac is a complete scam or just a very dangerous "nutritional" supplement that affects people in extreme ways - either very good or very bad. Either way, please do your research before trying it because it's better to be safe than sorry. I am sorry. I believed all the positive reviews and let down my "scam sniffing" guard because I did not think a pro-biotic could cause me harm. I have read that Australia and Canada do not allow this bacteria into food products because it is extremely resistant to antibiotics and pathogenic to some people. Most ironic that the packages say "Pure Natural Food" when it feels like i just unknowingly poisoned myself and may have to spend lots of money getting treatment since I am uninsured and may be waiting too long get proper medical help. Please do not give this to your children without trying it for yourself first. I read some sad experiences of well-intentioned parents giving it to kids with awful side effects.

I am heading back to the tried-and-true natural candida remedies that I used over ten years ago and worked: Caprylic acid, GOOD probiotics (that are legal in other countries with better oversight than our FDA), and and a Candida diet.

From Penny:

You need to get to a doctor pronto and get some vancomycin - if you add ginger juice when you take the vancomycin, it will help the resistance factor of the microbe to the vancomycin... this is from Steven Buhner's Herbal Antibiotics book... he gives a list of tinctures you could use, but screw that, get to a doctor - this bug can affect your heart...

Do not \*ever\* use Tylenide as it can deplete your glutathione... your body's main antioxidant - baking soda or calamine lotion might be helpful - any kind of clay might help also - according to the reviewer, you might go see your doctor and get some antibiotics...

I know it costs $100.00 but if you gave a stool sample to American Gut, they would give you the DNA of your gut flora and you could see what is actually killing you - at least bacteria-wise... the little buggers never travel alone...

[http://americangut.org/](http://americangut.org/)

But please, take the package of threelac to a doctor and have him test your blood for e. faecalis... this is quite serious...

### Linz — 2014-10-26T12:59:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-antibiotic-resistant-skin-infection.11993/post-145210>

Rain,
Some years ago I had a very bad rash/blistering skin that eventually required intravenous antibiotics for an infection and it was a frightening experience where none of the medics seemed to know what to do (seven of them!) It was before I modified my diet and I already had severe leaky gut/difficult to control auto-immunity and almost certain Vit A/D problem. I think a strong (medically approved and trialled) probiotic was probably the final trigger for the skin problem. The hospital treatment stopped the infection quickly but not the rash. At that point, feeling abandoned by the medical profession, I discovered a grain-free. sugar-free, high fat diet and the rash evaporated in days although hot itchy spots returned at the slightest provocation for some time. I still take very great care with manufactured probiotics (not so much natural sauerkraut,, kefir etc) and think even the best of them can cause imbalances in the wrong circumstances.

Some years later with greatly improved health and understanding of what I am doing ( and a heartfelt thanks to Dr K) , I have tried a few probiotics very cautiously - Mutaflor, Lactobacillus Plantarum and another ill-fated trial of VSL3 (rapid nausea and itchiness) - but I haven't seen much if any improvement and I'm very suspicious that they can sometimes cause more harm than good. Penny's explanation of the risks with leaky gut sounds plausible to me. Also good advice from Lah on the iodine. You will get plenty on an epi-paleo diet especially if you include seaweeds in your bone/ fishbroths and there are many warnings about the dangers of supplementing here.

Don't delay dealing with an infection, I'm sure one of the amazing people here will give you help with the immediate advice you may need on infection and you have already discovered that this is the most amazing place to deal with sorting out the underlying chaos.

Not helpful for your immediate crisis but I am beginning to think the only really effective way to use probiotics is faecal transplant which I think needs extensive testing of existing gut mo's and using an anaerobic process. Dr K does talk about re-engineering our own gut using raw fish, chitin, not over-chewing food and careful light exposure but I have no idea what would be the best approach for you with your digestive system problems. One supplement that might be worth looking at is vitamin K (esp mark IV). Poor supply in modern diets and otherwise obtained in the gut by good mo's. It helps put calcium in the right place - not sure if it helps prevent efflux though.

Really hope you start feeling better soon

### Justfourme — 2014-10-26T18:51:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-antibiotic-resistant-skin-infection.11993/post-145220>

With the severity of your rash, sounds like you may need to see a doc. I would advise against taking a probiotic with a compromised immune system and leaky gut while on an antibiotic. Antibiotics kill both good and bad bacteria in the gut. Instead of using a probiotic, use a prebiotic - that supports the good bacteria when taking meds. Once you are better and your leaky gut begins to heal, you can switch over to a probiotic and continue to take them.

# Thread: Protocoll for NST/BAT activation more cold restistance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protocoll-for-nst-bat-activation-more-cold-restistance.11994/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2014-10-26T12:26:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protocoll-for-nst-bat-activation-more-cold-restistance.11994/post-145208>

I'm trying to improve my tolerance to cold for a while since end of summer now. Everyday I do sessions with cold packs on my body for min. 1hr at shiver-intensity as well as using cold showers in the morning, sometimes cold baths but not regularly and dressing like summer. I know the CT-protocoll from Jacks site.
It seems to work slowly but how I know if I am cold adapted and what could I else do for massive BAT formation and activation? What shows me I am radiating heat? Jack says it doesn't take so long to cold adapt, but what does it really mean?

I often feel cold and shiver at 6 to 12°C when wearing shorts and T-shirt. I know it is maybe not a must but I want the peak of cold adaptation min. one time in my life to know how it is to not fear the cold and radiating heat in a massive amount. Or is it simply unrealistic? From a evolutionary standpoint I think its possible because somehow mankind had to survive in extrem temperatures without massive insulation gear
I also read studies about BAT-formation but they are only talking about minimal lower temperatures like 15-17°C and not as low as I want it.

I try to eat a high protein/fat diet direction keto with massive fish and DHA. What supplements could I also take to improve my BAT formation/activation?

### Da-mo — 2014-10-26T18:56:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protocoll-for-nst-bat-activation-more-cold-restistance.11994/post-145223>

Bitter melon extract is mentioned in the protocol.

> In the beginning of my adaptation, I also used [Bitter Melon extract](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000RE4CSK/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B000RE4CSK&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) to cold adapt. The reason is that bitter melon appears to be quite effective at causing formation of BAT from WAT especially in T2D or those with metabolic syndrome. No one knows why it really works. However, I believe it is has to do with the simultaneous loss of adiponectin and leptin from fat cells with the simultaneous induction of [Irisin](http://blogs.scientificamerican.com/observations/2012/01/11/newly-discovered-hormone-boosts-effects-of-exercise-could-help-fend-off-diabetes/) from the cold stimulus on the skin and subcutaneous fat.

[http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

### Butters — 2014-10-27T09:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protocoll-for-nst-bat-activation-more-cold-restistance.11994/post-145267>

Last evening I added some chilli and pepper to the meal and got pretty warm face/ears, my girlfriend mentioned I was radiating heat. Is that a good idea as well or could it be a coincidence? It wasn't so much, because not hot at all.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-27T09:58:45+0200

> Looks as though gravity took hold because it could be observed and measured in the 1700's and now we can observe and measure other things "below the surface" so to speak....Amazing how long science gets stuck on a model and or level.....

Electric Gravity

Paraphrased excerpts/notes (the video is a critique of an article about worm holes between 2 black holes):

All the electric dipoles in protons and neutrons line up to produce Gravity

The direct electric force between particles occurs INSTANTLY .... "entanglement" is just an electric resonance between 2 subatomic particles, each compromised of an identical system of orbiting charged subparticles (an atom within an atom) regardless of their relative movement or distance apart.

This model, which separates the EM waves in the medium from the direct longitudinal connection between all matter in the universe (electrical connection) opens up new insights for physicists (_TYW: understatement of the last 2 centuries_ )

Black holes are a mathematical mistake equivalent to trying to divide by zero. Using gravity, which is a billion billion billion times weaker than the EM force (close to zero), to explain the concentrated forces in the middle of galaxies, and calling them black holes, is bound to give you answers that make no physical sense.

The wormholes are a geometric construct in more than 3 dimensions, so they have nothing to do with the real space that physicists and engineers have to deal with.

The laws of gravity are not fundamental, but arise from quantum entanglement.

Modern physics is in the business of inventing new particles and concepts in the hopes of winning a nobel prize.

Mathematics use dimensions to represent degrees of freedom. Time has no direction (it is not a dimensino). Joining space and time has no significance. So you cannot produce physical wormholes in space.
​

I REALLY need to start going through all their videos

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-29T22:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145569>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=145552)
>
> Nice video. Thornhill is an interesting guy and has significant disagreements with Einstein, BTW. As did Tesla, whose quote:
>
> Jack has been paraphrasing a lot lately. When this discussion is all said and done, I'm not sure that even Einstein's theories will be any more safe from scrutiny and revision than everyone else...

Ha, I still need to process some of that stuff.

I was biased right from the start of the talk, when he invoked complexity theory to say that complex phenomena occurs as a result of simple rules. So much of the software I see is like that, eg: the TCP protocol that we're using to post to this forum has some remarkably simple algorithms controlling it's parameters. I was reading about the rollout of CoDel last year (used to manage bufferbloat), and I could understand it pretty easily.

When I could never do is discover an algorithm like that; Simple things are very hard to discover.

-   Firstly, because we seem to have a bias against simple things -- there's an arrogance that something that can be explained to a high school student must be "too unsophisticated to describe reality".

    Of course, this is my mind is a failure to understand that it is almost impossible to control complex phenomena (as nature does) if the underlying rules are complex.

-   Secondly, out of a huge set of possibilities, you have to pick out the few that actually matter. Because of emergent behaviour (cannot work backwards from a result), there is no way to figure out which factors are significant by examining their products. We're left with trial and error based on educated guesses

Despite any disagreements that Thornhill may have with Einstein, E=mc2 and the photoelectric effect are simple concepts that will likely stand the test of time. Any modifications will be additive, and we won't have to throw away E=mc2 like the way we'll have to throw away the notion of a black hole.

Because of emergent behaviour (cannot work backwards from a result), there is no way to figure out which factors are significant by examining their products. We're left with trial and error based on educated guesses
>

Any modifications will be additive, and we won't have to throw away E=mc2 like the way we'll have to throw away the notion of a black hole.

[http://www.holoscience.com/wp/synopsis/synopsis-11-some-basics/](http://www.holoscience.com/wp/synopsis/synopsis-11-some-basics/)

Thornhill's view:

> **E = mc2**
> Einstein’s famous mathematical expression E=mc2, equating energy and mass is known by almost everyone. However, most textbooks go on to use the word “matter” in place of “mass.” But nowhere has it been shown that mass and matter are interchangeable. In fact, we are entirely ignorant of what constitutes the mass of an object. So it is inadmissible to imply that energy and matter are interchangeable. The ultimate expression of this idea led to the nonsense of the big bang. It seems simpler and more sensible to suggest that both nuclear and chemical energy is released or absorbed by the rearrangement of the resonant orbits of charged particles. It is then common sense to suggest that mass is the measured response of a system of charged particles to an external electrostatic force. The more massive an object, the more the electrostatic force contributes to the elastic deformation of its protons, neutrons and electrons, rather than their acceleration. This is the phenomenon seen in particle accelerators and conventionally attributed to relativistic effects. But relativity reduces to classical physics in a universe where the electrostatic force has near-infinite speed. The first question to be asked is – if it is that simple, why hasn’t it been thought of long ago? The answer seems to lie in the propensity for mathematical theory to supersede common sense and observation. There is also a problem of language when mathematicians attempt to provide real meaning for their symbols.

Einstein's relativity equations have held up experimentally, as has the quantum theory! But the interpretation of those equations and their application to reality is a different matter. [Look at the collapse of the Copenhagen interpretation of quantum mechanics now underway.](http://www.techtimes.com/articles/15553/20140914/quantum-mechanics-orthodoxy-of-copenhagen-interpretation-may-be-replaced-by-pilot-wave-theory.htm) I think the implications of General Relativity will also be in trouble soon. I think Jack would agree with that. Special Relativity may be a different story. Its equations will hold up for sure, but will their meaning as they are now understood in terms of spacetime? I don't know...

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-10-30T01:05:58+0200

### NeilBB — 2014-10-30T01:17:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145599>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=145594)
>
> any measurement of anything perceivable and measurable only serve to reduce thw whole into incremental pieces to understand it fragmentally, which appears to be the default state that the modern human brain is tuned to in order to rationalise. all science (whilst we need it in the current world state for medicine etc) only serves to take the immeasurable whole, measure it as fragments, and attempt to explain the whole again using the sum of its parts.

That is undeniably factually true. That is the method of human cognition.

But I would maintain that the Lipton/Bohm implication that this makes the knowledge gained thusly somehow less valid or invalid because of that is a modern philosophical myth. Such an attitude would denigrate any epistemological method at all and stems from a false philosophical standard of what constitutes knowledge...

The whole reason we need epistemology is to validate human conceptual knowledge gained in this incremental, disintegrated way, and to reintegrate it properly into a cohesive whole, i.e., keep it tied to reality. It is very easy for it to become disconnected at any point, as I tried to explain in that blog post...

Philosophical epistemology collapsed in the 19th century (after and because of German revolutionary philosopher Kant). Thats a whole'nother story, but suffice it to say that the resulting epistemological corruption of most of the quantum physicists, most notably Neils Bohr, is in fact what lead them to all get lost in mathematics and lose sight of WTF they were actually talking about. Hence, we got the bullshit story from Copenhagen that is just now beginning to be unraveled...

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-10-30T02:04:51+0200

i also think this then puts us in the position to question what really does constitute knowledge, again this is defined only by our prior experiences and conditioning. for instance take an amazonian shaman, who is vastly knowledgable (in his own reality) but is considered a witch doctor by our conditioned modern templates. i would perhaps argue that the shaman or any truly naturally living human has an awareness and connected "knowledge" far more vast than our own, albeit less able to be explained via reductive methodology and teaching

this is where i find bohm a compelling read

### yewwei.tan — 2014-10-30T04:37:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-145624>

The video I linked earlier also talks briefly about space-time -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.ph...is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/#post-145269](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/#post-145269)

Specifically, this excerpt which I took:

Mathematics use dimensions to represent degrees of freedom. Time has no direction (it is not a dimension). Joining space and time has no significance. So you cannot produce physical wormholes in space.
​

Then, Ron Hatch tackled Relativity, length contraction, time dilation, etc ... during the Electric Universe 2013 conference.

Very math heavy and I don't understand anything , but I'm sure some of the answers I seek are in there.

I don't know why, but that description feels at ease with the idea that "time can move in both directions". It's almost like saying that absent a stimulus, a particle simply "waits" until it requires something to be done, and only then does it react. The notion of condensed matter somehow gives me the idea of a higher proportion of particles "waiting around not doing anything". Stronger magnetism or gravity (electrostatic potential) would "warp time" but allowing for this condensation of matter to occur. (And of course, I don't know what I'm talking about )

Then the 'Electricity of LIfe' Panel raised even more points:

Does the time directionality variance in quantum mechniam scale from small particles electrons to larger systems like the heart or the brain? Rupert Shelldrake seems to think so, but we definitely don't have the language to describe it exactly yet.

I think there is a direct correlation between space and time, but I believe they act to balance each other, so the rules work oppositely. ### Dewey C. Layman Jr. — 2019-08-07T09:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-275561>

This thread should be revisited.

Somebody mentioned special relativity being clearly correct. It actually is not. Neither is general relativity. This has been observed and shown to be false with newtons bucket experiment and the fact that gravity has 0 affect of light waves. Yes, gravitational lensing is false. In the end, Einstein actually did not feel that his theories were very sound. People dont seem to talk about that much. The truth is Einstein had integrity in this way but his work is very unscientific and very false. Getting rid of our standards of measurement and making everything relative is not physics. There are no black holes, neutron stars, gravitons, antimatter particles, gluons, etc. There are 3 fundamental particles: electrons, protons, and neutrinos.

Photons are a concept and are physically possible. A particle cannot have no mass and travel through a vaccum. It needs a medium by which to perturb. Light merely acting as if it has a duality does not mean that it is both a wave and particle. It is neither. Light is not understood to this day.

Our universe is likely smaller than we think and is static NOT expanding based on an electric model of the universe. Gravity emerges from the electric force. The electric dipoles that we have between our feet and the ground is where this force emerges. It is what keeps us on the ground. It acts the same way that magnetism does and YES that means gravity is not only attractive but is repulsive but it IS much much weaker than magnetism. This should give hint as to why planets and other planetary bodies will rarely, if ever, collide because like charges on the surfaces of bodies will repel. Bodies that get too close release electric discharges that change the mass and gravity of these bodies. Hint: Chicxulub Crater was not an impact crater. It was an electric discharge. Did it happen millions of years ago? Also no. More like in the thousands.

Do we know the age of the universe or our own planet? The age we think it is needs to be questioned big time because ill bet its way off the mark. So whats the human story? Whats the mitochondria story? It isnt known yet. We do have great reason to believe that our current sun isnt our first one and isnt the one we are actually optimized to. Saturn, our brown dwarf star, is the star that has been mentioned in ancient civilization. Ra is not the current sun god. It is synonymous with Kronos, or Saturn. Our world along with mars, venus (spoiler: a comet created by saturn), and jupiter were a nice happy family living in a giant red anode glow created by Saturn. Our skies were purple. Crops, fruit, vegetation was plentiful because of red and UVs affect on photosynthesis. During this time we actually saw saturn mars and venus very close to our planet. Thats how ancient civilization knew there was giant electrical scarring on Mars that they called Scarface. How else could they have known this scar was there without telescopes?

This was known as the Golden Age when life flourished. A life lived in the red and purple. Want to know where most of the water on our planet came from? Answer: Saturn

Thats why Saturns rings contain water very similar to our own. Its also why Titan has a similar atmosphere now compared to Earth's atmosphere in the past.

Could this be why water in us is optimized to red light to make it refract at 270nm. Could this is be why our aromatic amino acids respond to UVC light? I think so. Does it have anything to do with deuterium in our blood? I dont know. That needs to be tested. UVC light doesnt come from our current sun. It probably came from our old one. Maybe now we can see a hint of this by looking at the absorption of these proteins.

We need to go back to classical physics. Chuck Einstein out and even what we think about quantum weirdness. It isnt weird at all. Its electric. There is only one fundamental force. The Electric Force.

I say this respectfully, April 2016 needs to be redone as well. Time to get rid of false beliefs.

### Dewey C. Layman Jr. — 2019-08-07T09:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-think-gravity-is-emergent-here-is-a-clue.11995/post-275562>

Gravitational Lensing False

I suspect that I have a leaky gut, and its fall with winter approaching. Do I continue eating coconut oil through the remaining fall and winter? Thanks

Go with your "gut" so to speak. Use what you can handle...

# Thread: I work till 4am three times a week. How can I OPTIMIZE?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-work-till-4am-three-times-a-week-how-can-i-optimize.11998/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### erich53 — 2014-10-27T06:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-work-till-4am-three-times-a-week-how-can-i-optimize.11998/post-145260>

Three nights a week I work in a restaurant till 4am.
I can sleep as long as I want to in a pitch black room.
I eat well, am active, 10 lbs overweight,but healthy.

Should I always go to bed and get up at the same time?
Can you think of ways to optimize? sleep hacks, supplements, light therapy,earthing etc

Any more seasond Jack Kruse readers than me are welcome to pitch in. I am brand new here and really curious to learn more.

### caroline — 2014-10-27T09:20:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-work-till-4am-three-times-a-week-how-can-i-optimize.11998/post-145264>

Hi Erich ...and welcome!

Please put up a picture - JK's rules .....no pic - no Jack!

Also start a journal in the journal section .....Dr. K. needs context ... and we do too if we are to offer some help.

your age and health history
your family health history
where do you live?
what is your non native EMF situation?
Fake blue lite?
exercise? if so - what?
sleep? how is it?
grounding? do you get outside in the am? do you hug tress???
what do you eat? drink? R/O water?
libido?

the best way to get the most out of jackkruse.com is to become a gold member \[if you can manage it\] .... you will then have access to all Jack's webinars 24/7 and a monthly live Q&A

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX - it is a great way to get on board ...and gives you the first two years of the blog

Have you been reading Jack's blogs?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-27T15:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-work-till-4am-three-times-a-week-how-can-i-optimize.11998/post-145299>

Quit. This job will ruin you, is the short answer.

### erich53 — 2014-11-01T20:55:30+0200

# Thread: Something interesting for singers!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-interesting-for-singers.12004/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-10-27T19:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-interesting-for-singers.12004/post-145320>

On Saturday and Sunday mornings I sit out on my porch in the sunshine, drinking my coffee and singing. For the past two weeks I've noticed that when I sing looking directly into the morning sun (I'm usually out there from about 7:30 to 9 or 9:30) the ability to hit very high notes becomes absolutely effortless, as does vibrato. Not just sitting in the full sun, but looking directly into it. Just fascinating. Try it!

### ColdKnight — 2014-10-27T21:17:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-interesting-for-singers.12004/post-145329>

This sounds awesome! Your not worried about burning your retinas, or did I miss something and now its ok to do that also?

### Hansen Kenimer — 2014-10-27T21:29:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-interesting-for-singers.12004/post-145331>

Something about the sun at that time of day is different. It's easy to look at, and doesn't hurt my eyes.

### nonchalant — 2014-10-28T22:03:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-interesting-for-singers.12004/post-145452>

Oh neat! I'll have to try this. Maybe I'll be able to actually hit a few notes correctly then. ;-)
I wonder if reading Jack's blogs in the early morning sunlight would make the process easier?

# Thread: Why is moving air an electron stealer?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-moving-air-an-electron-stealer.12005/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2014-10-27T20:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-moving-air-an-electron-stealer.12005/post-145323>

So, I was telling the kids that when they run the casablanca fan, it is an "electron stealer" - but then I realized I actually had no idea why that was... but even more interestingly though, my neighbor with Sjogrens, Raynaud's and Fibromyalgia can't go out in the wind - regardless of the temperature without pain - don't remember if it was in her head or just her ears, but am thinking she is in really bad shape... if anyone can explain that to me, I would appreciate it thanks!

### JMO — 2014-10-28T00:15:16+0200

### Penny — 2014-10-28T23:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-moving-air-an-electron-stealer.12005/post-145459>

So, I was thinking about the wind at o'dark 30 this morning and thought about how a waterfall is invigorating because of the negative ions and thought maybe someone with almost zero extra electrons would be affected by wind because it is positively charged... So, I found this:

**IONS CREATED BY WINDS MAY PROMPT CHANGES IN EMOTIONAL STATES**
**By WALTER SULLIVAN
Published: October 6, 1981**

-   EMAIL
-   [PRINT](http://www.nytimes.com/1981/10/06/science/ions-created-by-winds-may-prompt-changes-in-emotional-states.html?pagewanted=print)
-   REPRINTS

FOR many years it has been suspected that certain weather conditions, notably such hot, dry winds as the foehn in Europe, the sharav in Israel and the Santa Ana in southern California, affect people's mood and health, even precipitating suicides, crimes and accidents.

One controversial explanation -electrification of the atmosphere by positive ions - has now acquired greater plausibility through a series of experiments on volunteers at New York University.

The findings, the researchers said, ''are potentially quite important, as atmospheric charge is a fundamental part of the air we breathe.''

''Since altered ion concentrations can result not only from changes in weather conditions but also from the presence of pollutants, highvoltage lines, and radioactivity,'' they said, ''the possibility of far-reaching implications emerges.''

Normally molecules of oxygen, nitrogen and other elements in the air carry their full complement of electrons and are therefore electrically neutral. In various ways, however, an electron may be knocked off such a molecule, leaving it with a positive charge. It is then a ''positive ion.''

The liberated electron may attach itself to a molecule that already has a full complement of electrons, whereupon the molecule becomes a ''negative ion.''

Positive ions may be generated by hot winds, radio and television transmitters, ventilators or direct current power lines. For a number of years some people, to neutralize the allegedly deleterious effects of such ions, have installed negative ion generators in their homes or offices.

When, in the 1950's, ion generators were intensively marketed as effective against a variety of diseases, the Food and Drug Administration forced the salesmen to claim only that the generators help clean the air.

Ever since then the scientific community, with some exceptions, has looked down its nose at suggestions that ions affect human health and behavior. Now, however, researchers believe they have documented some psychological effects of positive ions.

The experiments are described by Dr. Jonathan M. Charry of Rockefeller University and Dr. Frank B.W. Hawkinshire 5th of N.Y.U. in a recent issue of the Journal of Personality and Social Psychology.

The apparent effects of positive ions included increased tension and irritability as well as a slowing of reaction times. The reaction times were measured in terms of the lag between appearance of a red light and the pressing of a button by the subject.

These effects, however, were not obvious when findings from all 85 subjects were lumped together. They were evident chiefly when results from the 34 subjects with a less resilient autonomic nervous system were separately examined. The autonomic system controls reflexes, heart performance and the other involuntary functions. Its resilience can be seen, for example, in the return of heart rate to normal after a fright.

In an interview last week Dr. Charry pointed out that the study was the first conducted in this manner and its findings must be considered tentative until replicated elsewhere.

Much of the early research on this subject was conducted in Israel, where the sharav has a marked effect. It has been reported that 30 percent of the population becomes ill with migraine, nausea, vomiting, irritability, dimness of vision, respiratory symptoms and other effects.

The symptoms are said to appear a day or two before heat and dryness become severe, but when air blowing in from the desert is already laden with positive ions. Such winds in other parts of the world have also been associated with increased crime, suicide and accidents. These winds, in addition to the foehn, sharav, and Santa Ana, include the chinook of the Pacific Northwest, the sirroco of Italy, the ghibli of Libya and the zonda of the Argentine Andes.

The results of the current research derive some support from mouse experiments conducted for over two decades by Dr. Albert Paul Krueger and his colleagues at the University of Californ ia in Berkeley.

They have reported that exposure to high levels of positive ions raises blood levels of the hormone serotonin, a substance that has generated much recent research in a number of fields as scientists increasingly appreciate the hormone's importance in behavior. Negative ions have the opposite effect on serotonin in the blood. Both types of ion, however, depleted brain levels of the hormone. As noted by Dr. Charry, analysis of brain t issue of suicides has shown reduced levels of the hormone, as has c erebrospinal fluid from depressed people. In Israel, during a sharav, excess amounts of serotonin have been found in the urine of tho se classed ''weathersensitive.''

The discovery in mice that negative, as well as positive, ions depress brain levels of the hormone, Dr. Charry said, suggests that long exposure to artificially produced ions might be harmful. He and his colleagues are now conducting tests with rats to determine the effects on brain and behavior of various ion doses and lengths of exposure. Later they hope to continue the human tests.

The new results, according to Dr. Charry, suggest that much more needs to be known of ion effects on the body before the usefulness of devices generating negative ions can be assessed.

In the N.Y.U. experiments the volunteers were told that, while details regarding the purpose of the tests would be withheld until afterwards, lest they bias the results, the goal concerned factors that lead pilots and drivers to commit fatal errors. Each person was tested twice in the experimental chamber: once with the ion generators on and once with them off. The volunteers were unaware of the generators' role.

Whether each person was first tested with the generators on or off was randomly determined. The two sessions were spaced three days apart. The test for resilience of the autonomic nervous system was one widely used by heart specialists since that system controls heart performance and blood pressure.

It involves plunging one hand into cold water while electrodes attached to the palm of the other hand record changes in its electrical conductivity. The response was interpreted to identify those whose autonomic nervous system is less resilient.

It was those who proved most ion-sensitive. For members of this category the lag between display of a red light and the buttonpushing response increased after ion exposure.

Also used was a standard psychological test, known as the Nowlis Mood Adjective Checklist. Each subject was given an alphabetized list of 100 adjectives and asked to indicate those nearest his or her state of mind - from adaptable, adventurous, aggressive and so on to worried.

Scoring depended on the clustering of responses in various categories. The subjects were also rated on such subjective symptoms as headache, dizziness, blurred vision and drowsiness. The results, after ion exposure, showed reduced scores in the categories labeled sociability, surgency (elation) and task involvement.

Scores showed an increase in tension, inattention and fatigue. Males appeared more susceptible than females, but it was suggested that the women might be vulnerable to long-term exposure. The tests lasted only 90 minutes.

Those whose autonomic nervous systems appeared highly resilient - two-thirds of the total - showed no ion effects. ''It appears that consideration of individual differences in ion sensitivity,'' wrote the researchers, ''is essential for assessing performance effects of ions.''

# Thread: Wow, now they can nuke us even more...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-now-they-can-nuke-us-even-more.12007/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2014-10-28T02:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow-now-they-can-nuke-us-even-more.12007/post-145364>

[http://hardware.slashdot.org/story/14/10/27/1531222/haier-plans-to-embed-are](http://hardware.slashdot.org/story/14/10/27/1531222/haier-plans-to-embed-are)

Haier [has signed a development agreement with Energous](http://www.computerworld.com/article/2838591/your-next-fridge-could-someday-charge-all-your-mobile-devices.html), a maker of the WattUp wireless charging router. Haier plans incorporate the technology in appliances allowing enabled mobile devices and wearables to take a charge at up 15 feet away. The white goods maker is expected to come out with the enabled appliances in the next 14 months or so. The WattUp router uses radio frequency (RF) transmissions to send up to 4 watts of power in a 15-ft. radius. Within 5 feet of a WattUp wireless router, a mobile device can be charged at the same rate as if it were plugged into a wall socket, but as the distance increase the charging capability dissipates. For example, at a range of 5-to-10 feet, charging capability drops to 2 watts per device and at 10-to-15 feet, the router puts out 1 watt per device (4 watts total). Pleasanton, Calif.-based Energous raised nearly $25 million when it went public earlier this year. Its chief marketing officer said the company has joint development agreements in the works with battery makers, smartphone sleeve and wearable device manufacturers. Haier hasn't disclosed what products it plans to enable with wireless charging.

### nicld — 2014-10-28T03:56:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145383>

I am having some issues with figuring out my readings. For some reason I get a negative reading on my meter. I have the black and red hooked up right.

Anyone else get negative readings when grounding? Just want to make sure that I am doing this right since I am having doubts.

### Da-mo — 2014-10-28T07:20:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145393>

What happens when you swap the terminals over?

### nicld — 2014-10-28T15:43:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145422>

^^^^I did that last night but cannot remember what happened, I will try again tonight. I did double check the manual and with the hubby to make sure that I had the terminals right and they were.

Here is what I have been finding. I bought a copper grounding rod and drove it into the ground in my basement by another old pipe that was used when we had a shallow point well. Initially testing it and thought it was not too bad. Then I started testing upstairs. I was getting readings of about 400 mV in the living room (where all of the electronics are), not horrible but I still think too high. Odd thing was when I was sitting on the sofa and put my feet on the ottoman the reading dropped, very weird. It was lower in my kitchen and of course in the bedroom where the Magnetico is.

So this weekend I did some deeper diving into it. I shut off all the power to the house and retested. I got the exact same readings and this confused the hell out of me. I measure stuff for a living and this was stumping me. So I went back to the source, back to the grounding rod in the basement. With all the power off I touched the grounding terminal to the rod and it measured 500ish mV, WTF. Obviously it was NOT grounded. So I stuck the ground terminal in the ground right next to the rod and the readings went to 0. Back to square 1. I did have an old copper water pipe so I took that and stuck it outside in my garden beside the house and got 0 mV to negative mV readings. Ran a new wire from that pipe into the house and that is where I started to get more negative readings.

Last night I started testing the two wires right next to each other. The one that was attached to the pipe outside and one that was attached to the rod in the basement. I was getting negative readings from the one outside and 400’s again from the one it the basement. Based on some readings that I did, I attached the grounding terminal to the wire attached to the pipe outside and the reading terminal to the wire that is attached to the basement and got a reading over 800 mV. Then I put the ground on the wire in to the basement and the red to the wire going outside and got a negative 800mV reading. So I am seriously confused.

I also decided to test the readings on the hubby when we crawled in bed. Without the grounding strap on we both we at about 0 mV to just below 0mV. I then attached the strap to him and it read -200 mV, the same as I got so I know it is not just me.

Tonight I will be getting a new grounding rod since the one that it is the basement is way too deep to pull out now.

Like I said, I am a Quality Engineer which means I measure and analyze data but this one is just stumping me. Is it ok to have negative readings? I am thinking of borrowing one of the calibrated volt meters from work tonight and see if I get different results. The one I am using is also from work but it is not calibrated but really has not been used since we bought it.

Last night I bought a new grounding rod and stuck it in the ground right outside of the bedroom. I tested just the rod outside and got a 0 reading. Then I added the wire that I was going to use for it outside and again got a 0. Ran the wire in the house and tested it right next to the window. ### Penny — 2014-10-29T21:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-145563>

**I'm sure you know \*way\* more about this than I, but maybe you have this:

[http://www.dirtyelectricity.ca/dirty\_electricity\_four\_components.htm](http://www.dirtyelectricity.ca/dirty_electricity_four_components.htm)

Ground Currents and Plumbing Current**

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Every time an electron leaves a power plant to create electricity for our homes, schools and offices it must return to create more electricity – fundamental law of physics. Today, the earth’s surface has a higher conductivity then the utility’s overburdened neutral return wire, so now it's estimated that as much as 70 to 80% of returning current travels back along the earth’s surface in what’s known as the “skin-effect.”

Many homes today are now affected by plumbing current . The happens when some of the current that would normally return through the electrical service line is instead channeled into the grounding system, where it returns to the transformer by way of alternate paths, including water pipes, the public water main and neighboring residences.

Two laws of physics support the fact that returning electrical ground current takes the path of least resistance and will take any and all paths - including into your home. This EMF energy can be conducted into a home's electrical system through water and gas pipes, phones lines, and other conductive objects instead of back to the substation through the neutral return wire as originally designed.

These electrical contaminates can then intermingle with a building’s 60 Hertz (Hz) electricity, polluting it it with undesirable high frequeicies and in some cases higher voltages. We are capacitively coupled to this with this run-away electric energy by simply being in a contaminate building or in contact with common household fixtures like sinks, bathtubs and other conductive objects.

### nicld — 2014-11-04T22:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-146144>

Not sure if my “house” is getting gutted to get remodeled or if it is just getting more dilapidated. Been using my grounding wrist strap in the evening and while sleeping. Lately the head feels weird like I have a lot of synapses firing and the head is a bit hot (have felt like this before I think when I first got my Magnetico). The muscles were achy while sleeping last night, the feet got hot and sleep has not been the best. I have been waking up early again but not sure if that has to do with the time change. Concentration has not been bad so that is good and neither has the energy level so not sure what to think. I keep checking it to make sure that I am still grounded and it still is. ### NeilBB — 2014-11-07T20:01:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-146459>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=146458)
>
> Yep. My black ground terminal is either touching a grounding device or is attached to the copper wire that is attached to the grounding device outside. I hold the red positive terminal in my fingers or I have touched the inside of my check.
>
> When I lay on my Magnetico it drops to zero but when I attach the wrist strap my body voltage reading drops to negative numbers.
>
> Inger has had the same experience on her pad too, right Inger?
>
> I am also talking about it just dropping to -.2 volts so not real dramatic just odd. Also the highest reading I have ever had in my house with any of the grounding rods (which are in question) is 0.5 V. I feel like I am doing it right but it is not making sense to me right now.

If you are using 2 different grounding rods, one for the black terminal and one for the wrist strap, then that small voltage likely reflects a slight difference in potential actually in the ground at those two different sites, whether it's positive or negative just depends on which of the two rods is higher. There are no absolute voltages. It's all relative, even ground voltages. Calling ground "zero" is arbitrary. Personally, I don't think it's a big deal.

Also realize that if this is the case, then that small potential you measure on your skin will likely go away once you stop the measurement. The voltage is actually created by the process of measurement and being in contact with two slightly different grounds. When you are just lying on the Magnetico, not measuring, you skin voltage likely returns to zero.

I still think the RF could be an issue on the Magnetico though, like we talked about before.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-11-09T20:48:36+0200

Electrons move in circuits within and outside the body. the voltages are small in biophysics which upsets the belief system of most........on the order of 0.01 volts, but it is measurable. Go buy a high quality volt ohm meter and check for your self. You will see massive difference all over tissues. For example you nise is more positive than your mouth which is more negative. This sets up a classic cathode anode experiment that is the basis if redox electrochemistry. You'll find the voltage varies with respiration and HR. Why? magnetic flux in air flows due diatomic paramagnetic oxygen and in the blood DHA and O2 do the same. This means there has to be an opposing flow of metal ions between these circuits to close the circuit. Metformin provides those ions..............that is why it works. The electromagnetic force is amazing when you get how she works. Becker did.

So.........why is hypoxia bad for a eukaryote and good for a prokaryote? hypoxia = A low redox potential = a low delta psi. It is a state where water chemistry is very altered. Water is lacking inside and around cells, and cells are dehydrated. The water that is left behind has very little oxygen tension in it. This is why they call it hypoxia. It has altered levels of protons and/or electrons in it too. This metabolic situation leads to karyotype contraction. This means the number, **_size and shape of chromosomes becomes altered_**. The exclusion zone of water is smaller than it should be in most disease and it has an altered ability to allow protons to flow through ATP[synthetase Fo in mitochondria](http://www.bioinitiative.org/report/wp-content/uploads/pdfs/sec16_2012_Plausible_Genetic_Metabolic_Mechanisms.pdf). This does not allow water to bind to proteins and cell membranes properly and it alters their ability to sense the magnetic fields in their environment. It directly affects the mitochondrial magnetic sense. Sunlight and a magnetic field's flux flows are capable of ionizing water in the entire universe, including inside your cells. This very basic fact is not yet a biologic law, however, it is a natural physical law found everywhere we look. Biology and medicine just have not changed their gaze from mutated genes yet.

That is why I am going to give it one month and see where I am at and retest the HsCRP.

Thankfully I do have some experience in designing tests (DOE’s) and gage r&r’s and how to account for some variables.

I made sure to verify that the multimeter that I was using was correct by validating the results with a better reader. For what it is worth, I also checked my readings against my husband in various locations in the house and we were about the same.

I still cannot figure out why the grounding rod gives me different readings then the copper pipe when in the exact same location.

This weekend I will see what happens when I hook at a wire to our copper pipe hot water heating system. Also going to try and go outside and retest the pipe versus rod now that the ground is freezing. Might also try to see what the readings are with the water pipe coming into the house.

It is good to know that I am not the only one seeing negative readings on their meter.

### nicld — 2014-11-15T19:55:16+0200

### nicld — 2014-11-20T19:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-147988>

Update. So I no longer get the tingly feeling that I was getting when I hooked myself up to the grounding strap. Last time I checked my body voltage on my Magnetico while hooked up to the grounding strap the reading were lower than when I started which I think it is good. Sleep is still getting better and the brain fog is getting better every day.
The first pic is without the grounding strap on while on the Magnetico and the second is with it on on the Magnetico. It used to read 220 mV.

I will recheck myself in the living room this weekend and see how they compare.

I did get a salt lamp for work since that is a pretty high EMF environment. I have had it for about a week and when I first plugged it in I had such a weird feeling in my head that I had to turn it off. Now I am able to keep it on all day and I am hoping that it is helping. It was just so weird.

### nicld — 2014-11-25T18:52:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-148446>

^^^^ I am getting one for at home too now. I will be sure to check the body voltage when sitting by it.

Ok, so last night as I was starting to fall asleep in bed I thought that I have felt a surge come though my grounding wire and through the body. Needless to say I took it off and slept without it. Never felt that before and not even sure if it was real. I will have to do more testing tonight and see if anything changed.

I do know that my overall voltage has changed. When testing without being grounded the levels are not as high as they used to be and I don’t seem to be as negative when I am grounded. I will try to get some new pictures this weekend.

I am feeling pretty good and no slump in energy. I will be getting my HsCRP checked next month to see where I am at. Besides that I am not sure how else to check and see if the grounding strap at night is helping or hurting.

### nicld — 2015-06-18T20:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-168787>

**_UPDATE!!!!!!!!!!_**

So my living room is CRAP!!!!!!! Something is going on and now I am basically afraid to spend any time in there. I got a new meter that reads volts way better and am actually testing the AC current and NOT the DC current (yes, I am a blonde). Been testing it a couple of times and last night it was up to 11V in one area!!!! Yes it is carpeting so I am sure that may have something to do with it but when I turn off the breaker to the house, it drops to almost nothing. I do not get the same readings in the kitchen and the further away from the living room I get the better the readings get. And to think, I would lay on the floor before bed.

I am not getting my trifeild to really show anything expect near power cords. I did test with the power cords unplugged and still got a higher reading. When I turned off the breaker to the living room it dropped a bunch but was still a bit high.

Also, I have the computer grounding plug that Michael Neuert suggested and when I had that plugged into my laptop, my readings went over 20 (WTF!!!!!!).

So, next step will be to buy a outlet tester and see if the outlets in the living room are even grounded. I also got stuff to make a buzz stick that I will be trying to pinpoint where the high readings maybe coming from. [http://emfhelpcenter.com/](http://emfhelpcenter.com/)

I have already contacted 2 electricians to see what they know about EMF’s and if they can come out and give me an estimate on what is going on.

Then there is the fun part, convincing the hubby that something is wrong. He kinda poopoo’s what I am doing. He was off playing D and D last night when I did more testing so I sent him a video on body voltage to watch and said that I am very concerned about the testing that I did. He can’t deny that there is something going on when I see about replicating the testing I did for him. It might wait until Tuesday when we have the carpet replaced. I will be curious to see if it is just the carpeting, which would really really suck since we are keeping carpeting in the living room. We also had an outlet that just quit working a couple of years ago and we cannot figure out why. Maybe that is the source, just not sure.

Now I know why I feel better sitting outside!!!!

### nicld — 2015-06-19T15:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-168932>

I have an old old house. I think we saw the first easement on the property was in 1929. My "basement" is actually the old root cellar as the walls in that section are at least 8" think on each side.

I know I do not have a grounding rod since the power coming into the house is already buried from the electrical pole to the house.

After I do a little more testing this weekend (and take more pictures), I will be scheduling a time for an electrician to come out. One of the one that responded does have some knowledge about it but I will make sure that I do more reading this weekend and relisten to the EMF bootcamp again to make sure that I have as much knowledge as I can.

I just tried to stay out of the living room last night and when I was in it, I was grounded. I know that is not the best and that I need to get rid of the source of the high EMF's but that may take a little time so I will keep grounding myself until it gets fixed.

### nicld — 2015-06-21T00:14:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-169093>

Oh holy hell, almost none of my outlets in the house are grounded. One of the few ones is in the bathroom, which we just remodeled and our contractor ran new wires. Since this one is grounded I am thinking that my box is grounded but none of the wiring is. We have been in this house for almost 18 years and we had always talked about getting the wiring redone, guess we should have.

So for now, I am going to keep my grounding strap on as much as I can at home and keep it on the DD. I will be letting the DH know when he gets home that we will be needing to rewire and show him that none of the "three" prong outlets are actually grounded.

It took me a bit but glad I never gave up on rechecking the house and good thing I never bought anything to "plug" in to be grounded. I would have been just zapping myself.

### nicld — 2015-06-30T17:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170088>

So, still doing more testing and rearranging to get the nnEMF’s better in the house. The DH was working OT last night so I took the opportunity to do more testing and mainly in the bedroom. When I would test using the body voltage meter on my Magnetico bed I was still getting higher readings that I was like (2V), way too high. It was even higher on the DH side. I bought an amplifier to make a “buzz stick” to help pinpoint more stuff to look at. I have a sound machine that was plugged into an extension cord coming from the other side of the room but the plug in box was about 2 feet away. BINGO, that plug in went through the roof on the buzz stick. Since I am not ready to give up the sound machine it got moved 5 feet away and on the dresser and not the nightstand. My readings on the meter went down to 0.3 to 0.5V, way better. At these levels I feel it is ok to hook myself up to the grounding rod again at night.

Did some work on the DH’s side as well. We had a large extension cord that was running under his head to the window fan and his alarm clock (which I have now moved as far away from him as I can and inside his dresser drawer). Also rerouted the extension cord to come from the other wall so there is nothing plugged in on the wall where our bed is. I did see what difference I got from shutting off the breakers and did not see a real difference. It might not drop to nothing without shutting off the whole house and that is not going to happen. The readings from his side dropped to below 1V which is good. I will be trying to relisten to week 1 of the EMF webinar again.

### nicld — 2015-06-30T17:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170089>

Next task is getting the electrician to come out and rewire the living room. They were not sure if they could rewire the whole house or if that was even needed. I told him yesterday that the living room HAS to be rewired, that is going to be a non-negotiable task. The readings in that room are so high and my DD and DH spends most of their time in there that I just cannot have readings that high. We have a nice combo of newer ungrounded wiring and the old cloth covered wires. We were hoping to have all of the cloth covered redone but if adding the GFI to the breaker box for the bedrooms helps then I can hold off since the electrician did not sound that happy to do that and the costs that come with it. t was running under his head to the window fan and his alarm clock (which I have now moved as far away from him as I can and inside his dresser drawer). Also rerouted the extension cord to come from the other wall so there is nothing plugged in on the wall where our bed is. I did see what difference I got from shutting off the breakers and did not see a real difference. It might not drop to nothing without shutting off the whole house and that is not going to happen. The readings from his side dropped to below 1V which is good. I will be trying to relisten to week 1 of the EMF webinar again.

### nicld — 2015-07-01T16:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170222>

Received the quote yesterday and he tried not to scare me about the cost but worst case scenario is $4700 (gulp) but I kinda expected that. Hoping it will be alot less since for the rewiring of the living room they can go through the basement.

### nicld — 2015-07-01T17:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-reading.12009/post-170228>

Hope I am doing this right but I made my own grounding cord that plugs into the wall to test myself at work. Just have the ground cord attached to the plug and attached to my wire which I have attached to my meter. This is plugged into the wall since I have no way of attaching a wire to a grounding rod outside at work.

I work in an engineering wing, on the ground floor on a painted concrete slab that was added when my company bought the building and has all new electrical and is up to code. When I tested at my office space it looks GREAT (better than the house). With shoes on, my reading was about 0.32 V, when I took my shoes off and put my bare feet on the painted concrete, it dropped to 0.002V. WHOOOHOOOO, I can be grounded at work. I then went and touched my phone not is airplane mode and my salt lamp and it went up to about 1.5V. I then took my bare foot and placed it over by the surge protector and it went to 2.3-3V. I

I am so glad to know that where I spend 45 hours a week is not horrible. Now to just get the BluTech glasses to help protect against the blue light and I should be mitigating all the risks the best I can while still working. I do have a wired keyboard and wired mouse and my monitors are about 2 feet away. Now I will be trying to sit barefoot as much as I can at work. There was no way that I was going to try grounding at work using the electrical system and this just shows it was a good decision and not needed.
