### Da-mo — 2015-05-14T00:11:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164168>

Yes did the ice thing, popsicle thing and even icepacks on the neck which seemed to help. Gargled Colloidal Silver, Apple Cider Vinegar, Salty water, mouthwash etc.

It got too far out of control - not sleeping, having trouble breathing with the swelling, relentless generalized inflammation- so generalized pain added to acute throat pain. My digestion had pretty much shut down - didnt seem like anything was moving through so I felt as if any food added on top might just get rejected. Dropped 5kg bodyweight - NONE off it of my abdomen.

After trying to tough it out for 4 days I wasn't making any headway, just getting weaker. So off the the doc I went.

Nurse asked me if I was still trying to give up smoking - I said I had given up and never felt worse lol

Upshot - 7 day course of Phenoxymethyl Penicillin (antibiotic) along with Naproxen for pain (NSAID).

Yeah, I know . . . I dont take antibiotics and nsaids lightly - and probably have taken any in the last 10 years. Interestingly, my osteoarthritic knees feel better since taking the Naproxen than they have in many many years. They have no pain - I forgot what that feels like.

> Upshot - 7 day course of Phenoxymethyl Penicillin (antibiotic) along with Naproxen for pain (NSAID).
>
> Yeah, I know . . . I dont take antibiotics and nsaids lightly - and probably have taken any in the last 10 years. Interestingly, my osteoarthritic knees feel better since taking the Naproxen than they have in many many years. They have no pain - I forgot what that feels like.

I know I used to have it a lot because I smoked and being in rooms were people smoke. My parents both smoked. The strep throat started when I was pretty young. I didn't have one in about 4 years since a gave up smoking. Until last February. In winter I was a lot in places were people smoked. First I got flu and a week later strep throat. I took the antibiotics too. I had fever of 102 degrees for several days and started to dehydrated pretty bad. So I really understand you're choose. Stop with the NSAID (even if your knees feels better), better take asprine if you can/may. Get better first. Then try to recover from AB. And never start smoking again. I can really recommend it. And I was a bad ass nicotine junky (probably from conception and birth on).

O yeah I really had a bad craving for fresh white bread with peanut butter. That is really poison to me, but I ate it anyway because it somehow scrapped my throat. Pretty nasty.... never mind that.

Take care!

### Da-mo — 2015-05-14T01:19:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164176>

HA! Peanut butter was my comfort food for many years - but not often now being a legume.

I work with a guy who is 15 years younger than I. They will remove his tonsils next month. I have seen him almost always have a sore throat over the years I have worked with him. They say the bug is always in his tonsils waiting for his immune levels to drop.

From the local literature on the Naproxen

> Mechanism of Action Naproxen is a phenylpropionic acid derivative having analgesic, anti-inflammatory and antipyretic activity. Such activity is thought to be mediated via inhibition of the enzyme complex prostaglandin synthetase with consequent reduction in the synthesis of prostaglandins from arachidonic acid.

. . . gives me a clue of where to start looking for alternative methods of managing knee pain . . . .

### Inger — 2015-05-14T08:47:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164199>

So sorry you struggle Sandy
I used to have big issues with my tonsils... as a teenager. IDK if it is the same issue you have but mine got, and only the right side tonsil.... it got HUGE and so bad I could barely drink water. I got so dehydrated because i had very high fever too. It just hurt too bad to swallow anything! I could not even TALK, darn! That was the hardest part.. I remember I just cried, I was that miserable I had to take antibiotics too. And the doc had to cut the boil open and drain it.. it hurt so bad I almost passed out. So I got through that several times and the doc said I MUST cut my tonsils out, cause I am gonna get it again and again. And he said it is a dangerous bacteria in there... yeah.
But.... I was so freaking scared of the surgery so I did not go...lol

Instead I just did it my own way
Next time I got it, I did not went to the doc at all... I waited and suffered with high fever... tried to get down water.. did not take any medicine... tried to swallow and swallow even if it hurt like crazy... then the old wound opened up and it drained naturally (yuck) and then I did anything I could think of... like garlic.... raw onions around my neck etc etc. So it healed, no antibiotics. I got it again many times, every time it was a milder version and less pain and inflammation.... never went to the doc again and after a few years it stopped... I just got a little sore for a few days and that was it. So now... in maybe 12 years.. I have had no issues whatsoever with my tonsils, they are perfectly healthy

Maybe I have some dangerous bacteria lurking....lol but I feel very healthy and it just makes me take good care of me, so maybe it is a good thing after all
and I am so freaking happy I still have all the organs intact in my body

### prAna303 — 2015-05-14T17:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164258>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=164168)
>
> It got too far out of control - not sleeping, having trouble breathing with the swelling, relentless generalized inflammation- so generalized pain added to acute throat pain.

Okey a weird take on situations like this and how i react to the environment...

If i spend to much time indoors, to much time in fake light and warm places i get a growing pain and swelling in my neck, throat and around eyes. I take it like a reaction that i must change place, find natural solutions.

I go out, place my ass on the ground, under a pine or by the sea. Focus on my breath, since i do my freediving i love air but loves to control myself. Deep breath, in thru my nose and in best cases out that way, if i am under stress i breath out thru mouth. Starts to do 1 - 4 - 2 intervals. In for 1 second, hold for four and out for two, i adjust in proper ways to set systems into sync, i feel a calm very soon and pain goes away, swelling also. Good if you can not sleep also but not a long term solution.

### nonchalant — 2015-05-14T18:14:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164263>

One thing I'd try is inhaling steam (from a safe distance) through your mouth and nose. Burst of water chemistry energy right to the spots that need it.

I'll have a look at the cleanse, thanks

I got so dehydrated because i had very high fever too. It just hurt too bad to swallow anything! I could not even TALK, darn! That was the hardest part.. So I got through that several times and the doc said I MUST cut my tonsils out, cause I am gonna get it again and again. And he said it is a dangerous bacteria in there... yeah.

>

> Next time I got it, I did not went to the doc at all... I waited and suffered with high fever... tried to get down water.. did not take any medicine... tried to swallow and swallow even if it hurt like crazy... then the old wound opened up and it drained naturally (yuck) and then I did anything I could think of... like garlic.... raw onions around my neck etc etc. So it healed, no antibiotics. I got it again many times, every time it was a milder version and less pain and inflammation.... never went to the doc again and after a few years it stopped... I just got a little sore for a few days and that was it. So now... in maybe 12 years.. I have had no issues whatsoever with my tonsils, they are perfectly healthy
>
> Maybe I have some dangerous bacteria lurking....lol but I feel very healthy and it just makes me take good care of me, so maybe it is a good thing after all
> and I am so freaking happy I still have all the organs intact in my body

No Inger, I have never had big issues with my tonsils. My son did and had his adenoids out and grommets when he was 2yo and his tonsils later when he around 12yo.

As a child my biggest illnesses were hepatitis when I was around 6-7 and bronchitis a couple of years later. Other than that I had a very healthy childhood mostly outdoors exploring the world with my friends or else various sports.

I dont often get sick - If I do it is either in the spring or the autumn when the weather temperature changes and then only last a day or two. Most times I do not go t the doctor - I fight it off myself as you did - but this time I had the feeling that if I tried that I was going to do more damage to myself than if I just took the medicine. So I chose what felt like the lesser of two evils this time. I think that up to a certain point, you can tell whether you are going to have enough strength to fight the illness off without help, and when you need help. My wife had the same thing 1 week before i did and she was beginning to get better by herself after just 2 days compared to me still going downhill after 4.

### Da-mo — 2015-05-15T06:51:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164334>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=164258)
>
> Okey a weird take on situations like this and how i react to the environment...
>
> If i spend to much time indoors, to much time in fake light and warm places i get a growing pain and swelling in my neck, throat and around eyes. I take it like a reaction that i must change place, find natural solutions.
>
> I go out, place my ass on the ground, under a pine or by the sea. Focus on my breath, since i do my freediving i love air but loves to control myself. Deep breath, in thru my nose and in best cases out that way, if i am under stress i breath out thru mouth. Starts to do 1 - 4 - 2 intervals. In for 1 second, hold for four and out for two, i adjust in proper ways to set systems into sync, i feel a calm very soon and pain goes away, swelling also. Good if you can not sleep also but not a long term solution.

For me too much time indoors gives me a feeling of discomfort - the softest chair or bed still feels hard and lumpy - and time out in nature sitting on the ground or on logs or walking over uneven natural ground is the only thing that can restore that feeling of comfort. After that the the hardest old chair feels wonderful. When I visit my relatives in Fiji I sleep on the floor, or on the ground, and we spend the evenings outside under the stars around the fire. Feels like home to me - connected and solid and balanced sufficient all at once.

On the 2nd day of the tonsilitis the sun came out (it has been raining heavily since) so I went outside onto the grass at about 9am, removed my shirt and sat in the sun with my 2 dogs and even fell asleep with them for a few hours - dozing in on the grass under the bight sun. After about 4-5 hours I felt Id absorbed as much as I could handle and it was starting to make me feel worse again so I stopped.

But that night in a fitful sleep in a dream I woke with the distinct impression I had dreamed JK saying "tonsilitis strips the redox potential out of the cell" so I sat up in bed cross-legged and did the only thing I could think of at that moment to restore the redox which was to practice "chi up" by drawing in universal energy and then circulating it using a Buddhist breathing meditation I knew called "small circulation". It seemed to work temporarily but I dont seem to be able to store charge like I used to. Its not that my battery has stopped working - more like it has developed a memory and doesnt cycle as deeply as it used to.

I have not tried your breathing technique - we generally didn't ever hold our breath in martial arts - but since this is about health more than combat I shall give your method a try

### Da-mo — 2015-05-15T06:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164335>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=164263)
>
> One thing I'd try is inhaling steam (from a safe distance) through your mouth and nose. Burst of water chemistry energy right to the spots that need it.

I had at least one very hot soak in the tub - usually with Epsom Salt - each day and found it absolutely wonderful for relieving the general aches and pains for at least a few hour. I think the steam did have an effect directly on the infection too - kind of softening it - or softening the tissue so it could either circulate better or rupture and relieve pressure and let the yucky puss and stuff drain. I usually ended by rinsing off under a cold shower - something I've been getting more of a taste for since the scorcher of a summer we had in January.

### Da-mo — 2015-05-15T07:04:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-164336>

Anyway, 2 days on the meds and I'm feeling much better - to the point where I'm debating whther I should start doing some maintenance around the farm or rest awhile longer. The tonsils still feel swollen but much reduced in size and feels as if the infection is receeding and tissue is beginning to heal. Still difficult to swallow but breathing difficulties all gone.

Best part is that last night sleep improved dramatically. It was more continuous than fitful and I managed to sleep a couple of hours longer than I have been lately.

It almost feels since I cut all smoking/nicotine and have been doing very few nightshifts lately that my body wants to go into a bi-phasic sleep pattern - with a waking quiet time - almost meditative - in the middle of the night somewhere.

### Da-mo — 2015-06-25T08:33:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-169553>

Made the first step to getting off shiftwork. Working normal hours for the next 7 weeks. Only on day 4 but feeling better all the time. I skip lunch every day without feeling too hungry. Sleep is improving on its own without needing to be "managed". Mood has improved immensely!!!.

At the moment its looking like this day work will become a more regular thing - fingers crossed.

And to top it all off we are experiencing sub zero degC temperatures with sunny days lately. I wear a bare minimum of clothing at work - just shorts, overalls 2 sizes too large, socks and boots = so I'm getting CT and lots of sun too.

Happy days

### fitness@home — 2015-06-25T16:27:43+0300

### Da-mo — 2015-08-26T11:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-175773>

Back on shift now after 7 weeks of daywork. While on daywork I went back to a maintenance role as a tradesman - so a less sedentary, more physically active working day. Less sitting, way less time in front of computer screens (probably from 11 or so hours at a time on shift to maybe 3 hours max. while on daywork).

First day knees were quite sore but they steadily improved during that first week and were giving me no trouble by the second week.

In the third week I tweaked my back lifting something - used my knee to hoist it up so was stood on one leg for a moment which put my back into spasm. Took an hour out to ice it and carried on working - and it was all back to normal after three days. While working in OPS on shift Im sure this injury would have dragged on for several weeks.

Sleep during this time was phenomenal!!! I would wake up at the right time every day without an alarm and would be nodding off a couple of hours after sundown every night. Sleep quality consisted of several cycles of various depth of sleep with dreaming but generally uninterrupted. When I would wake up - I'd be fully awake and would carry this alertness and wakefulness throughout the day. Biggest difference was in energy levels - I'd say energy levels during the day were at least 75-100% better than when Im on shiftwork.

Eating habits quickly evolved to a BAB and no food during work hours and then dinner at around 5-6pm. By week two I could not manage as big a breakfast even if I ate smaller dinners the nights before - so food intake became self regulating.

Now that Im back on shift I dont feel too bad - probably built up a reserve while on daywork which will no doubt erode as more time on shift elapses.

I'm currently shceduled in so that I'll be spending one third of my time back on daywork - that is 16 weeks on shift then 8 weeks on days. I think by next year it will have evolved into a 50:50 split between shiftwork and daywork.

Still not ideal but all steps in the right direction so I wont let good be the enemy of optimal - just keep working towards the goal of eliminating nightshifts from my life.

The world still tempts me with phenomenal money working a Norwegian Roster on a rig - have to keep reminding myself that not only do I want years in my life, but life in my years as well.

### Lahelada — 2015-09-21T15:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-journal.12187/post-177475>

**Happy First Day of Spring !**

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-18T17:16:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sambucol.12188/post-147716>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11399518](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11399518)

[http://www.amazon.com/Sambucol-Original-Liquid-Black-Elderberry/dp/B001ESA36I](http://www.amazon.com/Sambucol-Original-Liquid-Black-Elderberry/dp/B001ESA36I)

Anyone have any experience with this stuff? As I don't have a car I generally just buy stuff in the Walgreens I work at. Feel free to give me other ideas, but depending on where they are I might not be able to get them.

I've had allergy symptoms for the past week varying in intensity. At least I think its allergies because I had similar symptoms last year that abated after doing to a nearby city, but considering they cleared up figured it was far enough away from the allergens to do the job.

Stuffed nose, irritated throat, and blocked ears at the same time is really annoying. I'll clear my nose pretty well in the morning, but than it comes right back completely stuffed at work.

I tried Nasacort for 3 days...someone told me afterword it took 10 for them to see results, but I'm not sure about using a product that warns about effecting growth anyway...I know thats for kids and I'm an adult and not growing anymore..but still.

I recently bought the product at the top and have read good reviews and I'm curious if anyone else has used to good effect...and I'm curious about the linked research as it mentions increases inflammatory cytokines...the research makes that sound positive..but I thought increasing cytokines is bad...maybe its an amount thing? I'm not sure how it works so if someone can explain that it would be great.

Thanks

**Posts:** 31

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-18T19:06:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-temps-how-long.12190/post-147722>

I know this is likely to vary..but I have no clue how long I should try to stay in for. Also why was it again that this isn't bad considering the bodies stress response? Stressing the body a ton with the cold..I guess its ok because it doesn't last too long?

I have 40-45 degree water at the moment. I know I should monitor my skin temp and not let it go below 50? I'm not sure how long that will take..I guess I'll just test it constantly.

Its so freakin cold..I really want to start losing this weight its driving me crazy, but I don't want to hurt myself...my feet feel a bit frozen for mins/hours depending on how long I stay in at this kind of cold. Does a frozen feeling mean potential damage?

A few days ago at a similar temp my feet had this frozen feeling after just one lap at this temperature.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-18T19:15:58+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-temps-how-long.12190/post-147724>

[http://www.amazon.com/Tilos-Neopren...F8&qid=1416331448&sr=8-5&keywords=scuba+boots](http://www.amazon.com/Tilos-Neoprene-Fin-Socks-LG/dp/B002OWKP0C/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1416331448&sr=8-5&keywords=scuba+boots) I use these and they do help.

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-18T19:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-temps-how-long.12190/post-147727>

As much as I would like to ignore half of that article...I forgot much of it...read it before...I guess I'll try and start with just my face. I'm using my pool outside. Since the water is already there its much easier than making icy water in the house so I'll be ignoring staying inside. I'll make sure to put on some socks though to keep my feet warm..and if I can find my hat thing..forget what they are called I'll use that to keep my ears warm.

Thanks for the input.

### Da-mo — 2014-11-18T19:48:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-temps-how-long.12190/post-147730>

If your water was warmer I would say you could try to skip some and go straight to immersion - but at the temps you are using it classifies as Deep CT which adds some danger.

> **8\. What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation?**
> CT should never induce hypothermia as the protocol is written. It is designed to only use the surface cold receptors at 50-55 degrees and does not utilize the deep cold receptors. This is why the protocol is safe. If one chooses to embark on Deep CT all bets are off and you need a spotter and a doctor to help you implement it. That is just not a safe choice for a beginner.[http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/](http://jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/)

Sounds like you are moving around in the water - we generally stay still. Staying still causes an exclusion zone to build up at the skin surface.

Your monitoring of skin temp is sound in theory - but in practice hypothermia causes changes in brain function and cognition - i.e. we make mistakes when we get too cold. Just be aware if you do it at those lower temps you have removed some margin for error.

You are not stressing the body with cold. When you say this you clearly are not understanding how mitochondria use cold.
In liquid water water volume shrinks when it is heated by infrared light released from mitochondria. This is why ice floats on liquid water. I cannot stress how critical these relationships are to one another. This shrinks cell volumes and mitochondrial volumes just using heat and water’s interaction. It also points out why cold thermogenesis works fundamentally because cold increase heat release from mitochondria naturally. This makes you more energy efficient. This makes water the ideal substance for life because of Einstein’s mass equivalence equation. It sounds like you've been listening to Robb Wolf too long. He is clueless about how water works with mitochondria. It is not a hormetic stress it is evolutionary coupled design.

My brain is bad..just very bad. I'm always tired, and have been for years. Parts of my memory feel decent...people are constantly astounded that I remember their buying preferences at my job at Walgreens as a Cashier, but I don't remember my childhood very well at all except for weird things like my dad letting me try a very tiny disgusting sip of Budweiser between ages 4-6. I remember a few other things, but not very much at all...but maybe thats normal. I fluctuate between 270-280 and keep going back to eating junk after only 3-4 days at most of trying to eat well. I want to have a wife someday..maybe even kids and I want to be healthy. I want to get a better job than Walgreens. ### [email redacted] — 2014-11-19T22:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-thermogenesis-temps-how-long.12190/post-147850>

Jonathan. First off I find you very honest and very un-pretentious. It takes real courage to lay out your problems as you do. About the whole subject of "Cold" and let me be very clear here I am speaking ONLY for myself but I can relate to what you are saying very much. Personally I CANNOT do it unless in the presence of 'warmth'!!! What I mean is I do it at the beach here in LA only after a good dose of sunshine and I go in but only as long as it does not begin to feel 'masochistic'. Now this can vary also according to circumstance like I have notices if I am with someone talking etc in the water I can and tend to say MUCH LONGER than I do by myself. By myself it can easily start to feel 'masochistic' and not actually good for me.

I know I am getting into the area of 'feelings' here and Dr Kruse is more concerned with the biology and all the rest of it. But the way he describes the "Fournier Effect" well that seems to work for me heat alternating with cold. Also I find if I do it that way and never 'force' myself to do, do it as it feels good and as I WANT even to do it I find some really amazing 'mental effects' it literally works like a 'shrink' as is used in psycho-therapy but a better shrink than I have found up to now!! It the 'shrink' of the ocean itself and the cold in it. It's like I can 'get under' a lot of my resentments mental blocks of all kinds. I don't feel this happening at all if I 'force' it like have an ice bath in the morning or something.

Dr Kruse says a lot cold is not 'hormetic' I think that means it causes stress. But I wonder COULD it or CAN it cause stress. I suspect it can and I think it is worth thinking about this.................cold is not just cold there is a context and lots of other factors. Also I am tempted to borrow Dr Kruse's statement like maybe cold can open the doors of Heaven or Hell depending......................I think there is a danger of 'masochism' and inducing stress and yet it can be the complete opposite. Also I know something about the dangers of 'masochism' I was brought up in a very strict Irish Catholic family and for years I was involved in what is called "Primal Therapy" which while again can and sometimes is a good idea also goes over into 'masochism' and become counter-productive. I think as biological creatures we are hard wired to seek pleasure and what feels good, we recoil from pain and if the cold feels 'painful' we recoil from it and I think it is quite possible that it is 'stressful'

Also I am aware there are other issues...............for example I pretty much know I do not have nearly enough DHA in my membranes which I think makes in much harder to tolerate cold. After all isn't it the presence of lots of DHA which allows fish to live (and see) in some REALLY cold environments. So for all these reasons Jonathan my 'advice' for what it's worth is go easy on yourself a bit you may be trying to do too much too quickly and there is then a danger of getting disillusioned. I hope you find this somewhat helpful

# Thread: Italian Pot Roast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-pot-roast.12192/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2014-11-18T21:16:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/italian-pot-roast.12192/post-147744>

This was delicious and had my entire house smelling absolutely divine. Highly recommend if you have a lazy weekend day at home. Perfect for a fall/winter day.

**Italian Pot Roast
(Caveman Books)**

**Ingredients**
2-lb rump or chuck beef roast
½ - t TBL sea salt
4 TBL extra-virgin olive oil, divided
2 medium carrots, diced (about 1C)
2 medium celery stalks, diced (about 1C)
1-2 medium red onions, diced(about 1.5-2C)
2-3 cloves garlic, minced
¼ flat leaf parsley, chopped fine
1 bay leave
2 TBL fresh sage, chopped fine
2 C medium-bodied Italian red wine or red wine vinegar, divided

**Directions**
1\. Trim some fat if desired and then pat dry with paper towels, Sprinkle generously with sea salt to season.
2\. Place 2TBL olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed stockpot or Dutch oven and allow it to heat up over medium-high heat. When oil begins to shimmer, but is not smoking, add the roast to pot and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning as needed until meat is browned on all sides.
3\. Transfer meat to platter and set aside.
4\. Return pot to stove and place over medium heat. Add 2 TBL olive oil and heat until shimmery. Add in the diced carrot, celery, and onion. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally until veggies start to lightly brown and begin to stick to pot. Then add minced garlic and the herbs. Move herbs around pot for about 1 minute or until they become fragrant.
5\. Add in 1.5C of the red wine and begin stirring quickly, scooping up any veggies that try to stick to bottom of pot. When wine has mostly evaporated and the veggies are heavily coated in the wine herb mixture, return the roast to pan and turn roast to coat it well on all sides with the soffritto base. Make sure the roast’s surface is coated with the base, brushing more of the mixture on and rubbing it into the roast.
6\. **MY STEP:** Pour yourself a glass of wine – there is leftover in the bottle! Enjoy
7\. Increase heat to high and add in the remaining ½ C wine and the bay leaf. Bring to boil. Once boiling is reached cover pan with a lid and reduce heat to simmer only. Contiune turning roast every 30 minutes, until the meat is very tender and flakes easily when pierced with a fork – about 3 hours.
8\. Turn off heat and let roast rest in juices for about an hour.
9\. Transfer to platter and cover loosely with foil. If sauce is too thin – increase heat, bring to boil, and reduce to a medium-thick consistency. Adjust seasoning now (salt, pepper).
10\. Cut roast into thick slices and drizzle each slice with the remaining juices.

# Thread: Jonathan's Path to Optimal?..hoping so
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-18T22:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-147751>

Hmm interesting no stress? The body doesn't produce cortisol or adrenaline due to cold? Isn't that what a stress response is? I was under the impression it did both. Or is it just that this "stress" cortisol/adrenaline release which is generally seen as bad if adrenal fatigued etc.. is offset by the beneficial effects to the energy efficiency created due to the mitochondrial change..

I guess I'm asking if its like a math formula in a way...mitochondrial energy efficiency increase + cortisol + adrenaline = health...I know sorta silly to put it that way..or maybe not..don't think my cluelessness is related to Rob Wolf I haven't read much from him recently..just basically clueless all by myself.

My brain is bad..just very bad. I'm always tired, and have been for years. Parts of my memory feel decent...people are constantly astounded that I remember their buying preferences at my job at Walgreens as a Cashier, but I don't remember my childhood very well at all except for weird things like my dad letting me try a very tiny disgusting sip of Budweiser between ages 4-6. I remember a few other things, but not very much at all...but maybe thats normal. I fluctuate between 270-280 and keep going back to eating junk after only 3-4 days at most of trying to eat well. Changing my brain feels impossible...I wish I could afford better food and could stop my brain from spazzing every time I try to deny myself carbohydrates...I've read constantly about being able to get over carb cravings, but can't think of how to do this when I can't even get beyond a week eating low amounts. Coconut Oil tastes bad to me when taken alone and the process of using my French Press to make Bulletproof coffee makes me inconsistent with that..

No point in continuing with all my excuses..anyway thanks for your post..really want to change..so frustrating failing over and over again...not sure what else to say or what else I can do. Sorry for the pointless ..not even sure what to call it...

I need to get past this somehow...I've always had problems with my drive...to do more to do better. I had a manager at a Dollar General ask if I was slow once...I don't really think I'm low intelligence in general. I don't work very fast..she coulda just mean moving slow, but it felt like she meant the other...and I've had people say they thought I was autistic..I know with the current definition of the Autism spectrum that doesn't necessarily mean low intelligence..I'm way too sensitive and bad and dealing with stress. When I get worked up its hard to stop crying because of a stress cycle where I keep thinking about how pathetic I am and feel and how I can't break out. I've never tried much to get a girlfriend as I have no clue how to, and the only one that showed an interest was someone that was low intelligence..at least I think probably Cerebral Palsy..not sure. She was nice but it makes me wonder if she saw a similarity in me somehow...I remember feeling bad about her asking me out basically...I automatically looked down on her in a way thinking like that. I felt callous and bad for it. I didn't even consider it for a second...not sure why I'm going in to all this. I guess just for background...so I can hear what others think about it..though I'm afraid of the response..

Sorry if this is annoying to read or if I'm annoying because I keep saying sorry..anyway I guess this'll do for now...not sure what I expect..but I hope something can help me. Some advice some..Anyway I guess I'm done for now. Thanks for any thoughts or ideas anyone can give me.

I want to have a wife someday..maybe even kids and I want to be healthy. I want to get a better job than Walgreens. My self esteem issues and my health in combination with everything is holding me to a life where I feel like screaming..there are people with far worse lives than me that handle them better..I just don't understand how..how to change how to be a better person....how to not feel pathetic...Anyway before I write for days I'll stop for now...really this time...

Thanks
Jonathan

p.s. since I included everything else I'm 31 Male never been on a date and only asked someone once...even though I knew they would say no.

**[fitness@home](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/fitness-home.5474/) said I should start a Journal..not sure if I'll post to this all that often..we'll see. Hoping to see good things in the coming years.**

### caroline — 2014-11-18T23:43:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-147754>

Welcome Jonathan. Baby steps ....there is lots to take on board here. Read other's journals for inspiration.

Read the journal by Mr Pinkies ....this is one amazing woman.

Also read about Jeremy Thomley. Dr. Kruse did a webinar about him and a live Q&A with him. He is a remarkable young man and he is your age. He has his own website ....get inspired!

Please keep reading and asking questions.

Have you read all the welcome stuff on the home page? Have you read the thread that Dr. K. Started about the importance of newbies?

Please read the leptin reset .....it will knock out carb cravings.

It doesn't have to be expensive at all ..... Dr. K. Is all about the three legged stool - light, water and magnetism

Get outside as much as possible, drink bottled water and eat tons of seafood ...like cans of sardines etc.

We all help each other here and Dr. k. Is the guy with the flashlight.

Good morning from Oz!

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-19T20:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-147845>

So far I've used some Elderberry today...[http://www.amazon.com/Sambucol-Orig...=1416422252&sr=8-3&keywords=elderberry+liquid](http://www.amazon.com/Sambucol-Original-Liquid-Black-Elderberry/dp/B001ESA36I/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1416422252&sr=8-3&keywords=elderberry+liquid)

I don't care for the 8 grams of sugar it has in it due to the glucose syrup, but my allergy/cold symptoms are getting better..though it could possibly be allergens going down or something else...def nothing conclusive. I might as well use up what I have...at least it tastes good.

Anyway I took about three tablespoons coconut oil and 1 ghee from Pure Indian Foods to go get in my cold water for the day and my huge list of supplements that I wish I could sustain, but I'll just have to start eating better cause I really really can't its expensive having all of them. I took some bitter melon...but realized I don't remember anywhere it mentioned how to use it. Do I use it a certain amount of time before cold exposure? I'm guessing either right before I go in or a certain amount of minutes after...this time I'm just gonna wing it. Going in and prob only be there for 5 minutes at most monitoring my skin temp to make sure I don't go below 50. I'll be near the steps the whole time so I can easily get out and won't attempt to swim since I am definitely not cold adapted.

I really want to get in shape and figure my life out. I'm tired of feeling tired, being alone, and having all this body pain; knees, back, allergies etc...etc..related to all this extra weight on me. I don't mind the people at Walgreens for the most part...I even like several people there..quite a few in fact I like quite a bit though there are always issues occasionally in any relationships work or otherwise.

Will probably make a post after I freeze myself. Well I'm off. Thanks to anyone who can either link to or quote something mentioning when to take Bitter Melon for best effect.

[http://www.amazon.com/Bitter-Melon-Extract-600mg-Capsules/dp/B000RE4CSK](http://www.amazon.com/Bitter-Melon-Extract-600mg-Capsules/dp/B000RE4CSK)

This is what I used one capsule of as recommended by Dr. Kruse page I think. Anyway I'm off...before I psych myself out.

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-19T21:08:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-147847>

Followup...I'm completely unsure of what to think about this. I haven't started feeling it. I'm about 5 minutes after finishing. I stayed in for about 5 minutes. My skin temp while in showed as 44 on my arms 65 on my head which I only dipped a few times. Immediately on getting out my skin temp was showing as 46 or 2 degrees increase really fast..not sure if thats normal speed or not. Head skin temp was 70s after 5 mins and skin temp up to 65 after 5 mins. Its about 8 mins after writing this atm and I'm finally doing a bit of shivering...not extremely heavy but not light.

I'm not chattering my teeth like I tend to do when really cold. I did use Neoprene Boots which might have helped and the Bitter Melon along with the Coconut oil and Ghee. I'm wondering how beneficial 5 mins of 44 degree water can really be, but I guess the concept is you start doing more as your body adapts and heats up better due to your brown fat activating..I'm sure theres a more complicating way of saying that...anyway.

Interesting first try in a long while. I've been avoiding the water due to temperature but I think as long as I leave my hands out and cover my feet I can do this.

It is a nice 62 degrees in Lewisville atm so not extremely cold. I'm sure that helped in increasing my skin temp decently fast though I would think the water on my skin would cause some evaporative loss, but maybe the water came off without doing this and hence it didn't make a difference.

### Inger — 2014-11-22T09:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-148147>

Nice that you have a journal now Jonathan!
You will do it
just baby steps. step by step.
I would take real foods any day over supplements... can you just load up on seafood? Like Caroline says, canned sardines in water or olive oil.. ditch the oil. Frozen wildcaught fish.. do you have that? Here I can get very cheap frozen fish. I do a lot of herring... 6 € / kg for fillet. If I get whole herring I buy it too and use the heads for smoothie. Mackerel is super too, and very cheap here. Do you have a fish market? You could ask for roe.. maybe you get it for free? Can you pick wild herbs in the woods? Like chickweed, nettles etc? They are amazing... you need no veggies they are way more nutritious and you will save a lot of money. You do not even need to eat much.. they are so powerful. Just a handful or so every day.
When you buy you food, just make sure to be super disciplined and never ever buy any crap! Just the best stuff for you. Throw away any crap food that is in your home. That way it is easier, because you have only good stuff at home. And make sure to have enough food or eat a lot before going to work so you will not get hungry and eat the crap there. You can do it. If you get cravings... just eat fish until you are so full that nothing else will fit in your belly
Sp great you have started with CT. 5 minutes is awesome.. ANY CT is awesome  I am proud of you
If you feel down.. take a walk in the woods....... watch up in heaven.. listen to the birds... suck in all the beauty.. it will help a lot.. that is how I do it too

I have a pool in the backyard to use for CT this time of year when the water temps are cool enough. Outside is better for me. Inside the time in the bathtub passes so slowly.

Thanks for the encouragement. Sadly one of the reasons I can't take part of the advice is one of the reasons I really need to start advancing. Right now I live in a 3 bedroom 2 bathroom house thats pretty filled up with my mom whose around 52-53...I forget lol...my brother whose 33 a sister who is 21, and my brothers wife whose...I'm not sure but I'll say 31 cause I think shes a bit younger...though she might be 1 year younger or older than me....not sure. She really wants kids it seems and my brother has hinted that he would like me to try to move toward independence so there is room for baby(ies).

Anyway of course the part I'm talking about is the throwing junk out. I can do the part about not contributing by not buying more...at least if I can find the willpower not to, but I can't throw out what they buy at all while I'm living here. Living on a job at Walgreens...that doesn't pay that well makes it a bit hard to move out at this point and I'm saving for a car also at the moment. It'll make it somewhat better I hope that they are on South Beach...I wish they would ditch the Pita Bread they put on their list and just go Paleo...but they are still trying to hang on a bit to junk carby foods.

I can't say too much about it though due to all my transgressions as far as buying junk when I'm trying to eat Paleo without any carbohydrates. Anyway I have Coconut Oil in my mouth because my gums are really inflamed. I'm hoping if I can get consistent with this and the Waterpik that's now in my brothers bathroom I can get some of this mouth inflammation down and that will help me lose weight. I'm sure some of it is fueled by my bad eating habits...but I'm hoping by cleaning out my mouth it will help me eat better. I know the mouth is tied into the whole system and I'm hoping it helps.

Anyway I'll post more later. I need to take my supplements and do my CT talk to ya later. Thanks again

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-22T20:10:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-148174>

I'm about 10-15 minutes post 5-15 minute CT in my approximately 50 degree pool..feeling alright though I'm shivering a bit and was wondering if anyone has any experience with repeated CT. By that I mean waiting to warm up till you feel normal than doing it again. I was considering waiting till I'm fully warm and feel normal than repeating...not sure how many times. Is there a negative to doing that? Physiologically is my body gonna have any issues? Perhaps detox as I've heard people talk about that being an issue with CT?

### nicld — 2014-11-22T20:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-148175>

On the weekend I try to do 2 sessions about 4 hours apart. First one is 2 hours. the second one is only about 20 minutes but I add snow to my water to do deep CT but I have been doing CT for over a year and a half and started off slow.

### Raiken3712 — 2014-11-22T21:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-148178>

I'm still getting slight shivers and I think its been 45 minutes to an hour....does it really take me this long to be back to normal. Its not heavy shivering..just light but still sheesh. Whats the temperature on your 2 hours? I know I shouldn't do two hour sessions when I'm not ready for ti just curious. Also with the snow whats the temperature? My pool is 40-50 at the moment, but depending on if it gets cold again it'll go into the 30s. I'm probably not going to do too much when it gets to that point. Maybe in and out in less than 10 seconds in the 30s lol.

I've done it before in 30s so I know its not too much a shock to the system, but doubt I can last all that long without some serious adaption. I wish my pool would stay between 50-80 year round...would need more money to have access to that though.

>

My rain barrel had 37 F this morning... and the grass was a bit frozen as I walked barefoot over the lawn from my horse barn bedroom to the house... I like that
I am not staying in long at all tho.. I take a warm shower in the mornings and after that I dip for a minute or two in the barrel. I am lazy CTer! But I feel so good and healthy so I guess it is enough - but for less fat and hotter body I could do longer... I might start to do up to 5 minute dips soon. I need to take my timer. And hat and gloves and socks. That way I can stay in longer no problem. I do not shiver at all after a 1 minute dip in ice cold water... it is just too short a time to get that cold, except of the skin. I have to say I much prefer short duration and icy temps.. maybe because it all goes so fast and the icy water really feels energizing!

### nicld — 2014-11-22T23:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jonathans-path-to-optimal-hoping-so.12193/post-148187>

I finished 2 hours ago and am still a bit cold but I have my house temp set at 67F.

Water temp starts at 45F but I have an overhead heater that I use to create the Fournier effect the best I can.

I usually get it down to 33-35F depending on the starting water temp and how many buckets of snow I use but at that temp I am very very careful and very aware of how I feel and do not try to suck it up and stay in longer. DO NOT DO THIS yet. It took me 6-7 months of regular CT to be able to get down that low and Jack does not recommend this unless one is totally cold adapted. I also do not do this all the time, only in winter and only on the weekends when I have plenty of time to rewarm. I like to be cool when I go to bed but I cannot fall asleep if I am too cold.

55 degree water at approximately 27 minutes in...got out a few times so 25 at the lowest....resulting in skin temperatures on areas that were in for 99% of that time...60 degrees. I exercised a bit jumping up and down...but shouldn't that lower the temperature..shouldn't keep it high should it?

I really wish I had the resources to get my RMR tested while doing that. Very interesting results.

I've had low hormones (Testosterone, DHEA and Progesterone) over the past 3 years.
